variations: Three tests before marriage: traffic, IKEA, Wi-Fi outage. You two failed all three in front of each other. And here you are. Cheers to that.
Here's to every traffic jam, every IKEA box, every dropped signal still to come — and to still choosing each other through it.
If you can build a BILLY bookcase together and still want to be married — you'll be fine forever.

items.sourceNote: 長距離戀愛 / 跨城市戀情走到結婚的賓客致詞 register（致詞給「終於不用機場接送」的新人），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
你們有多少個夜晚
是隔著螢幕睡著的？

多少次
機場接機、機場送別，
眼眶都還沒乾、
下一張機票就訂好了？

多少個節日
你過你的、他過他的，
靠一通電話
假裝坐在同一張桌子？

今天起——

不用了。

你不用再算時差、
不用再省假期、
不用再在登機門前
說「下次見」。

下一次見，
是回家。

敬你們，
敬你們撐過的那些距離，
更敬以後——

每一個
睜開眼就在身邊的早晨。
zh-tw.editorNote: 遠距離走到結婚的故事最戳人。這版具體列舉遠距離的痛點（時差、機票、機場、節日各過各的），不抽象說「你們愛得很辛苦」，而是讓賓客腦中浮現畫面。最後「下一次見，是回家」是這類致詞的金句結構——一句話翻轉所有過去的距離
variations: 你們撐過了時差、機票、機場、各過各的節日——今天起，下一次見就是回家。敬你們。
敬以後每一個睜開眼就在身邊的早晨，不用再算時差、不用再說下次見。
你們把「想念」過成了日常，今天起把日常過成了「在一起」。

en.content:
How many nights
did you fall asleep on a screen?

How many airport pickups,
airport drop-offs,
eyes still wet
while you booked the next flight?

How many holidays
you had yours, they had theirs,
phone propped up
pretending to share a table?

Starting today —

you don't.

No more time zones.
No more rationing vacation days.
No more 'see you soon'
at a departure gate.

The next time you see each other
is home.

Here's to you,
to every mile you closed,
and to every morning from here on —

where you wake up
and they're already there.
en.editorNote: Long-distance-to-marriage stories hit hardest. This piece names the specific pains (time zones, airport farewells, holidays apart, FaceTime dinners) so guests can picture it, instead of abstractly saying 'you've worked so hard for this.' The pivot line — 'the next time you see each other is home' — is the entire emotional payoff
variations: You survived time zones, airport goodbyes, holidays apart. Starting today, the next time you see each other is home. Cheers to that.
Here's to every morning from here on where you wake up and they're already there.
You turned missing each other into a daily routine. Today, the routine becomes being together.

items.sourceNote: 創意俏皮紅包賀詞 register（「婚姻是地獄／天堂」的台灣俗諺重新詮釋），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
聽人說——

婚姻是地獄，
還是有人擠破頭要進去。
婚姻是天堂，
還是有人哭著想出來。

看來地獄天堂，
看的不是地點，
是身邊那個人。

你選對了。

剩下的，
就讓地獄也變成天堂。

新婚快樂。
zh-tw.editorNote: 改寫自台灣常見的「婚姻是地獄 vs. 天堂」俗諺，但翻轉視角——重點不是婚姻好壞，是「身邊的人對不對」。短短幾句，比「百年好合」有梗、比「永浴愛河」有溫度，紅包袋寫得下、IG 限動截得到。最後一句「就讓地獄也變成天堂」是金句
variations: 婚姻是地獄還是天堂，看的不是地點，是身邊那個人。你選對了，恭喜。
地獄天堂只差一個人——你選對了，剩下交給時間。新婚快樂。
聽說婚姻是地獄，但你帶對了人，地獄也能裝潢成家。

en.content:
They say —

marriage is hell,
and still people fight to get in.
Marriage is heaven,
and still some people cry to get out.

Turns out heaven and hell
aren't about the place.
They're about the person
standing next to you.

You picked right.

The rest is just
turning hell
into somewhere worth living.

Congratulations.
en.editorNote: Reframe of the old 'marriage is hell / heaven' cliché — the twist is that the verdict depends on the partner, not the institution. Short enough for a card, sharp enough for an IG caption. The closing — 'turning hell into somewhere worth living' — is the quotable line
variations: Heaven or hell, it's not about the place — it's about who's standing next to you. You picked right.
The difference between heaven and hell is one person. You found yours. Congratulations.
They say marriage is hell. But you brought the right person, so you'll just renovate it into home.

items.sourceNote: 祖父母 / 阿公阿嬤致詞 register（長年婚姻智慧），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我跟阿公結婚五十二年了。

年輕的時候我以為，
婚姻最難的是找到對的人。

後來我才知道——
找到對的人只是入場券。

真正難的是：
每天早上起床，
再選一次他。

我選了一萬八千多次。
從來沒選錯過。

孫女，今天起換你選。
慢慢選，認真選，每天都選。
zh-tw.editorNote: 祖母 / 阿嬤視角的致詞最有重量——把「一萬八千多次的選擇」這個具體數字講出來，比任何抽象的「白頭偕老」都動人。建議真實長輩當場唸；長輩沒空，就由新人幫忙轉述「阿嬤交代的話」
variations: 婚姻不是找到一次對的人就好，是每天早上起床，再選一次他。我選了五十二年。
阿嬤的祕訣只有一句：每天起床，重新選他一次。選了一萬多次，從來沒選錯。

en.content:
I've been married to my husband for fifty-two years.

When I was young, I thought
the hardest part of marriage
was finding the right person.

Later I learned —
that's just the ticket in.

The hard part is this:
waking up every morning
and choosing him again.

I've chosen him over eighteen thousand times.
I've never picked wrong.

Granddaughter, now it's your turn.
Choose slowly. Choose honestly. Choose every day.
en.editorNote: Grandmother-perspective toast carries the most weight of any speech register — the specific number ('eighteen thousand mornings') beats any abstract 'till death do us part.' Have the actual elder deliver it if possible; otherwise the bride/groom can read it as 'what my grandmother told me to say'
variations: Marriage isn't choosing the right person once. It's waking up every morning and choosing them again. I've done it eighteen thousand times. Not a single regret.
Grandma's only secret: wake up, choose him again, every single day. Fifty-two years of choosing. Never picked wrong.

items.sourceNote: 二婚 / 重組家庭致詞 register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我們不是第一次站在這裡。

上一次我們以為，
愛情會自己長大。

後來才知道——
愛情要有人澆水、有人修剪、
有人在它快要枯掉的時候，
還記得它原本長什麼樣子。

這一次我不會再以為。
這一次我會記得澆水，
會記得修剪，
會在它枯的時候，
陪它重新長一次。
zh-tw.editorNote: 二婚 / 再婚致詞最難寫——既要承認過去、又要不黏在過去。這版用「澆水」的具體動作比喻，避開「這次一定不一樣」的空泛承諾，把學到的事化成可執行的承諾。適合自證、適合宣讀，也適合給孩子聽
variations: 上一次我以為愛情會自己長大，這一次我知道愛情要有人陪它長。我會陪到底。
這不是我第一次說「我願意」，但這是我第一次知道，「願意」是每天的事，不是一天的事。

en.content:
We're not standing here for the first time.

Last time, we thought
love would grow on its own.

We learned —
love needs someone watering it,
someone pruning it,
someone who remembers
what it looked like
when it was almost dying.

This time I won't assume.
This time I'll remember the watering.
I'll remember the pruning.
And when it withers,
I'll be there to help it grow back.
en.editorNote: Second-marriage vows are the hardest to write — they have to acknowledge the past without dragging it into the room. This version uses 'watering' as the concrete metaphor and turns hard-won knowledge into actionable promises. Works as a vow, a toast, or a private letter the kids can read later
variations: Last time I thought love grew on its own. This time I know it needs a gardener. I'm signing up for the job — for life.
This isn't my first 'I do.' But it's the first time I know that 'I do' is a daily verb, not a one-day promise.

items.sourceNote: 證婚人 / 主婚人開場 register（儀式開頭），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
今天我站在這裡，
不是來主持一場儀式，
是來見證一件事——

兩個原本是陌生人的人，
在某一天遇見了，
決定把彼此放進
自己人生最重要的那個位置。

各位來賓，
你們也不是來吃喜酒的。
你們是這件事的證人。

請記得今天，
以後他們吵架的時候，
你們有資格說：
「我看過你們最初的樣子，
你們值得撐下去。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 證婚人 / 主婚人開場萬用版——把「來賓 = 證人」這個設定講清楚，比一般「歡迎大家蒞臨」更有儀式感，也賦予全場觀禮的人一個任務，後面新人致詞、長輩致詞會更有重量
variations: 今天各位不是來吃喜酒的，是來當證人的。請好好記得他們今天的樣子，以後派得上用場。
婚禮是一件需要證人的事。今天到場的每一位，都是這份承諾的見證者。

en.content:
I'm not standing here today
to run a ceremony.

I'm standing here to witness something —

two people who started as strangers,
met on some ordinary day,
and decided to give each other
the most important seat
in the rest of their lives.

Guests, you're not here for the dinner.
You're witnesses.

Remember today.
Because one day, when they fight,
you get to say:
'I saw who you were at the start.
You're worth staying for.'
en.editorNote: Universal officiant / opener that reframes the guests' role — they're not attendees, they're witnesses with a future job. Gives weight to everything that comes after (vows, toasts, family speeches), and gives the audience a reason to be present, not just polite
variations: You're not here for the dinner. You're here as witnesses. Remember today — they'll need you to remember it later.
A wedding needs witnesses. Every person in this room signs onto that role the moment they walk in.

items.sourceNote: 現代家庭日常 register（含寵物 / 共同空間），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我答應你——

貓砂我會去鏟，
地板我會去掃，
你忘記關的瓦斯我會去關，
你半夜爬不起來的時候，
我會幫你回那則重要的訊息。

我不會說我會永遠愛你。

我會說：
以後家裡所有麻煩的事情，
我們一人做一半。

剩下的那一半愛——
會自己長出來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 現代誓詞神級結構——前段堆疊四件家事的具體承諾，中段反轉「不說永遠」，後段把愛定義成「分擔麻煩的副產品」。比「我會永遠愛你」可信一百倍，新人之間實際生活也用得上
variations: 我不答應你永遠的愛——我答應你，家裡所有的麻煩事我們一人一半，愛會自己長。
貓砂、地板、瓦斯、半夜的訊息——我答應你這四件事。其他的愛，會慢慢自己長出來。

en.content:
Here's what I'm promising —

the litter box is mine on the weeks you're tired.
The floor, I'll sweep.
The stove you forgot to turn off, I'll turn off.
The text you can't answer at 2 a.m.,
I'll answer for you.

I'm not going to say I'll love you forever.

I'll say this:
every annoying thing in this house,
we split fifty-fifty.

The other half — the love —
grows on its own.
en.editorNote: Modern vow at its strongest — four concrete domestic promises up front, the 'I won't say forever' pivot in the middle, then the closer that redefines love as a byproduct of shared chores. More believable than any 'forever' line, and it actually works in real life
variations: I'm not promising forever love. I'm promising fifty-fifty on every annoying thing in this house. Love grows from that on its own.
Litter box, floors, the stove you forgot, the 2 a.m. text — those four I'll handle. The love part figures itself out.

items.sourceNote: 寵物視角 / 家裡的狗或貓「致詞」register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
在他們決定結婚之前，
家裡有一個小評審。

那隻貓——
如果不喜歡，會躲到衣櫃裡。
如果可以接受，會睡在床尾。
如果是對的人，
會直接趴在那個人的胸口睡著。

我可以告訴你們，
從她第一次來家裡開始，
那隻貓沒從他胸口下來過。

所以這場婚禮，
其實貓早就批准了。
我們只是來補簽名的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 養寵物的新人圈最神的致詞角度——讓「家裡那隻貓 / 狗」當審核員，比朋友吐槽更安全（不會踩雷）、比官式祝福更有畫面。如果新人有真的寵物，這個 register 全場必融化
variations: 在他們結婚之前，那隻貓就已經幫我們做了決定。今天我們只是把貓的決定，補一個簽名。
判斷一個對象適不適合的最高標準，是家裡那隻貓願不願意睡在他身上。這個審查，他早就通過了。

en.content:
Before they decided to get married,
there was already a small judge in the house.

That cat —
if she doesn't like you, she hides in the closet.
If she tolerates you, she sleeps at the foot of the bed.
If you're the right one,
she falls asleep on your chest.

I can tell you,
from the first time she came to that apartment,
that cat has not gotten off his chest.

So really, this wedding
was already approved by the cat.
We're just here to countersign.
en.editorNote: Strongest speech angle for pet-owning couples — use the household cat or dog as the unimpeachable judge. Safer than roasting friends, more concrete than a generic blessing. If the couple has an actual pet, the room melts every time
variations: Before they got engaged, the cat had already decided. We're just here to countersign her ruling.
The truest test of whether someone's the one isn't your gut. It's whether the family cat falls asleep on them. He passed years ago.

items.sourceNote: 閨蜜 / 死黨多年友誼 register，「我們三個」結構，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我跟她認識十五年。

吃過幾百頓飯、
哭過幾十次、
陪她搬過四次家、
聽她講過所有不該講的話。

我以為我這輩子
會是她講最多話的那個人。

結果他出現了。

她開始把好笑的事先傳給他，
把難過的事先傳給他，
連半夜想吃宵夜，
都先問他要不要。

說真的，我有一點吃醋。

但更多的，
是放心。

因為終於有人，
比我還想聽她每天的廢話。
zh-tw.editorNote: 閨蜜 / 死黨致詞神級結構——先用「我以為我是她最親的」鋪陳，再用「結果他出現了」反轉，最後落在「終於有人比我還想聽」的放心。比一般「祝你們幸福」深一層，朋友圈會掉眼淚
variations: 我以為我會是她講最多話的人，結果他出現了。我有點吃醋，但更多的是放心——終於有人比我更想聽她的廢話。
認識她十五年，第一次有人比我還早收到她的訊息。我交棒交得心甘情願。

en.content:
I've known her for fifteen years.

Hundreds of meals together.
Dozens of crying sessions.
Four apartment moves.
And every single secret she shouldn't have told anyone.

I honestly thought
I was going to be the person she talked to most
for the rest of her life.

Then he showed up.

Now the funny things get sent to him first.
The sad things, him first.
Even the 'should we order food at midnight' texts —
him first.

I'll admit, I was a little jealous.

But mostly?
I'm relieved.

Because somebody finally wants to hear her ramble
more than I do.
en.editorNote: Long-friendship maid-of-honor / best-friend speech structure — set up 'I thought I was the closest one,' pivot on 'then he showed up,' land on 'finally someone wants it more than I do.' Deeper than 'be happy' — friends in the room will cry
variations: I thought I'd be the one she talked to most for life. Then he showed up. I was jealous for a second — then I was relieved. Somebody finally wants her ramble more than I do.
Fifteen years of being her first text. Now it's him. I handed the baton over gladly — he runs the next leg better than I ever could.

items.sourceNote: 戒指象徵 register，物件當載體的誓詞，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
戒指是圓的。

沒有開始，
沒有結束。

我把它戴到你手上，
不是因為它代表「永遠」——

是因為它沒有缺口。

以後你會看著它做菜、
看著它打字、
看著它牽我們以後的小孩——

每一次低頭，
它都會提醒你：

有一個人，
在外面那一圈，
圍著你，
不會斷。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把戒指當實體載體的誓詞——避開「永遠」「天長地久」的抽象空話，改用「沒有缺口 / 圍著你」這種視覺承諾。每次低頭都能被提醒，比口頭誓言更日常、更可驗證
variations: 我把這個圓圈戴到你手上，不是因為它代表永遠，是因為它沒有缺口——它每天提醒你，外面那一圈是我，不會斷。
戒指最動人的不是「圓」，是「沒有開口」。我會是那個讓圈圈不斷的人。

en.content:
A ring is a circle.

No beginning.
No end.

I'm putting this on your hand
not because it means 'forever' —

because it has no gap.

You'll see it while you cook.
You'll see it while you type.
You'll see it on the hand that holds our kids one day.

Every time you look down,
it'll remind you:

somebody is the outer ring.
Somebody is wrapped around you.
And that ring doesn't break.
en.editorNote: Vow that uses the ring as a literal, visible carrier — replaces abstract 'forever' with a concrete daily reminder ('no gap / wraps around you'). Every glance at the hand re-delivers the promise. Far stronger than verbal-only oaths
variations: I'm not putting this on your hand because it means forever. I'm putting it there because it has no gap — and I'll be that gap-less ring around you for life.
The point of a ring isn't the circle. It's the absence of a break. I'm signing up to be the part that doesn't break.

items.sourceNote: 婚禮俳句 / 三行詩 register（極短致敬），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
新郎西裝筆挺。
新娘白紗光亮。
剩下的話，留給時間慢慢說。

乾杯。
zh-tw.editorNote: 三行詩 + 一句乾杯——適合敬酒席間每一桌輪流敬時用，比長篇致詞更尊重客人吃飯時間，留白比話多更有質感。賓客還會記得你是「那個三句話就敬完酒的人」
variations: 西裝筆挺，白紗光亮，故事還很長。乾杯。
今天他帥，今天她美，今天我們乾。三件事說完，剩下的時間還給你們。

en.content:
Groom looks sharp.
Bride looks radiant.
The rest of the story — let time tell it.

Cheers.
en.editorNote: Three-line haiku-style toast plus one 'cheers' — perfect for table-by-table rounds when you don't want to monopolize the dinner. Restraint reads as confidence; guests will remember you as 'the one who got it done in three lines'
variations: Sharp suit. Bright dress. Long story ahead. Cheers.
He's handsome tonight. She's glowing tonight. We drink tonight. The rest, we'll see.

items.sourceNote: 未來回望 register（從五十年後回看今天），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
五十年後的某一天，
你們可能會坐在一張舊沙發上，
手很皺、頭髮很白、
旁邊有一壺涼掉的茶。

那時候你們會聊到今天——

聊我穿了哪件醜西裝、
聊誰喝得太多、
聊你們忘了背的誓詞。

然後其中一個人會說：
「那真的是好久好久以前了。」

另一個會說：
「但我記得每一分鐘。」

我來敬的，
就是那個你們會一起記得的——
好久好久以後。
zh-tw.editorNote: 致詞最高招——直接把鏡頭快轉五十年，從未來回看今天。賓客會被「舊沙發 + 涼掉的茶 + 記得每一分鐘」這個畫面釘在椅子上。比「祝你們白頭偕老」具體一千倍，全場會安靜下來
variations: 敬五十年後的那張舊沙發、那壺涼掉的茶，和那個會說「我記得每一分鐘」的人。
我來敬的不是今天，是五十年後你們回頭看今天的那一刻——那時候你們會記得每一分鐘。

en.content:
Fifty years from now,
you'll probably be sitting on some old couch,
hands wrinkled, hair white,
a pot of tea gone cold beside you.

You'll talk about today —

about the ugly suit I wore,
about who drank too much,
about the vows you forgot halfway through.

And one of you will say:
'That was such a long time ago.'

And the other will say:
'But I remember every minute.'

That's what I'm raising my glass to —
the day, fifty years from now,
when you both remember every minute.
en.editorNote: Highest-register speech move — fast-forward fifty years and look back at tonight. The image of 'old couch, cold tea, remembering every minute' pins the room. A thousand times more concrete than 'happily ever after,' and the room goes quiet
variations: Here's to the old couch, the cold tea, and the person fifty years from now who says 'I remember every minute.'
I'm not toasting today. I'm toasting the moment, fifty years from now, when you look back at today — and still remember every minute.

items.sourceNote: 交友軟體 register（現代戀愛起點），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
他們的愛情故事其實很童話。

男生往右滑。
女生往右滑。
約出來喝杯咖啡，
先確認對方不是詐騙也不是怪人。

然後——就走到今天了。

你看，現代版的「命中注定」，
是兩個人剛好都按到同一個方向。
zh-tw.editorNote: 交友軟體哏現在最有共鳴——年輕賓客全部點頭，長輩也聽得懂。「剛好按到同一個方向」這個收尾把搞笑轉成小浪漫，比硬掰命運更真
variations: 他們的相遇沒有月老、沒有雨中撞傘，只有一個往右滑、一個也往右滑——但能滑到今天這一桌，已經是最浪漫的演算法了。

en.content:
Their love story is honestly a fairytale.

He swiped right.
She swiped right.
They met for a coffee
just to make sure neither one was a scammer or a total weirdo.

And somehow — here we all are.

Turns out the modern version of 'meant to be'
is two people happening to tap the same direction.
en.editorNote: The dating-app bit lands hard right now — younger guests nod, older ones still follow. The 'tap the same direction' close flips the joke into a small romance, more honest than forcing fate
variations: No matchmaker, no bumping umbrellas in the rain — just one right swipe meeting another. Swiping all the way to this table is the most romantic algorithm there is.

items.sourceNote: AI 致詞 register（科技哏＋讚美），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
為了今天，
我先去問了AI怎麼寫致詞。

我打：「請幫我形容這對新人有多相愛。」

AI想了三秒，回我：
「錯誤：這種程度的愛無法計算。」

所以接下來這段，
是連電腦都當機的版本——
我盡量講，講不完的部分，
就讓大家自己看他們的眼神。
zh-tw.editorNote: AI哏是2026最新鮮的破冰——「連電腦都當機」這個轉折同時搞笑又是超大讚美。最後一句「看他們的眼神」把笑點順勢收進感動，全場會笑完又安靜
variations: 我問AI這對新人有多配，它回我「資料超出範圍」。連機器都算不出來的東西，今天我們現場見證。

en.content:
To prep for today,
I asked an AI to write my speech.

I typed: 'Describe how much these two love each other.'

It thought for three seconds and replied:
'Error: love of this magnitude cannot be computed.'

So what comes next
is the version that even crashed a computer —
I'll do my best, and whatever I can't put into words,
just watch the way they look at each other.
en.editorNote: The AI bit is the freshest 2026 icebreaker — 'even crashed a computer' is a laugh and a huge compliment at once. The 'watch the way they look at each other' close folds the joke straight into the feeling. Room laughs, then goes quiet
variations: I asked an AI how well-matched these two are. It said 'data out of range.' If a machine can't calculate it, we'll just have to witness it in person tonight.

items.sourceNote: 現代誓詞 register（日常瑣事＝浪漫），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我答應你——

一起分擔那些提到手痠的菜、
一起笑你每次都一樣的冷笑話、
一起把亂七八糟的日子，
過成我們自己的樣子。

我不是因為人生很簡單才選你，
是因為有你，
再麻煩的人生都變得值得。
zh-tw.editorNote: 現代誓詞的精髓是「把瑣事講成浪漫」——提菜、冷笑話、亂七八糟的日常，比「天長地久」可信。最後一句反轉是記憶點，新人念了會哽咽
variations: 我答應你，分擔每一袋菜、每一個冷笑話、每一個一團亂的星期一。不是因為日子好過，是因為有你，難過的日子也甘願。

en.content:
I promise you —

to carry the heavy grocery bags together,
to laugh at the same bad jokes you always tell,
to take our messy, ordinary days
and make them into something that's just ours.

I didn't choose you because life is easy.
I chose you because with you,
even the hard parts feel worth it.
en.editorNote: The heart of a modern vow is turning chores into romance — grocery bags, repeat jokes, the messy ordinary day beat 'forever and always.' The closing reversal is the keeper; couples choke up reading it aloud
variations: I promise to split the grocery bags, the bad jokes, and every chaotic Monday. Not because life is easy — but because with you, even the hard days are worth showing up for.

items.sourceNote: 短句紅包 register（曖昧轉終身制哏），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
恭喜你們，
把曖昧正式升級成終身制。

願你們吵架有上限、
撒嬌沒下限、
零食永遠買兩份，
好天氣永遠記得拍給對方看。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「曖昧轉終身制」是現在很紅的俏皮哏，搭四句生活排比剛好填滿紅包——年輕朋友圈通用，寫得進、貼得上、笑得出來
variations: 恭喜把曖昧玩成終身制——願你們吵架有上限、撒嬌沒下限，這輩子的零食都記得買兩份。

en.content:
Congrats —
you officially upgraded the 'are we / aren't we'
to a lifetime subscription.

May your fights have a ceiling,
your cuddles have no floor,
may you always buy snacks in twos,
and always remember to send each other the good weather.
en.editorNote: The 'situationship to lifetime subscription' line is a hot playful frame right now, paired with four everyday parallels that fill a card perfectly — fits a red envelope, an Instagram caption, or a guestbook, and gets the laugh
variations: Congrats on turning the situationship into a lifetime subscription — capped fights, bottomless cuddles, and snacks always bought in twos.

items.sourceNote: 搞笑致詞 register（AI / ChatGPT 時代哏），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我本來想叫AI幫我寫這篇致詞。

結果它打了一行字給我：
「資料異常——這兩個人也太配，無法分析。」

所以接下來的內容，
是我這個真人，
含淚手寫的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 2026最新哏的搞笑開場——用「AI都算不出你們多配」當笑點，比老套的破冰更跟得上時代。記得後面接真情流露才不會只剩玩笑
variations: 我問AI要怎麼形容你們的感情，它當機了三秒，然後回我：「超出可分析範圍。」所以剩下的，只能靠我親口講。

en.content:
I tried to get AI to write this speech for me.

It just spat back one line:
"Error — these two are too perfect to analyze."

So everything after this
is from an actual human being,
written by hand, through tears.
en.editorNote: A 2026-current opener — 'even the AI couldn't compute how good you two are' lands as both a joke and a compliment. Pivot to something sincere after so it isn't all gimmick
variations: I asked AI how to describe these two. It froze for three seconds and replied: 'Outside analyzable range.' So the rest of this one's on me.

items.sourceNote: 祖父母 / 長輩祝福 register（五十年婚姻視角），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
阿公阿嬤結婚五十年，
要送你們一句話。

不是「永遠不要吵架」——
我們吵了五十年。

是「吵完一定要先回頭」。

你們會懂的，
等你們也走到五十年那天。
zh-tw.editorNote: 祖父母 / 長輩致詞或卡片用——用「我們吵了五十年」自嘲破冰，再給出真正的智慧，比空泛的「白頭偕老」可信一百倍，全場長輩會點頭
variations: 結婚五十年教會我們一件事：重點不是不吵架，是吵完誰先伸手。願你們也學會這件最難的小事。

en.content:
Fifty years married,
and here's the one thing we'll give you.

Not "never fight" —
we fought for fifty years.

It's "always be the first to turn back around."

You'll understand it
the day you reach fifty years too.
en.editorNote: For a grandparent's speech or card — self-deprecating 'we fought for fifty years' breaks the ice, then delivers real, earned wisdom. Far more believable than 'grow old together'
variations: Fifty years taught us this: it's not about never fighting, it's about who reaches back first. May you both learn that hardest little skill.

items.sourceNote: 現代誓詞 register（今天 + 所有明天概念），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
你是我的今天，
也是我所有的明天。

我現在愛你的方式，
跟認識你那天不一樣了——

少了一點心跳加速，
多了一種「回家」的安心。

我選的，就是這種愛。
zh-tw.editorNote: 現代誓詞用——把「現在比剛認識更愛你」這個概念講得有層次，避開「初心不變」的口號，承認感情會變、而且變得更好，真摯又不假掰
variations: 你是我的今天和所有明天。我愛你的方式變了，從心跳加速變成回家的安心——而我更喜歡現在這一種。

en.content:
You are my today
and all of my tomorrows.

The way I love you now
isn't the way I loved you on day one —

less racing heartbeat,
more the quiet relief of coming home.

This is the kind of love I'm choosing.
en.editorNote: For a modern vow — gives the 'I love you more now than ever' idea some texture by admitting love changes, and changes for the better. Honest without being cheesy
variations: You're my today and all my tomorrows. The way I love you changed — from racing heart to the relief of home — and I like this version more.

items.sourceNote: 致詞 register（把困難當隊伍面對概念），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
婚姻不是兩個人對坐解決問題，
是兩個人並排，
把問題放到對面去解決。

錢、工作、小孩、未來——
那些都是擺在你們前面的關卡，
不是擺在你們中間的牆。

敬你們，永遠站在同一邊。
zh-tw.editorNote: 致詞中段轉煽情的金句——把「夫妻同隊、問題在對面」這個觀念講成畫面，比「同甘共苦」更具體，長輩晚輩都聽得進去
variations: 好的婚姻不是面對面吵問題，是肩並肩把問題擺到對面一起打。敬你們永遠同一隊。

en.content:
Marriage isn't two people facing off to solve a problem.

It's two people side by side,
putting the problem across the table from both of you.

Money, work, kids, the future —
those are levels in front of you,
not a wall between you.

Here's to staying on the same side. Always.
en.editorNote: An emotional-pivot line for the middle of a toast — turns the 'face challenges as a team' idea into a clear image (problem across the table, not a wall between). Lands for any age
variations: Good marriage isn't facing off over the problem — it's standing shoulder to shoulder and putting the problem across the table. To the same side, always.

items.sourceNote: 短句紅包 register（六月婚季 / 陽光意象），原創

zh-tw.content:
新婚快樂。

願你們有六月的陽光、
有說不完的笑話、
有一種越過越深的愛——
那種不靠新鮮感，
靠日子養出來的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 六月婚季限定——用「陽光、笑話、越養越深的愛」三個意象填紅包剛好，比「百年好合」溫暖、又比俏皮版正式，卡片留言本通用
variations: 新婚快樂——願你們有六月的陽光、滿出來的笑聲，和一種靠日子養深的愛。

en.content:
Happy wedding day.

May you have June sunshine,
endless inside jokes,
and the kind of love that keeps getting deeper —
not the kind that runs on novelty,
but the kind the years quietly grow.
en.editorNote: A June wedding-season fit — sunshine, inside jokes, and 'love the years grow' fill a card nicely. Warmer than a formal blessing, more grown-up than a one-liner
variations: Happy wedding day — June sunshine, overflowing laughter, and a love the years grow deeper, not newer.

items.sourceNote: 搞笑一句話 register（婚姻三個戒指梗），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
他們說婚姻有三個戒指：

求婚戒、結婚戒，
還有——吵架完誰先去買飲料的「服軟戒」。

前兩個是金的，
第三個才是真的撐一輩子的那個。

敬你們，三個都戴好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 敬酒短梗——「三個戒指」是老笑話，但把第三個改成台灣味的「服軟戒（買飲料）」就活過來了，破冰後直接舉杯，全場好接
variations: 婚姻有三個戒指：求婚戒、結婚戒，和吵架後願意先傳「在幹嘛」的那個戒指。第三個最難戴，但最值錢。

en.content:
They say marriage has three rings:

the engagement ring, the wedding ring,
and the "I'll-go-grab-the-snacks" ring you put on after a fight.

The first two are gold.
The third one is the one that actually lasts a lifetime.

Here's to wearing all three.
en.editorNote: Short toast bit — the 'three rings' joke is old, but swapping the tired 'suffer-ring' punchline for the make-up gesture (going to grab snacks first) revives it. Get the laugh, raise the glass
variations: Marriage has three rings: the engagement ring, the wedding ring, and the one where you text 'you okay?' first after a fight. The third's the hardest to wear and worth the most.

items.sourceNote: Velveteen Rabbit「Real」概念 2026 真實趨勢，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
有一本童書說，
一隻玩偶被愛到舊了、毛掉了、眼睛掉了一顆，
才終於變成「真的」。

你們的愛情也會這樣。

會有皺紋、會有口角、會有狼狽的時刻，
但那不是磨損——
那是你們被彼此愛到變成真的的證據。
zh-tw.editorNote: 呼應2026婚禮「真實感」趨勢——把絨毛兔「被愛到變真」的概念翻成婚姻會老會舊但更真，致詞中段轉煽情用，文青賓客特別吃這套
variations: 玩偶要被愛到掉毛才變真的，婚姻也是——願你們愛到舊，舊到真，真到誰都拆不散。

en.content:
There's a children's book where a stuffed rabbit
gets loved until its fur rubs off and an eye falls out —
and only then does it finally become Real.

Your love will be like that too.

There'll be wrinkles, arguments, unglamorous days.
But that's not wear and tear —
that's the proof you've been loved into something Real.
en.editorNote: Leans into the 2026 'authenticity' wedding trend — reworks the Velveteen Rabbit's 'loved into Real' idea so the aging and the fights become evidence, not damage. A mid-speech emotional pivot
variations: The rabbit only became Real after it got worn out from being loved. May you two get gloriously worn out — loved until you're undeniably, unbreakably Real.

items.sourceNote: 現代紅包賀詞 register，台灣生活畫面，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
新婚快樂。

願你們的導航永遠不吵架，
回家的路永遠不塞，
冰箱裡永遠有對方留的最後一塊，
手機永遠先看到對方的訊息。

小事順了，日子就甜了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 四句排比、全是台灣人懂的日常小確幸——紅包寫得進、留言本貼得上，比「百年好合」有畫面，年輕長輩都接收得到
variations: 願你們導航不吵架、回家不塞車、冰箱有對方留的最後一塊——小事都順，就是最大的祝福。

en.content:
Happy wedding day.

May your GPS never argue with you,
the drive home never hit traffic,
the last slice in the fridge always be saved for the other one,
and each other's texts always be the first you open.

Get the small stuff right, and the days turn sweet.
en.editorNote: Four parallel lines of everyday small joys — fits a card, signs a guestbook. More vivid than a formal blessing, warm enough that older and younger guests both get it
variations: May your GPS never bicker, your drive home never jam, and the last slice in the fridge always have your name on it. Small things smooth, days stay sweet.

items.sourceNote: 「愛上同一個人很多次」概念，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
有人說，
幸福的婚姻要愛上很多次——
而且每一次都是同一個人。

我會愛上現在的你，
也會愛上十年後比較囉嗦的你，
會愛上生氣的你、生病的你、
半夜打呼吵到我的你。

同一個人，我願意重新愛上一萬次。
zh-tw.editorNote: 誓詞 / 致詞通用——把「愛上同一個人很多次」這句名言展開成具體的好幾個版本的「你」，比抽象的『永遠愛你』可信，也更有層次
variations: 我答應你的不是只愛你一次，是每隔一陣子就重新愛上你一次——而且永遠都是你。

en.content:
Someone said
a happy marriage means falling in love many times —
always with the same person.

I'll fall for the you standing here today.
I'll fall for the slightly naggier you ten years from now,
the angry you, the sick you,
the you that snores and wakes me at 3 a.m.

Same person. I'd fall all over again, ten thousand times.
en.editorNote: Works as a vow or a toast — unpacks the classic 'fall in love many times with the same person' line into specific future versions of the partner. More believable than abstract 'forever,' and it builds
variations: I'm not promising to love you once. I'm promising to keep falling for you, over and over — and it'll always, only, be you.

items.sourceNote: 阿公阿嬤的婚姻智慧 register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我問過結婚五十年的阿嬤，
他們的祕訣是什麼。

她想了一下，說：
「沒什麼祕訣，
就是吵架不過夜、
難過的日子一起笑掉、
然後到老了還是牽著手去看醫生。」

今天，我把這句話送給你們。
zh-tw.editorNote: 引用長輩真實對話最有重量——適合長輩致詞或新人引述。把「祕訣」拆成三個具體動作，比任何成語都好記、好抄
variations: 結婚五十年的阿嬤說，祕訣就是吵架不過夜、難過一起笑掉、老了還牽著手——這三件事，做一輩子就夠了。

en.content:
I asked my grandma, married fifty years,
what the secret was.

She thought for a moment and said:
"No secret really —
never go to bed angry,
laugh the bad days away together,
and still hold hands to the doctor when you're old."

Today, I'm passing that on to you two.
en.editorNote: Quoting a real elder carries weight no cliché can — great for a grandparent's toast or a couple citing wisdom. Breaks 'the secret' into three concrete actions that stick
variations: My grandma, fifty years married, said the secret is simple: never sleep angry, laugh the bad days away, still hold hands to the doctor when you're old. That's the whole thing.

items.sourceNote: 現代搞笑 register（傳訊息日常梗），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
大家都把婚姻講得很浪漫。

但說真的，
婚姻有百分之九十，
是互相傳訊息問：
「家裡還有沒有衛生紙？」

剩下的百分之十，
是你發現，
問你這句話的人，
是你最想一起變老的人。

敬那百分之十。
zh-tw.editorNote: 搞笑致詞中段神句——先用「衛生紙訊息」這個全場都懂的日常破冰，再用「百分之十」反轉煽情，笑完馬上被打動
variations: 婚姻百分之九十是傳訊息問「還有沒有牛奶」，百分之十是你發現，那個人你想問一輩子。敬那百分之十。

en.content:
Everyone makes marriage sound so romantic.

But honestly,
ninety percent of it
is texting each other:
"Do we still have toilet paper?"

The other ten percent
is realizing
the person asking you that
is the one you most want to grow old with.

Here's to the ten percent.
en.editorNote: Mid-speech gold — open with the universally relatable 'do we have milk' text, then flip to the ten-percent line for the sincere turn. Laugh first, melt second
variations: Marriage is ninety percent texting 'do we need milk?' and ten percent realizing you want to ask that one person forever. Cheers to the ten percent.

items.sourceNote: 致詞長度自嘲梗（迷你裙比喻），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
有人跟我說，
婚禮致詞要像一條好的領帶——

夠短，大家才不會睡著；
夠長，重點才講得完。

所以我盡量。
如果我超時了，
請看新郎的表情，
他比鬧鐘準。
zh-tw.editorNote: 破冰開場萬用——用「領帶長度」自嘲致詞時間，再把「新郎表情當計時器」帶進來，全場立刻笑、壓力瞬間歸零
variations: 好的婚禮致詞要像領帶：短到不無聊，長到夠講重點。我會抓好分寸——抓不好的話，新娘會提醒我。

en.content:
Someone once told me
a wedding speech should be like a good tie —

short enough that nobody nods off,
long enough to cover the point.

So I'll do my best.
If I run over,
just watch the groom's face —
he's more accurate than a timer.
en.editorNote: Universal icebreaker opener — the 'good tie' line self-deprecates about length, then hands the room a running gag (the groom's face as a clock). Instant laugh, zero risk
variations: A good wedding speech is like a tie: short enough to stay interesting, long enough to cover the essentials. I'll aim for that — and if I miss, the bride will let me know.

items.sourceNote: 婚禮諧音雙關 register，中文在地化重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我今天特地查了字典——

「幸福」這兩個字，
上面都有一個寶蓋頭，
意思是要有一個家，把對方蓋住、護好。

「囍」這個字，
是兩個喜疊在一起——
你的喜，加上他的喜，
才算數。

敬你們，從今天開始，喜上加喜。
zh-tw.editorNote: 中文在地化的拆字哏——把「幸福」「囍」拆開講出新意，比英文諧音梗更貼台灣婚宴，長輩晚輩都聽得懂、會點頭
variations: 「囍」就是兩個喜疊在一起，你的喜加他的喜才算數。敬你們，喜上加喜，一輩子。

en.content:
I looked it up today —

they say marriage has a nice ring to it.
Turns out, so does the bride.

For butter or worse,
through thick and thin frosting,
here's a toast to two people
who finally found their other half
(of the cake).

Aisle drink to that.
en.editorNote: A pun-stacked toast for a lighthearted crowd — 'nice ring,' 'for butter or worse,' 'aisle drink to that.' Lands best when delivered fast and shameless. Keep it to one breath
variations: Marriage has a nice ring to it — and so does the bride. For butter or worse, aisle drink to that. Cheers!

items.sourceNote: 團隊 / 隊友比喻 register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
婚姻不是一場比賽，
你們不是對手。

你們是同一隊的。

所以以後——
不要急著贏對方，
要急著一起贏生活。

吵架的時候記得：
你們的目標從來不是分出對錯，
是兩個人都好好的，走到最後。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「同一隊」比喻最實用——適合致詞或誓詞，把「不要爭輸贏、要一起贏生活」講清楚，是婚姻最該記住的一句，長輩朋友都受用
variations: 你們是隊友，不是對手。吵架時記得：目標不是分對錯，是兩個人都好好走到最後。

en.content:
Marriage isn't a competition.
You're not each other's opponents.

You're on the same team.

So from here on —
don't rush to win against each other,
rush to win at life together.

And when you fight, remember:
the goal was never to decide who's right.
It's for both of you to come out okay,
all the way to the end.
en.editorNote: The 'same team' frame is the most useful line in any toast or vow — it reframes fighting from win/lose to win-together. The one piece of advice worth keeping
variations: You're teammates, not opponents. When you fight, remember: the goal isn't who's right, it's both of you making it all the way, okay.

items.sourceNote: 搞笑致詞 register（日常瑣事比喻），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
婚姻說穿了，
就是找到一個你願意煩他一輩子的人。

煩他洗碗沒洗乾淨，
煩他襪子又亂丟，
煩他冷氣到底要開幾度。

恭喜你們，
從今天起正式擁有彼此這個
可以煩一輩子的對象。
zh-tw.editorNote: 致詞破冰用——「願意煩他一輩子」這個反差笑點，把婚姻講得真實又溫暖，全場會笑、新人會點頭。冷氣溫度之爭是所有同居過的人的共同記憶
variations: 婚姻就是找到一個你不介意每天為了冷氣幾度跟他吵的人，然後一吵就吵到白頭。

en.content:
Marriage, when you boil it down,
is finding the one person
you want to annoy for the rest of your life.

Annoy them about the dishes they didn't rinse.
Annoy them about the socks on the floor.
Annoy them about what temperature the AC should actually be.

Congratulations —
starting today, you officially have
someone to bug forever.
en.editorNote: Toast ice-breaker — the 'someone to annoy forever' reversal makes marriage sound real and warm at once. The thermostat war is a memory every cohabiting couple shares
variations: Marriage is finding the one person you don't mind fighting with about the thermostat every single day — and then doing it until you're both gray.

items.sourceNote: 搞笑致詞 register（簡訊日常比喻），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
大家以為婚姻是天天浪漫。

其實婚姻百分之九十，
是互相傳訊息問：
「家裡還有沒有牛奶？」
「你吃了沒？」
「你在哪？我到了。」

但你知道嗎——
能有一個人，永遠在乎你有沒有喝到牛奶，
那就是這輩子最浪漫的事了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 致詞中段轉煽情神器——先用「問牛奶」的反高潮逗笑，再翻成「有人在乎你」的溫暖。日常簡訊是每對情侶都懂的畫面，比抽象祝福更打動人
variations: 婚姻不是電影裡的浪漫，是有一個人每天問你吃飽沒、喝水沒、到家沒——這種瑣碎的在乎，才是真的。

en.content:
Everyone thinks marriage is romance every day.

The truth is, ninety percent of marriage
is texting each other:
"Do we still have milk?"
"Did you eat yet?"
"Where are you? I'm here."

But here's the thing —
having one person who always cares
whether you got your milk
is the most romantic thing in the world.
en.editorNote: A mid-speech pivot to sincerity — open with the anticlimax of 'do we have milk' for the laugh, then flip it into the warmth of being cared for. The everyday text is something every couple recognizes
variations: Marriage isn't the movie romance. It's someone asking every day if you ate, if you drank water, if you got home — and that small, constant caring is the real thing.

items.sourceNote: 搞笑致詞 register（「你說得對」婚姻祕訣），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我要送新郎一句
幸福婚姻的祕訣，
只有三個字——

「你說得對。」

不管什麼時候，
不管在吵什麼，
把這三個字學起來，
你後半輩子會非常順利。

敬一段，永遠你說得對的婚姻。
zh-tw.editorNote: 敬酒前的萬用笑點——「你說得對」是跨文化都懂的婚姻梗，伴郎、長輩、同事致詞都能用，逗笑全場又不得罪任何一方
variations: 送新郎三個字保平安：「你說得對。」送新娘三個字保幸福：「他會懂的。」

en.content:
Let me give the groom
the secret to a happy marriage.
It's just three little words —

"You're right, honey."

Doesn't matter when.
Doesn't matter what the argument is.
Learn those three words,
and the rest of your life runs smooth.

Here's to a marriage where you're always right.
en.editorNote: A reliable pre-toast laugh — 'you're right, honey' is a cross-cultural marriage joke that works for best men, elders, and coworkers alike. Lands the laugh without offending either side
variations: Three words for the groom: 'You're right, honey.' Three words for the bride: 'He'll learn eventually.'

items.sourceNote: 祖父母 / 長輩致詞 register（長久婚姻的權威），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我跟你們的奶奶，
在一起五十年了。

我不會講什麼大道理，
我只告訴你們一句話——

吵架可以，
但永遠不要忘記，
你身邊那個人，
是當初你拼了命也要在一起的人。

記住這句，你們就會走得很遠。
zh-tw.editorNote: 長輩 / 祖父母致詞最有重量的版本——五十年婚齡本身就是權威，不需要華麗詞藻，一句『拼了命也要在一起』勝過一切。建議致詞時放慢、停頓，全場必安靜
variations: 我們吵了五十年，也牽了五十年的手。祕訣只有一個：永遠記得當初為什麼選對方。

en.content:
Your grandmother and I
have been together fifty years.

I'm not going to give you a big speech.
I'll give you one line —

Go ahead and argue.
But never, ever forget
that the person beside you
is the one you once fought tooth and nail to be with.

Remember that, and you'll go a long, long way.
en.editorNote: The heaviest version of a grandparent / elder toast — fifty years of marriage is its own authority, no fancy words needed. 'The one you fought tooth and nail to be with' beats any flourish. Deliver slowly, with a pause; the room goes silent
variations: We've argued for fifty years and held hands for fifty years. One secret: never forget why you chose each other in the first place.

items.sourceNote: 「婚姻是友情的最高級」概念，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
最好的婚姻，
不是兩個情人，
是兩個最好的朋友，
剛好也愛上彼此。

戀人會吵架、會冷掉，
但好朋友會把問題講開、
會笑著陪你度過爛日子。

你們兩個——
剛好兩種身分都是。
zh-tw.editorNote: 好友致詞中段轉煽情用——「兩個最好的朋友剛好相愛」這個角度比「他們很相愛」更有層次，把婚姻拉回友情的本質，全場會點頭
variations: 你們不只是愛人，你們是彼此最好的朋友——這才是會走得久的那種。

en.content:
The best marriages
aren't two lovers.

They're two best friends
who happened to fall for each other.

Lovers fight, lovers cool off.
But best friends talk it out,
and laugh you through the bad days.

You two
just happen to be both.
en.editorNote: The emotional pivot in a close-friend's toast — 'two best friends who happened to fall in love' adds a layer that plain 'they love each other' can't reach. Pulls marriage back to its friendship core
variations: You're not just lovers — you're each other's best friend. That's the version that lasts.

items.sourceNote: 閨蜜俏皮祝福 register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
當她的閨蜜二十年，
我聽過她講最多的一句話是：
「這個我先不要。」

衣服她說先不要、
約會對象她說先不要、
甚至甜點她都會猶豫三秒。

結果她看到你，
第一次什麼都沒說，
直接點頭。

所以你別緊張——
你是她這輩子第一個沒有「先不要」的選擇。
zh-tw.editorNote: 閨蜜致詞神級結構——先吐槽她猶豫不決，最後反轉成「但她選你毫不猶豫」。把口頭禪「先不要」當笑點，全場會笑、新郎會被融化
variations: 她對什麼都會說『再想想』，唯獨對你，她連零點一秒都沒想。

en.content:
I've been her best friend for twenty years,
and the thing I've heard her say the most is:
"Mm, maybe not."

Clothes — maybe not.
Dates — maybe not.
She'll hesitate three seconds over dessert.

Then she met you,
and for the first time
she didn't say a thing.
She just nodded.

So relax —
you're the first choice in her life
that never got a 'maybe not.'
en.editorNote: Best-friend speech gold — roast her chronic indecision, then flip to 'but she picked you instantly.' Her catchphrase becomes the punchline. Reliable laugh, groom melts
variations: She says 'let me think about it' to everything. About you, she didn't think for even a tenth of a second.

items.sourceNote: 聯機遊戲比喻 register（隊友概念），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
新婚快樂。

從今天起，
你們開了一場沒有登出鍵的聯機。

願你們永遠在同一隊、
血量不夠的時候互相補、
打不過的時候不會嫌隊友爛，
而是說一句「沒事，再來一次」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 聯機遊戲比喻適合年輕朋友圈——「再來一次」這個關鍵字是記憶點，比官式賀詞更有共鳴。紅包、IG、留言本通用
variations: 願你們永遠是同一隊，永遠不互相棄坑，永遠在對方倒下的時候說『沒事，再來一次』。

en.content:
Happy wedding day.

Starting today,
you've joined a co-op game with no log-out button.

May you always be on the same team,
heal each other when health runs low,
and when a level beats you,
never blame your teammate —
just say, "It's fine, let's run it again."
en.editorNote: Co-op gaming metaphor for a younger crowd — 'let's run it again' is the keeper line, lands harder than a formal blessing. Fits a card, caption, or guestbook
variations: May you always be on the same team, never rage-quit on each other, and always say 'it's fine, run it again' when one of you goes down.

items.sourceNote: 「分擔減半、分享加倍」概念，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
結婚之後，
你們的麻煩會除以二，
你們的開心會乘以二。

壞消息有人一起扛，
好消息有人一起跳。

敬一個從今天起，
沒有什麼事情需要一個人面對的人生。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「分擔減半、分享加倍」的老概念寫得口語——適合敬酒結尾，數學比喻簡單好記，賓客一聽就懂、新人會被打動
variations: 從今天起，難過的事有人分一半，開心的事有人多給一份。敬你們的乘除法。

en.content:
After today,
your troubles get divided by two,
and your joys get multiplied by two.

Bad news, someone helps you carry.
Good news, someone jumps with you.

Here's to a life where, starting now,
nothing has to be faced alone.
en.editorNote: The classic 'halved sorrows, doubled joys' idea in plain speech — a clean toast closer. The math metaphor is instantly understood and quietly moving
variations: From today, your sorrows split in half and your joys come back doubled. Here's to the arithmetic of the two of you.

items.sourceNote: 短句紅包 register，平凡日常畫面，原創

zh-tw.content:
新婚快樂。

願你們最浪漫的事，
不是燭光晚餐，
是某個普通的傍晚，
一個問「晚餐吃什麼」，
另一個說「都可以，你想吃我就想吃」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 短到能寫紅包、暖到能當留言——避開「燭光晚餐」的浪漫套路，把幸福放在「晚餐吃什麼」這種日常對話裡，記憶點強、所有人都共鳴
variations: 願你們的浪漫不在特別的日子，而在每一個『晚餐吃什麼』『你想吃我就想吃』的平凡傍晚。

en.content:
Happy wedding day.

May your most romantic moment
not be the candlelit dinner —
but some ordinary evening
when one of you asks, "What's for dinner?"
and the other says,
"Whatever you want is what I want."
en.editorNote: Short enough for a red envelope, warm enough for a guestbook — skips the candlelit-dinner cliché and lands romance in a 'what's for dinner' exchange everyone recognizes
variations: May your romance live not in the special days, but in every ordinary 'what's for dinner' / 'whatever you want is fine' evening.

items.sourceNote: 搞笑開場 register（AI 寫不出來的愛），現代科技梗，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我本來想偷懶，
叫AI幫我寫這篇致詞。

結果它跑出一行字：
「資料不足，世上沒有這麼剛好的兩個人。」

所以接下來只能我自己講了——
因為連機器都算不出你們有多配。
zh-tw.editorNote: 致詞開場破冰神器——用「AI寫不出來」當笑點，順勢變成對新人的大讚美，2026年最吃香的科技梗，全場會笑也會暖
variations: 我問AI你們的愛情有多難得，它想了三秒，回我一句『超出計算範圍』。所以今天請容許我這個人類，慢慢說給你們聽。

en.content:
I was going to be lazy
and have AI write this speech.

It spat out one line:
"Error: a couple this well-matched does not compute."

So I guess I'm on my own here —
because even a machine can't calculate how perfect you two are.
en.editorNote: An icebreaker opener built on the 'AI can't write it' joke that flips into a genuine compliment — the most on-trend tech bit for 2026, gets the laugh and the warmth at once
variations: I asked AI how rare your love is. It thought for three seconds and said 'beyond computational range.' So tonight, let this human take it from here.

items.sourceNote: 祖父母 / 長輩智慧 register（友誼是地基），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我阿公阿嬤結婚快六十年了。

我問阿嬤秘訣，
她想了一下說：
「我們先是朋友，後來才是夫妻，
到現在，還是朋友。」

願你們也是——
先愛上對方，
再喜歡上那個可以一起變老的朋友。
zh-tw.editorNote: 長輩致詞最有重量的一段——引用真實的「六十年婚姻」當佐證，把秘訣濃縮成「先是朋友」，比抽象祝福更可信，全場長輩會猛點頭
variations: 婚姻能走遠的關鍵，不是天天浪漫，是你愛的那個人，剛好也是你最好的朋友。

en.content:
My grandparents have been married almost sixty years.

I once asked my grandma the secret.
She thought about it and said:
"We were friends first,
then we were married,
and we're still friends now."

May you two be the same —
fall in love first,
then fall for the friend you get to grow old with.
en.editorNote: The most weighted line for an older speaker — anchors the blessing in a real sixty-year marriage and distills it to 'friends first.' More credible than any abstract wish; the older guests will nod hard
variations: What carries a marriage the distance isn't daily romance — it's that the person you love also happens to be your best friend.

items.sourceNote: 祖父母賀詞 register（在不順的日子也選擇彼此），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
新婚快樂。

願你們在心情好的時候相愛，
在心情爛的星期一也相愛，
吵架時快點原諒，
肚子餓時記得一起吃點什麼。

婚姻沒有那麼難，
就是天天重新選對方一次。
zh-tw.editorNote: 長輩寫給晚輩的紅包賀詞——避開官腔，把祝福放在「心情爛的星期一」這種真實時刻，溫暖又接地氣，年輕人看了也買單
variations: 願你們在每一個不想愛的日子，還是選擇對方；在每一次餓到生氣前，記得一起吃飯。

en.content:
Happy wedding day.

May you love each other on the good days,
and on the moody Mondays too.
Forgive quickly when you fight,
and remember to snack together when you're hungry.

Marriage isn't that complicated —
it's just choosing each other, fresh, every single day.
en.editorNote: A grandparent-to-grandchild card message that skips the formal register and lands the blessing on 'moody Mondays' and snacking — warm, grounded, and quotable for any generation
variations: May you keep choosing each other on the days you don't feel like it — and may you always eat before you get angry.

items.sourceNote: 搞笑致詞 register（婚禮花費 / 同居現實），現代梗，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
他們交往的時候，
為了「衛生紙要朝內還朝外」吵過一架。
為了「冷氣幾度」冷戰過兩天。
為了「這沙發到底買不買」研究了三個月。

結果今天，
他們願意為了彼此辦一場
比那張沙發貴一百倍的婚禮。

這就是愛。
zh-tw.editorNote: 敬酒致詞中段笑點——用同居才懂的雞毛蒜皮（衛生紙、冷氣、沙發）鋪陳，最後用「婚禮比沙發貴一百倍」反轉收尾，全場會笑、新人會認，超有共鳴
variations: 他們可以為了一張沙發吵三個月，卻在三秒內決定要共度一輩子。愛情的數學，從來就不講道理。

en.content:
While they were dating,
they fought over which way the toilet paper should hang.
They cold-warred for two days over the thermostat.
They spent three months deciding whether to buy that couch.

And yet today,
they willingly threw a wedding
that costs a hundred times more than that couch.

That, my friends, is love.
en.editorNote: A mid-speech laugh line built on real cohabitation friction (toilet paper, thermostat, the couch) that flips into 'a wedding that costs more than the couch' — relatable, lands the laugh, and the couple will own it
variations: They argued for three months over a couch, then decided in three seconds to spend a lifetime together. The math of love has never made sense.

items.sourceNote: 祖父母 / 長輩視角致詞 register（過來人經驗），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
五十年前，
我跟你阿嬤也站在你們今天站的位置。

那時候我們什麼都不知道——
不知道會有幾個孩子，
不知道會搬幾次家，
不知道日子會怎麼把我們磨成現在這樣。

現在回頭看，
那個「什麼都不知道」，
才是最好玩的部分。

日子難的時候，抓緊對方。
音樂沒響的時候，也記得跳一下舞。
zh-tw.editorNote: 祖父母 / 長輩致詞的最高境界——用「五十年前我也站在那裡」開場，把人生經驗濃縮成兩句具體建議（抓緊對方、沒音樂也跳舞），全場長輩會點頭、年輕人會起雞皮疙瘩
variations: 我跟你阿嬤吵了五十年，也牽手了五十年。秘訣只有一個：吵歸吵，手不要放開。

en.content:
Fifty years ago,
your grandmother and I stood right where you two are tonight.

We didn't know a thing —
not how many kids would come,
not how many times we'd move,
not how the years would slowly shape us into this.

Looking back,
that not-knowing
was the best part.

When the days get hard, hold on to each other.
And dance a little, even when there's no music playing.
en.editorNote: The highest register for a grandparent's toast — open with 'fifty years ago I stood there too,' then distill a lifetime into two concrete instructions (hold on, dance with no music). Elders nod, the young get chills
variations: Your grandmother and I have argued for fifty years and held hands for fifty years. The secret is just one thing: argue all you want, but never let go of the hand.

items.sourceNote: 現代誓詞 register（雜貨袋 / 爛笑話具體承諾），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我答應你——

買菜的時候，重的那袋我提。
你講的爛笑話，再爛我都笑。
搶被子這件事，我大概贏不了，
但我願意輸一輩子。

生活不用完美才美好，
只要是跟你一起，
連決定今天吃什麼，都變簡單了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 現代誓詞範本——三個超具體的日常承諾（提重的袋、笑爛笑話、被子認輸）勝過任何「天長地久」，最後用『連決定吃什麼都變簡單』收尾，真摯又不矯情
variations: 我不答應你完美的人生，我答應你：重的袋我提、爛的笑話我笑、被子我認輸——這些小事，我做一輩子。

en.content:
Here's what I promise you —

I'll carry the heavy grocery bag.
I'll laugh at your bad jokes, no matter how bad.
I'll probably never win the battle for the covers,
but I'm happy to lose it for life.

Life doesn't have to be perfect to be wonderful.
With you, even deciding what's for dinner
feels easy.
en.editorNote: Modern vow template — three hyper-specific daily promises (heavy bag, bad jokes, losing the cover war) beat any 'forever and always,' closing on 'even deciding what's for dinner feels easy.' Sincere without the syrup
variations: I won't promise you a perfect life. I'll promise you this: the heavy bag is mine, your worst jokes get my laugh, and the covers are yours. Small things, for a lifetime.

items.sourceNote: 搞笑致詞 register（短期記憶 / 婚姻秘訣 one-liner），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我問過一對結婚四十年的長輩，
幸福婚姻的秘訣是什麼。

他們說兩個字：
幽默感，
還有——
超爛的短期記憶。

敬這對新人，
願你們笑得多、記仇得少。
zh-tw.editorNote: 敬酒前的萬用笑點——「幽默感＋超爛的短期記憶」是經典結構，先讓全場笑出來，再轉「笑得多、記仇得少」收尾，破冰兼祝福一次到位
variations: 幸福婚姻的兩大支柱：一是笑點要低，二是記性要差。敬你們，祝你們兩樣都有。

en.content:
I once asked a couple married forty years
for the secret to a happy marriage.

They gave me two words:
a sense of humor —
and a truly terrible short-term memory.

So here's to the newlyweds.
May you laugh often
and forget the fights fast.
en.editorNote: A reliable pre-toast laugh — the 'humor plus terrible short-term memory' structure is a classic. Get the room laughing, then pivot to 'laugh often, forget the fights fast.' Ice-breaker and blessing in one
variations: The two pillars of a happy marriage: a low bar for jokes and a bad memory for grudges. Here's to you both having plenty of both.

items.sourceNote: 好友致詞 register（見證關係轉變），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我認識他的時候，
他是一個人吃飯、一個人看電影、
一個人把所有事情扛下來的那種人。

直到他遇到你。

現在他吃飯會等人、
看電影會留一個位子、
難過的時候，
終於知道要先打給誰。

謝謝你，把他從一個人，
變成了兩個人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 好友致詞最有畫面的版本——用「一個人吃飯 / 看電影」鋪陳對方以前的孤單，再用三個轉變收尾，比「他變得更好了」具體一百倍，全場會被觸動
variations: 他以前什麼事都自己扛，遇到你以後才學會：難過的時候，原來可以先打給一個人。謝謝你成為那個人。

en.content:
When I first met him,
he was the kind of guy who ate alone,
watched movies alone,
carried everything alone.

Then he met you.

Now he waits for someone before he eats.
He saves a seat at the movies.
And when he's down,
he finally knows who to call first.

Thank you
for turning him from one person
into two.
en.editorNote: The most vivid version of a close-friend speech — set up his old solitude with 'ate alone, watched movies alone,' then land three concrete changes. A hundred times more specific than 'he's become a better person.' The room feels it
variations: He used to carry everything by himself. Since you, he's learned that when it's hard, there's someone to call first. Thank you for being that someone.

items.sourceNote: 短句紅包 / 賓客留言本 register，現代生活畫面，原創

zh-tw.content:
新婚快樂。

願你們吵架的時候，
還是會把對方那份飯留好；
冷戰的時候，
還是會偷偷幫對方充好手機；
生氣的時候，
還是會在睡前把腳伸過去碰一下。

愛不在順利的時候，在這些時候。
zh-tw.editorNote: 賓客留言本 / 紅包賀詞的進階版——避開「白頭偕老」，用三個吵架冷戰時的小動作（留飯、充電、碰腳）寫祝福，溫暖又有記憶點，年輕人特別買單
variations: 願你們在最生氣的時候，還是會留好對方那份飯。愛就藏在這種不甘願的溫柔裡。

en.content:
Happy wedding day.

May you, even mid-argument,
still save the other one a plate;
even in the silent treatment,
still secretly charge their phone;
even when you're furious,
still reach a foot over to touch theirs before sleep.

Love isn't in the easy moments —
it's in these.
en.editorNote: An elevated guestbook / red-envelope message — skips 'grow old together' for three small acts during fights (saving a plate, charging the phone, the foot under the covers). Warm, memorable, and it lands with younger guests
variations: May you, even at your angriest, still save the other one a plate. Love hides in that reluctant kind of tenderness.

items.sourceNote: 搞笑致詞 register（婚姻＝一起變老的日常吐槽），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
婚姻不只是愛與信任。

婚姻是找到一個人，
讓你願意吵一輩子的「冷氣要開幾度」。

是傳一輩子的
「家裡是不是沒牛奶了」。

恭喜你們找到了
那個值得吵到老的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 致詞破冰用——把婚姻吐槽成「冷氣溫度大戰」和「沒牛奶了」這種所有同居過的人都懂的日常，笑點精準，接受度高
variations: 婚姻就是找到一個你願意為了冷氣溫度吵一輩子，但又捨不得分房睡的人。恭喜你們。

en.content:
Marriage isn't just about love and trust.

It's about finding the one person
you're willing to fight with forever
over the thermostat.

It's about texting each other
"do we need milk?"
for the rest of your lives.

Congrats on finding
the person worth all of that.
en.editorNote: Toast icebreaker — frames marriage as 'thermostat wars' and the eternal 'do we need milk?' text, the universal stuff anyone who's lived with a partner recognizes. Reliable laugh
variations: Marriage is finding someone you'll argue with about the thermostat forever, and still never want to sleep in separate rooms. Congrats.

items.sourceNote: 搞笑致詞 register（AI 寫稿自嘲），2026 現代梗，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我本來想叫AI幫我寫這篇致詞。

我輸入了他們兩個的故事，
結果AI回我：

「資料異常，這種程度的相愛
超出系統運算範圍。」

所以接下來這幾句，
是我自己寫的——
雖然爛，但至少是真心的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 2026 必紅的AI梗開場——用「AI算不出他們有多相愛」當笑點，順便自嘲「我寫的雖然爛但真心」，全場會笑、新人會暖
variations: 我問AI怎麼形容他們的感情，AI想了三秒，回我：「建議直接放棄形容。」所以我決定，用我自己的話，慢慢講。

en.content:
I tried to get AI to write this speech for me.

I fed it their whole story,
and it replied:

"Error: love this perfect
exceeds system parameters."

So the next few lines
are all mine —
clumsy, but at least they're real.
en.editorNote: The 2026 go-to AI opener — 'a computer couldn't compute their love' as the joke, then a self-deprecating pivot to 'mine are clumsy but real.' Gets the laugh, earns the warmth
variations: I asked AI to describe their relationship. It thought for three seconds and said, 'Recommend you don't try.' So I'll just use my own words, slowly.

items.sourceNote: 現代誓詞 register（平等夥伴概念），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我答應站在你旁邊，
不是站在你前面保護你，
也不是站在你後面靠著你——

是並肩。

你的夢想，我當成自己的；
你的難關，我陪你一起卡關。

我們不複製別人的劇本，
我們慢慢寫一個只有我們看得懂的版本。
zh-tw.editorNote: 現代誓詞的高級版——避開「我會保護你 / 照顧你」這種把對方放低的說法，改用「並肩」「平等」，把另一半當隊友而非被照顧者，更現代也更被尊重
variations: 我不站在你前面，也不站在你後面，我站在你旁邊——當你的隊友，不當你的依靠或包袱。

en.content:
I promise to stand beside you.

Not in front of you to shield you,
not behind you to lean on you —

beside.

I'll treat your dreams as my own,
and when you're stuck, I'll get stuck right there with you.

We're not copying anyone's script.
We're slowly writing the one
only we will ever understand.
en.editorNote: An elevated modern vow — drops 'I'll protect you / take care of you' (which quietly puts your partner below you) for 'beside you, as equals.' Treats your partner as a teammate, not someone to be managed
variations: Not in front of you, not behind you — beside you. Your teammate, not your crutch and not your burden.

items.sourceNote: 誓詞 register（「我得以」privilege 句型），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我不把這些當作承諾，
我把它們當作我「得到」的權利——

我得以跟你一起笑，也一起哭；
得以照顧你，也被你照顧；
得以跟你一起跑，也一起慢慢走；
得以跟你一起蓋一個家，再一起住進去。

我得以，把你當成
我接下來一輩子要一起過的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 誓詞中最溫柔的句型——把「我承諾」改成「我得以（我很幸運能夠）」，瞬間從義務變成感恩，整段誓詞的重量完全不同，新人讀的時候自己會先哽咽
variations: 這些不是我欠你的承諾，是我有幸得到的事：跟你一起笑、一起哭、一起慢慢變老。

en.content:
I don't see these as promises.
I see them as privileges I get —

I get to laugh with you, and cry with you.
I get to care for you, and be cared for by you.
I get to run with you, and walk slowly with you.
I get to build a home with you, and then live in it.

I get to make you
the person I spend the rest of my life beside.
en.editorNote: The most tender vow structure there is — swap 'I promise' for 'I get to,' and the whole thing turns from obligation into gratitude. Couples often choke up reading it themselves
variations: These aren't debts I owe you. They're privileges I get: to laugh with you, cry with you, and grow old slowly beside you.

items.sourceNote: 搞笑敬酒 register（三個戒指梗反轉），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
聽說婚姻有三個戒指：

求婚戒、結婚戒，
還有——「受氣」。

開玩笑的。

我認識你們這麼久，
看到的其實是第四個戒指：
不管多氣，最後都還是會牽起對方的手。

敬這第四個。乾杯。
zh-tw.editorNote: 敬酒詞萬用結構——先用「三個戒指」的老梗逗笑，再反轉成「第四個戒指（還是會牽手）」收尾煽情，破冰加催淚一次到位
variations: 婚姻有三個戒指：求婚戒、結婚戒、受氣。但你們有第四個——再氣都還是會牽手的那個。敬第四個。

en.content:
They say marriage has three rings:

the engagement ring, the wedding ring —
and the suffer-ing.

Kidding.

Knowing you two this long,
I've actually seen a fourth ring:
no matter how mad you get,
you always end up holding hands again.

Here's to ring number four. Cheers.
en.editorNote: All-purpose toast structure — open with the well-worn 'three rings' joke, then flip to a fourth ring ('you always hold hands again') for the emotional landing. Icebreaker and tearjerker in one
variations: Marriage has three rings: engagement, wedding, and suffer-ing. But you two have a fourth — the one where you always hold hands again. To ring four.

items.sourceNote: 祖父母 / 長輩致詞 register（用長久婚姻當權威），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我跟你們阿嬤結婚五十年了。

年輕人常問我祕訣，
以為有什麼了不起的答案。

其實沒有。

就是每次想放棄的時候，
剛好那天她煮了我愛吃的菜；
每次她受不了我的時候，
剛好我把垃圾倒了。

婚姻不是大事撐起來的，
是這些剛剛好，撐了五十年。
zh-tw.editorNote: 長輩 / 祖父母致詞最有重量的版本——長久婚姻本身就是權威，不用講大道理，用「剛好煮了愛吃的菜」這種具體小事收尾，全場必安靜
variations: 結婚五十年沒什麼祕訣，就是兩個人都在快撐不住的時候，輪流多撐了對方一下，撐著撐著就到今天了。

en.content:
I've been married to her grandmother for fifty years.

Young people keep asking me the secret,
like there's some grand answer.

There isn't.

It's that every time I wanted to give up,
she happened to cook my favorite dish that night.
Every time she couldn't stand me anymore,
I happened to have taken out the trash.

Marriage isn't held up by the big things.
It's these little well-timed ones — for fifty years.
en.editorNote: The grandparent / elder toast carries weight no one else in the room can match — a long marriage is its own credential. Skip the grand wisdom; land it on a small, specific habit. The room goes quiet
variations: Fifty years, no secret. Just two people taking turns carrying each other through the moments they almost couldn't — until somehow it added up to today.

items.sourceNote: 二度婚姻誓詞 register（坦然面對過去、向前看），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我沒想過會再站在這裡。

上一次，我以為自己什麼都懂了。
這一次，我只知道一件事——

我不會假裝過去沒發生，
但我也不會讓它決定我們的未來。

我帶著學過的東西來愛你，
小心一點、誠實一點、
也比以前更感謝一點。

謝謝命運繞了一圈，把你留給我。
zh-tw.editorNote: 二度婚姻誓詞最動人的版本——坦然承認過去但不沉溺，用「帶著學過的東西來愛你」取代「忘掉一切重新開始」，比假裝清白更可信
variations: 我不會假裝這是我第一次相信愛情。但我可以保證，這是我最清楚自己在做什麼的一次。

en.content:
I never thought I'd stand here again.

Last time, I thought I had it all figured out.
This time, I only know one thing —

I won't pretend the past didn't happen,
but I won't let it decide our future either.

I come to love you with everything I've learned:
a little more careful, a little more honest,
a lot more grateful.

Thank you, life, for taking the long way around
and saving you for me.
en.editorNote: The strongest second-marriage vow is honest about the journey without dwelling on it, and forward-looking without ignoring it. 'Everything I've learned' beats pretending it's a blank slate
variations: I won't pretend this is the first time I've believed in love. But I can promise it's the first time I've known exactly what I'm doing.

items.sourceNote: 繼親 / 混合家庭誓詞 register（對孩子說的話），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
今天我不只是娶你媽媽。

小傢伙們，這句話也是說給你們聽的——

我不會取代任何人，
我也不打算假裝是你們的爸爸。

我只想當那個——
你們半夜做惡夢時會去敲門的人，
你們球賽輸了會在場邊等的人，
你們長大以後，會願意打電話回來講垃圾話的人。

從今天起，我們是一家人了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 混合家庭誓詞最珍貴的版本——直接對孩子說話而不是只對伴侶，用「不取代任何人」化解戒心，再用三個具體場景承諾陪伴，孩子聽得懂、大人會哭
variations: 我不會當你們的爸爸，我會當你們多出來的那個人——多一個接送、多一個加油、多一個永遠在你們這邊的人。

en.content:
Today I'm not just marrying your mom.

Kids, this part is for you too —

I'm not here to replace anyone.
I'm not going to pretend to be your dad.

I just want to be the one
you knock on the door for after a bad dream,
the one waiting on the sideline when your team loses,
the one you'll call years from now
just to talk nonsense.

Starting today, we're a family.
en.editorNote: For blended families, speaking vows directly to the stepchildren is deeply meaningful. Disarm with 'I'm not replacing anyone,' then promise presence through three concrete scenes — the kids understand it, the adults cry
variations: I won't be your dad. I'll be the extra one — an extra ride home, an extra cheer, an extra person who is always, always on your side.

items.sourceNote: 搞笑致詞 register（『要不要買牛奶』日常梗），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
有人說，
婚姻就是兩個人互相傳訊息問
「家裡還有沒有衛生紙」，
一直傳到其中一個離開人世為止。

聽起來不浪漫對不對？

但你想想——
能找到一個人，
願意陪你討論衛生紙討論一輩子，
那才是真的浪漫。
zh-tw.editorNote: 致詞破冰神句——先用「傳訊息問衛生紙」逗笑，再反轉成「願意陪你討論一輩子才是浪漫」，笑點加暖點一次收齊，敬酒前用最佳
variations: 婚姻就是把「晚餐吃什麼」這個問題，跟同一個人討論到天荒地老。恭喜你們找到願意一起煩惱這題的人。

en.content:
Someone once said
marriage is just two people texting each other
"do we still have toilet paper?"
until one of them dies.

Doesn't sound romantic, right?

But think about it —
finding one person
willing to discuss toilet paper with you for a lifetime?

That's the real romance.
en.editorNote: Icebreaker gold — open with the universal 'do we need milk / toilet paper' joke, then flip it into 'a lifetime of that IS the romance.' Laugh and warmth in one. Perfect right before a toast
variations: Marriage is debating 'what's for dinner' with the same person until the end of time. Congrats on finding someone willing to be stuck on that question with you forever.

items.sourceNote: 遠距離 register（終於同一個城市），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我們愛了三年，
其中兩年隔著一個時區。

我習慣了對著手機說早安，
習慣了把想說的話打成字、
等你醒來才看得到。

從今天起——
我可以直接轉過頭跟你說話了。

不用等訊號、不用算時差、
不用把擁抱存到下一次見面。

這就是我要的全部：
你，在伸手就摸得到的距離。
zh-tw.editorNote: 遠距離戀愛修成正果的誓詞——用「對手機說早安」「把擁抱存起來」這些遠距特有的細節鋪陳，最後落在「伸手就摸得到的距離」，經歷過遠距的人會瞬間破防
variations: 談了三年遠距離，我最大的願望從來不是什麼海誓山盟，就是早上醒來，你真的在旁邊。今天開始，這個願望成真了。

en.content:
We loved each other for three years —
two of them across a time zone.

I got used to saying good morning to a screen,
used to typing out everything I wanted to say
and waiting for you to wake up and read it.

Starting today —
I can just turn my head and tell you.

No signal to wait for, no time difference to do the math on,
no hugs saved up for the next visit.

This is all I ever wanted:
you, close enough to reach.
en.editorNote: A vow for couples who survived long distance — build with the specific details (good morning to a screen, hugs saved for the next visit), then land on 'close enough to reach.' Anyone who's done long distance loses it
variations: Three years of long distance, and my biggest wish was never some grand forever — it was just waking up and having you actually there. Starting today, that wish is real.

items.sourceNote: AI 梗一行致詞 register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我本來想叫 AI 幫我寫這段致詞，
結果它跑出一行字：

「錯誤：這種程度的相愛，無法運算。」

所以接下來這幾句，
是我自己想的，
可能沒那麼工整，
但至少是真心的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 2026 最應景的致詞開場——先用「叫 AI 寫卻當機」自嘲，再順勢轉成「所以以下是我真心話」，既破冰又顯得誠懇，年輕來賓秒懂
variations: 我問了 AI 怎麼形容你們兩個，它想了三秒回我「資料不足，這種好運世上沒幾筆」。所以我也不囉嗦了，恭喜你們。

en.content:
I tried to get AI to write this toast for me.

It just spat back one line:

"Error: a love this good does not compute."

So whatever I say next
is all me —
probably not as polished,
but at least it's true.
en.editorNote: The most on-brand 2026 opener — self-deprecate with 'I asked AI and it crashed,' then pivot to 'so the rest is from me, for real.' Breaks the ice and earns sincerity points at the same time
variations: I asked AI to describe you two and it just went 'insufficient data — luck this good is statistically rare.' So I'll keep it simple: congratulations.

items.sourceNote: 致詞長度自嘲 register（像新郎服裝一樣簡短），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
新人交代我，
這段致詞要像今天的婚紗——
好看、經典，
而且最好快點結束。

那我就長話短說：

你們很配，我很感動，
敬你們，乾杯。
zh-tw.editorNote: 完美的「短到讓人鬆一口氣」致詞——先用「致詞要像婚紗一樣快點結束」自嘲冗長，再真的三句收尾，全場會因為你守信用而鼓掌
variations: 他們叫我講短一點，像新郎的頭髮一樣——年年都在變少。那我就一句話：恭喜你們，我愛你們，喝。

en.content:
The couple asked me
to keep this toast like today's dress —
gorgeous, timeless,
and ideally, over quickly.

So here it is, short version:

You two are perfect, I'm a wreck,
here's to you. Cheers.
en.editorNote: The perfect 'short enough to be a relief' toast — joke that the speech should end as fast as the dress is elegant, then actually land it in three lines. The room applauds you just for keeping your word
variations: They told me to keep it short, like the groom's hairline — getting thinner every year. So, one line: congrats, I love you both, drink up.

items.sourceNote: 誓詞「每天重新選擇你」register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我今天選擇你，
用一聲安靜、篤定的「好」。

然後——
我會在往後每一個早晨，
醒來時再選一次你。

不是因為非你不可，
是因為每一次睜開眼，
我都還是想要你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 極簡又後勁強的誓詞——核心是「每天早上重新選擇你」，把「結婚」從一次性的承諾變成日復一日的選擇，唸出來時放慢、停在最後一句，效果最好
variations: 結婚不是把你鎖進一個承諾裡，而是我願意每天早上睜開眼，再一次、心甘情願地選你。今天是第一次，我選你。

en.content:
Today I choose you
with one quiet, steady yes.

And then —
every morning after this,
I'll wake up and choose you again.

Not because I have to,
but because every time I open my eyes,
you're still the one I want.
en.editorNote: A minimalist vow with a long tail — the core idea is 'I choose you again every morning,' turning marriage from a one-time promise into a daily choice. Read it slow and let the last line sit
variations: Marriage isn't locking you into a single promise — it's me waking up every morning and willingly choosing you all over again. Today is the first time. I choose you.

items.sourceNote: 六月新娘 register（June bride 季節梗），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
六月的新娘，
聽說會幸福一輩子。

但我覺得，
讓你們幸福的不是這個月份，
是你們願意在最熱的季節、
穿著最厚的禮服，
還是決定要牽手走完全程。

這種傻氣，才是會白頭的那種。
zh-tw.editorNote: 六月喜宴限定的暖句——先借「六月新娘最幸福」的老說法，再反轉成「真正讓你們幸福的是那份願意」，夏季婚禮致詞或紅包都好用
variations: 六月結婚的人，是明知道會曬會熱會流汗，還是堅持要一起走紅毯的人。這份傻勁，剛好就是婚姻最需要的東西。

en.content:
They say a June bride
stays happy for life.

But honestly,
it's not the month that does it.

It's that you two were willing
to wear the heaviest outfits
in the hottest season
and still decide to walk the whole way together.

That kind of stubborn — that's the kind that lasts.
en.editorNote: A made-for-June toast — borrow the old 'June bride is the happiest' line, then flip it to 'it's your willingness, not the month.' Works for any summer wedding speech or card
variations: Getting married in June means knowing it'll be hot, sweaty, and a little ridiculous — and choosing to walk down that aisle together anyway. That stubbornness is exactly what marriage runs on.

items.sourceNote: 伴郎致詞 self-deprecating opener，核心反轉保留、台詞全改

zh-tw.content:
大家好，我是今天的伴郎。

本來新郎叫我講三分鐘，
說一個關於他的真實故事——

結果我準備了三分鐘的優點，
跟二十分鐘的，呃，補充資料。

放心，今天是好日子，
補充資料我們留到回門再說。
zh-tw.editorNote: 伴郎開場最怕冷場，這版用「優點三分鐘、黑歷史二十分鐘」的反差製造笑點，又留一句「回門再說」當收尾梗，安全不得罪人
variations: 新郎請我今天只講他的優點，所以這篇致詞大概十五秒就會結束。開玩笑的——我們慢慢來。
我跟新郎認識十年，今天本來想全爆料，但新娘剛剛看了我一眼，所以我決定改成歌功頌德版。

en.content:
Hi everyone, I'm the best man.

The groom asked me to keep it to three minutes
and tell one true story about him.

So I prepared three minutes of his good qualities…
and about twenty minutes of, let's call it, additional context.

Don't worry — it's a happy day.
We'll save the additional context for the after-party.
en.editorNote: A safe, crowd-pleasing best man opener — the 'three minutes of good stuff, twenty minutes of dirt' gap lands the laugh without actually embarrassing anyone, and the after-party tag gives you a clean exit
variations: The groom asked me to only mention his good qualities tonight, so this speech should wrap up in about fifteen seconds. Kidding — settle in.
I've known the groom for ten years and planned to spill all of it tonight, but the bride just gave me a look, so we're going with the highlights-only edition.

items.sourceNote: 現代生活化笑點（Netflix/洗碗機），梗概念保留、句子重寫

zh-tw.content:
婚姻不是童話，
是兩個人為了「洗碗機到底該怎麼擺」吵了三年，
最後還是願意一起吃晚餐。

敬你們——
敬共用的 Netflix 帳號，
敬永遠喬不攏的冷氣溫度，
也敬那個明知道對方所有毛病、還是選他的決定。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「永浴愛河」換成洗碗機、Netflix、冷氣溫度這些真實的生活摩擦，反而比空泛的祝福更有共鳴；2026 的致詞早就不流行「嫁雞隨雞」那套了
variations: 婚姻就是兩個人共用一個 Netflix 帳號，卻永遠在搶最後一塊雞排——而且還是甘願搶一輩子。
祝你們以後吵架都吵些小事：洗碗機怎麼擺、冷氣開幾度、誰又忘了倒垃圾。因為會為小事吵的人，通常大事都站在同一邊。

en.content:
Marriage isn't a fairy tale.

It's two people arguing for three years about the correct way to load the dishwasher
and still choosing to eat dinner together.

So here's to you —
to sharing one Netflix password,
to never agreeing on the thermostat,
and to looking at every single one of each other's flaws
and picking each other anyway.
en.editorNote: Swaps the empty 'forever and always' for real friction — dishwashers, Netflix, the thermostat war — which lands warmer than a generic blessing. Modernized for 2026, no 'ball and chain' jokes
variations: Marriage is two people sharing one Netflix account and still fighting over the last slice of pizza — and happily fighting over it for the rest of their lives.

May all your future fights be small ones: how to load the dishwasher, what to set the thermostat to, whose turn it is to take out the trash. People who argue about the little stuff are usually on the same side for the big stuff.

items.sourceNote: 短誓詞 modern register，情感核心保留、措辭全改

zh-tw.content:
我不會承諾每天都是好天氣，
但我承諾——
下雨的時候，我會在你旁邊。

我答應跟你分擔買菜的袋子、
你冷掉的笑話，
還有中間所有亂七八糟卻很美的時刻。

今天我安安靜靜地對你說一聲「好」，
往後每一個早晨，我都會再說一次。
zh-tw.editorNote: 短誓詞最怕落入「無論貧窮疾病」的公式；這版用「分擔買菜袋子和冷掉的笑話」這種具體小事，把承諾講得可信。長度約三十秒，唸起來不卡
variations: 我不保證我們的每一天都順利，但我保證每一個不順利的日子，你回頭都會看到我還站在原地。
我願意跟你共享所有：購物袋、難笑的梗、還有那些說不清楚卻捨不得的瞬間。我選你，今天選，明天也選。

en.content:
I won't promise you sunshine every day.

But I promise this —
when it rains, I'll be standing right next to you.

I promise to carry the grocery bags,
to laugh at your bad jokes,
and to stay for all the messy, beautiful moments in between.

Today I say yes to you, quietly and for sure —
and every morning after, I'll say it again.
en.editorNote: Short vows fall flat when they default to 'for richer or poorer'; grounding the promise in grocery bags and bad jokes makes it land as real. About thirty seconds spoken, reads smoothly out loud
variations: I can't promise every day will go our way, but I promise that on the days it doesn't, you'll turn around and find me still standing right here.
I'll share all of it with you — the shopping bags, the terrible puns, and the moments I can't quite explain but never want to lose. I choose you. Today, and every day after.

items.sourceNote: 父母／長輩致詞 warm-funny register，概念重組改寫

zh-tw.content:
養了女兒這麼多年，
我一直以為自己在等一個「夠好」的人。

後來我發現，
我等的不是完美的人，
是一個會在她難過時，第一個放下手機看著她的人。

孩子，今天我把她交給你，
不是因為你完美，
是因為我看見你願意。
zh-tw.editorNote: 父親／母親致詞最動人的不是誇女婿多優秀，而是「願意」二字。把「條件」換成「在她難過時第一個放下手機」這種具體畫面，眼淚就出來了
variations: 我這輩子最難的決定，就是相信另一個人會像我一樣愛她。今天看著你們，我放心了。
我不是把女兒交給一個完美的人，我是交給一個願意陪她把日子過好的人。這就夠了。

en.content:
I raised this girl for years,
always thinking I was waiting for someone 'good enough.'

Then I realized
I wasn't waiting for someone perfect.

I was waiting for the one who, when she's having a hard day,
puts the phone down first and actually looks at her.

So today I'm not handing her over because you're perfect.
I'm doing it because I've seen that you're willing.
en.editorNote: The most moving parent toast isn't about how impressive the in-law is — it's the word 'willing.' Trading a checklist for one concrete image (putting the phone down first) is what brings the tears
variations: The hardest thing I've ever done is trust that someone else could love her the way I do. Watching you two today, I can finally let that worry go.
I'm not giving my daughter away to someone perfect. I'm giving her to someone willing to build a good life with her. That's more than enough.

items.sourceNote: 搞笑致詞 register，網路 quotes 重新改寫成原創段子

zh-tw.content:
想真正認識一個人，不用看他怎麼對服務生，
你只要遞給他一支只剩百分之三電量的手機，再給他一條打結的充電線。

撐得過這場考驗的，就值得娶。
所以各位，恭喜這兩位——他們已經一起撐過更可怕的東西了，比方說組裝那組從來不附說明書的衣櫃。
zh-tw.editorNote: 伴郎致詞最怕老套，這版把「考驗一個人」的哏換成現代人都有共鳴的 3C 崩潰瞬間，笑點接地氣又不會冒犯到任何人，適合開場暖場
variations: 他們交往三年，一起搬過兩次家、組過五個 IKEA 櫃子，還活著坐在這裡——這比任何誓詞都有說服力。
婚姻是兩個人的神聖約定，我說的是「神聖」，不是「神經」，雖然今天看新郎的表情我有點分不清。

en.content:
You don't really know someone by how they treat the waiter.
You hand them a phone at three percent battery and a charging cable tied in a knot.

Whoever survives that is marriage material.
So congratulations to these two — they've already made it through worse together, like assembling a wardrobe that came with zero instructions.
en.editorNote: Best-man speeches die on cliches — swapping the old 'test a person' line for a modern tech-meltdown moment lands a laugh everyone recognizes, with zero risk of offending anyone. Great as an opener
variations: Three years together, two moves, five flat-pack cabinets, and they're still here and still talking to each other — that's more convincing than any vow.
Marriage is a sacred bond between two people. I said sacred, not scared — though looking at the groom right now, I can see how you'd mix them up.

items.sourceNote: Rilke「兩個獨立的人彼此守護」意象，重新詮釋成原創致詞句

zh-tw.content:
好的愛情不是把兩個人融成一個，
而是兩個完整的人，站在彼此身邊，
替對方守著一片不必解釋的空間。

今天我看著你們，
看到的不是誰需要誰，
而是兩個人，清清楚楚地、刻意地，選擇了對方。
願你們往後的每一天，都還願意這樣選一次。
zh-tw.editorNote: 想講得有深度又不矯情，關鍵是「選擇」兩個字——把愛情從「命中注定」拉回「每天主動選」，比喊永遠更打動成熟的賓客，適合長輩或證婚人致詞
variations: 願你們做彼此最舒服的沉默，也做彼此最想分享的那則消息。
兩個獨立的人能長久，靠的不是黏在一起，而是各自站穩，再伸手把對方拉近。

en.content:
Good love isn't two people melting into one.
It's two whole people standing side by side,
each guarding a little space for the other that needs no explanation.

Watching you today,
I don't see who needs whom.
I see two people, clearly and on purpose, choosing each other.
May you keep choosing each other, every single day, for the rest of your lives.
en.editorNote: To sound deep without sounding sappy, lean on the word 'choose' — reframing love from 'destiny' to 'a daily decision' moves grown-up guests more than promises of forever. Ideal for an officiant or elder toast
variations: May you be each other's most comfortable silence, and the first person you each want to text the good news to.
Two independent people last not by clinging, but by standing firm on their own and then reaching out to pull each other close.

items.sourceNote: 現代短誓詞 register，real-couple vow 句型重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
今天我選你，
當我的隊友、我的平輩、我最想回家見到的人。

我答應：日子順的時候陪你笑，
日子難的時候不先放手；
該說話的時候開口，
不必說話的時候，安安靜靜陪你坐著就好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 誓詞不用長，一分鐘以內、一百多字最剛好。重點是具體承諾而非空泛形容——「不必說話時陪你坐著」這種畫面，比一百句「我愛你」更實在
variations: 我不保證給你完美的生活，但我保證：每一個難關，我都會站在你這邊，而不是站在問題那邊。
我答應牽著你的手過每個晚上，也答應我們不會把那點火花弄丟。

en.content:
Today I choose you —
as my teammate, my equal, the person I most want to come home to.

I promise to laugh with you when life is easy,
and to not let go first when it isn't;
to speak up when words are needed,
and to just sit quietly beside you when they aren't.
en.editorNote: Vows don't need to be long — under a minute, around 150 words, is the sweet spot. The power is in concrete promises, not vague adjectives: 'sit quietly beside you' lands harder than a hundred 'I love you's
variations: I can't promise you a perfect life, but I promise this: in every hard moment, I'll stand on your side, not on the problem's side.
I promise to hold your hand every night, and I promise we'll never let that spark slip away.

items.sourceNote: 短句敬酒祝福 register，short toast 句型改寫

zh-tw.content:
敬這對新人：
願你們的爭吵都很短，
和好都很快，
WiFi 永遠很穩，
而那份想坐在彼此對面吃飯的心情，永遠很長。

乾杯！
zh-tw.editorNote: 敬酒詞越短越好記，也越不會冷場。這版用四個「願」串成節奏，前三個輕鬆好笑、最後一個突然走心，落差感就是讓全場舉杯的關鍵
variations: 願你們的存款比吵架多，假期比加班多，而愛，永遠多到不用計算。
敬一段不用裝、不用演、累了還是想回到對方身邊的關係。乾杯！

en.content:
Here's to the newlyweds:
may your arguments be short,
your makeups be quick,
your WiFi be strong,
and may the urge to sit across the table from each other last the longest of all.

Cheers!
en.editorNote: The shorter the toast, the easier it is to remember and the less the room goes quiet. Stacking three light, funny wishes before one that suddenly turns sincere is exactly what gets everyone to lift their glass
variations: May your savings outgrow your arguments, your vacations outnumber your late nights, and your love stay too big to ever count.
Here's to a relationship with no acting, no pretending — the kind where, tired as you are, you still want to come home to each other. Cheers!

items.sourceNote: 現代交友軟體梗的誓詞，重新改寫成口語版

zh-tw.content:
如果明天我又在交友軟體上看到你的個人檔案，
我還是會毫不猶豫地往右滑。

不是因為照片，
是因為我已經知道，
滑到你，就是滑到我整個人生最對的那一次。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合愛情長跑、或是真的在 App 上認識的新人。把科技感的梗收在一句走心的告白裡，既有時代感又不流於玩笑，誓詞唸出來全場會先笑後鼻酸
variations: 認識你之前我滑過很多右，但只有你，讓我想關掉那個 App。
別人都在找對的人，我只是運氣好，先遇到了你。

en.content:
If your profile popped up again tomorrow,
I'd still swipe right without a second of hesitation.

Not because of the photos —
but because I already know
that swiping to you was the most right my whole life has ever been.
en.editorNote: Perfect for couples who met on an app, or had a long road to the altar. Wrapping a tech-era joke around a sincere confession keeps it current without turning the vow into a punchline — the room laughs first, then quietly tears up
variations: I swiped right on plenty of people before you, but you're the only one who made me want to delete the app.
Everyone's out there looking for the right person. I just got lucky and found you first.

items.sourceNote: 伴郎伴娘致詞笑話 register，重新組合改寫

zh-tw.content:
我認識新郎十年了，
說真的，從沒看過他把任何一件事堅持這麼久——
除了愛上新娘這件事。

所以今天我一點都不擔心，
因為這大概是他這輩子第一次，
做了完全正確的決定。
zh-tw.editorNote: 伴郎致詞最怕尷尬冷場。這版先吐槽再反轉，調侃和祝福各佔一半，分寸抓得剛好——既不會太刻薄，又能讓新郎和全場一起笑出來
variations: 我們從小一起長大，他做過無數蠢事，但娶到她，是他人生唯一一個我打從心底佩服的選擇。
他總是三分鐘熱度，唯獨對她，熱了整整五年還沒退燒。

en.content:
I've known the groom for ten years,
and honestly, I've never seen him stick with anything this long —
except falling for the bride.

So today I'm not worried at all,
because this is probably the first time in his life
he's made a completely right decision.
en.editorNote: The best man's nightmare is the room going silent. This one roasts first, then flips — half ribbing, half blessing, with the dosage just right, so it never tips into mean and gets the groom laughing along with everyone else
variations: We grew up together, and he's done plenty of dumb things — but marrying her is the one choice I genuinely admire.
He never sticks with anything past three minutes. With her, he's been hooked for five years and counting.

items.sourceNote: 長輩祝福語 register，重寫成有溫度的版本

zh-tw.content:
婚姻不是找到一個完美的人，
是找到一個願意陪你，
把不完美的日子，
一天一天過成家的人。

祝你們，把往後的每個平凡日子，
都過得不平凡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合父母、長輩或主婚人致詞。不講大道理，用「不完美」破題反而更真實——長輩說出口特別有重量，也最容易讓新人紅了眼眶
variations: 願你們吵架的時候記得對方的好，順利的時候記得一起走過的難。
結婚不是終點，是兩個人開始學著把日子過成彼此都喜歡的樣子。

en.content:
Marriage isn't about finding a perfect person.
It's about finding someone willing to stay,
and turn imperfect days,
one at a time, into a home.

Here's wishing you a life
where every ordinary day feels anything but.
en.editorNote: Made for a parent, an elder, or whoever's officiating. It skips the lecture and opens on 'imperfect,' which lands as honest — and coming from someone older, it carries the kind of weight that gets the couple misty-eyed
variations: May you remember each other's good in the fights, and remember how far you've come in the good times.
Getting married isn't the finish line — it's where two people start learning to build a life you both love.

items.sourceNote: 短誓詞 haiku register，改寫成口語短句

zh-tw.content:
我答應愛你、敬你，
也樂意尊重你——
但要說「順從」？
那就免談了。

我嫁的是夥伴，
不是老闆。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合不愛傳統台詞、想要一點態度的新人。短、好記、唸完全場會笑，又偷偷宣告了這段關係的平等——比一長串「我願意」更有記憶點
variations: 我答應愛你一輩子，但碗我們輪流洗。
我願意牽你的手，但我的意見我自己保留——這樣才公平。

en.content:
I promise to love you,
to honor you,
and gladly to respect you —
but obey?
No chance.

I'm marrying a partner,
not a boss.
en.editorNote: For couples who can't stand the traditional script and want a little attitude. Short, easy to remember, gets a laugh — and quietly declares this is a marriage of equals, which sticks better than a string of solemn 'I do's
variations: I promise to love you forever, but we're taking turns on the dishes.
I'll gladly hold your hand — but I'm keeping my own opinions, thanks. That's only fair.

items.sourceNote: 幽默致詞 register（吵架名言改寫），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
婚姻長久的秘訣只有一句話：

你錯的時候，馬上認錯；
你對的時候，閉嘴就好。

敬這對以後每天都用得到的智慧。
zh-tw.editorNote: 敬酒前帶這一句最安全——長輩聽了會心一笑、同輩笑到拍桌，又不會講到誰的私事。短到背得起來，喝一杯前剛好講完
variations: 幸福婚姻的公式：對的時候不邀功，錯的時候跑第一個說對不起。
吵架要記得只有兩個字能用——「你對」，剩下的回房間再說。

en.content:
The whole secret to a long marriage is one rule:

When you're wrong, admit it fast.
When you're right, zip it.

Here's to the only advice you'll both actually use.
en.editorNote: The safest joke to drop right before a toast — elders chuckle, friends howl, and nobody's private business gets aired. Short enough to nail without notes, finished before your glass is empty
variations: Formula for a happy marriage: when you're right, don't gloat; when you're wrong, be the first one to say sorry.
In every fight, you only get two words — 'you're right.' Save the rest for the bedroom.

items.sourceNote: Rita Rudner 名言 register（找一個可以煩一輩子的人），重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
人生最難的，
是找到一個你願意煩一輩子的人。

更難的是——
他居然也願意被你煩一輩子。

恭喜你們，互相中獎了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合伴郎伴娘致詞、IG限動或喜帖內頁。把「白頭偕老」翻譯成「互相煩一輩子」，俏皮又真實，比官腔祝福更像在講這對人的日常
variations: 婚姻就是找到一個你願意一直碎念、對方還甘願聽的人，恭喜你們找到了。
願你們一輩子互相嫌棄，又一輩子誰都不肯放手。

en.content:
The hardest thing in life
is finding someone you'll happily annoy forever.

The harder part?
They signed up to be annoyed right back.

Congrats — you both hit the jackpot.
en.editorNote: Great for a best man or maid of honor toast, an IG story, or the inside of a card. It swaps 'happily ever after' for 'annoy each other forever,' which lands as real and feels written for this couple, not copied off a list
variations: Marriage is finding the one person who'll nag you for life — and somehow still wants to hear it.
May you two drive each other crazy forever, and never once let go.

items.sourceNote: Mignon McLaughlin 名言 register（一再愛上同一個人），重新詮釋

zh-tw.content:
美滿的婚姻，
是一次又一次地墜入愛河——
而且每一次，
都是同一個人。

願你們往後的每一年，
都重新喜歡上彼此一遍。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合長輩致詞、紅包賀詞或喜帖。語氣溫柔不肉麻，把「白頭偕老」說成「一再愛上同一個人」，更有畫面也更耐讀——抄進紅包剛剛好
variations: 願你們吵過、累過、又笑著和好，然後在某個平常的早晨，再一次愛上對方。
婚姻不是只愛一次，是愛同一個人很多很多次。

en.content:
A good marriage is falling in love
over and over again —
and every single time,
with the same person.

May every year ahead
hand you a brand-new reason
to fall for each other again.
en.editorNote: Perfect for an elder's toast, a red envelope, or a card. Warm without being sappy — it reframes 'till death do us part' as 'falling for the same person again and again,' which paints a picture and reads well even on a small card
variations: May you fight, get tired, make up laughing — and then, on some ordinary morning, fall for each other all over again.
Marriage isn't loving once. It's loving the same person, again and again and again.

items.sourceNote: 現代俏皮 register（2026 時事梗：AI、約會軟體），原創

zh-tw.content:
在這個連點杯咖啡都靠 AI 推薦的時代，
你們居然靠自己，
滑掉幾百個人之後，
選中了對方。

演算法都沒這麼準。
敬這個全世界最對的配對。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合年輕朋友當伴郎伴娘致詞或喜宴敬酒。2026 的婚禮聽眾就吃約會軟體、AI 這類時事梗——比起老套祝福，這版講的是這代人怎麼相遇，全場會更有共鳴
variations: 別人靠演算法配對，你們靠的是直覺，而且還配得比 AI 準，恭喜。
在右滑了整個城市之後，你們停在彼此這裡——這大概就是命中註定的高科技版本。

en.content:
In an age where AI picks your coffee for you,
you two did it the hard way —
swiped past a few hundred people
and still chose each other.

No algorithm's that accurate.
Here's to the best match either of you ever made.
en.editorNote: Built for a younger crowd's toast or a round of drinks at the reception. 2026 audiences eat up dating-app and AI jokes — this beats a generic blessing because it's about how this generation actually meets, and the room feels it
variations: Everyone else lets the algorithm match them. You two went with your gut — and beat the AI. Congrats.
After swiping past an entire city, you stopped on each other. Call it fate, 2026 edition.

items.sourceNote: 現代俏皮 register（婚姻＝互傳訊息梗），重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
大家都說婚姻是一輩子的承諾。
講白一點，
婚姻其實就是接下來幾十年，
反覆傳訊息問對方
「家裡的衛生紙還有嗎」。

敬這兩位，
願你們補貨永遠補得很開心。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合伴郎伴娘或好友敬酒，把全場逗笑後再收尾——觀察式幽默最好用，現場每對結了婚的夫妻都會默默點頭
variations: 婚姻的浪漫，到後來就是兩個人為了「晚餐要吃什麼」認真討論二十分鐘，最後還是叫了一樣的外送。
願你們吵歸吵，但永遠記得對方愛吃哪一家、討厭哪一種香菜——這才是真愛的細節。

en.content:
Everyone says marriage is a lifelong promise.
Let's be honest —
marriage is mostly the next few decades
of texting each other
"are we out of toilet paper?"

Here's to you two.
May the restocking never get old.
