en.editorNote: Great for a best man, maid of honor, or close-friend toast — land the laugh, then toast. Observational humor lands because every married couple in the room is quietly nodding along
variations: The romance of marriage eventually becomes a 20-minute debate over what's for dinner, before you both order the exact same thing anyway.
May you argue when you have to, but always remember which place they love and which topping they can't stand. That's the real stuff.

items.sourceNote: 經典語錄 register（一個靈魂住在兩具身體／亞里斯多德概念），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
有人說，愛是一個靈魂
住進了兩個身體。

我以前不太懂，
直到遇見你——
才發現原來「想到你」
已經變成我的反射動作。

往後的日子，
我想繼續和你共用這一個靈魂。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合新人本人念誓詞，或寫進婚禮小卡。把抽象的古典名句翻成「想到你變成反射動作」這種具體畫面，念出來不會像在背書，反而更動人
variations: 我不需要你變得完美，我只想要每天醒來，旁邊還是你這個一點都不完美但剛剛好的人。
從今天起，我的快樂有人分，我的爛日子也有人陪著一起爛——這就是我要的婚姻。

en.content:
They say love is one soul
living in two bodies.

I never quite got it
until I met you —
and realized that thinking of you
had quietly become a reflex.

For all the years ahead,
I'd like to keep sharing this one soul with you.
en.editorNote: Written for the couple's own vows, or a card insert. It takes the classic Aristotle line and grounds it in a concrete image — 'thinking of you became a reflex' — so it sounds lived-in, not recited
variations: I don't need you to be perfect. I just want to wake up tomorrow next to the same wonderfully imperfect, exactly-right you.
From today, my good days have someone to share them, and my worst days have someone to sit in them with me. That's the marriage I want.

items.sourceNote: 經典語錄 register（婚姻＝無數細線縫合／Simone Signoret 概念），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
維繫一段婚姻的，
從來不是什麼堅不可摧的鎖鏈，
而是日復一日
一針一線縫起來的小事——

一杯記得加糖的咖啡，
一句睡前的晚安，
一次又一次選擇留下來。

願你們縫的這件，
穿一輩子都不退色。
zh-tw.editorNote: 長輩致詞或紅包賀詞都適用，溫度夠又不肉麻。把婚姻比成一針一線縫起來的衣服，比「白頭偕老」這種口號更有畫面，年輕人長輩都聽得進去
variations: 願你們的婚姻不是綁在一起，而是一天一天，自己選擇要走在一起。
祝你們把那些不起眼的小事——倒垃圾、留一盞燈、忍住不回那句氣話——都織成最暖的日子。

en.content:
What holds a marriage together
was never some unbreakable chain.
It's the small things,
stitched in one by one, day after day —

the coffee made exactly how they like it,
the goodnight said even when you're tired,
the quiet choice to stay, again and again.

May the life you're stitching
never fade, no matter how long you wear it.
en.editorNote: Works for an elder's toast or a heartfelt card. Reframing marriage as cloth sewn thread by thread paints a clearer picture than 'grow old together,' and it lands with both younger and older guests
variations: May your marriage never be about being tied together, but about choosing, one ordinary day at a time, to walk side by side.
Here's to weaving the unglamorous stuff — taking out the trash, leaving a light on, biting back the comeback — into the warmest years of your lives.

items.sourceNote: 現代俏皮 register（Dr. Seuss 互相覺得對方很怪＝愛 概念），重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
聽說，
當你覺得一個人怪怪的，
對方也剛好覺得你怪怪的，
而你們都很享受彼此的怪——

那大概，
就是愛了。

恭喜你們找到那個
跟你一樣怪的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合伴郎伴娘致詞或喜宴敬酒，輕鬆又暖。把 Dr. Seuss 那句「mutual weirdness」翻成口語，全場有伴侶的人都會偷笑著看身邊那位
variations: 願你們一輩子都覺得對方有點奇怪，但每天還是選擇跟這個怪人一起吃早餐。
全世界這麼多正常人，你們偏偏挑中彼此這個怪咖——這就是最好的緣分。

en.content:
They say
when you find someone a little weird,
and they happen to find you a little weird too,
and you both genuinely enjoy each other's weird —

that,
right there,
is love.

Congrats on finding
the person who's exactly as weird as you.
en.editorNote: Built for a best man, maid of honor, or reception toast — light and warm. It puts Dr. Seuss's 'mutual weirdness' into plain speech, and everyone in the room with a partner sneaks a glance at theirs
variations: May you find each other a little strange for the rest of your lives, and still choose to eat breakfast with that strange person every single day.
Out of a whole world of normal people, you two picked each other's brand of weird. That's the best kind of luck there is.

items.sourceNote: 伴娘致詞開場自嘲哏（loyal/caring 其實在講自己）重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
她善良、體貼、聰明，
還是我這輩子最好的朋友——

好，自我介紹到此結束，
接下來，
讓我來說說新娘。
zh-tw.editorNote: 伴娘 / 死黨致詞的神級開場，先把一串好話講得像在誇新娘、最後反轉成「其實在講我自己」。三秒破冰、全場先笑，後面講真心話就好接了
variations: 她忠誠、可靠、人見人愛——抱歉，我又在講自己了，今天的主角明明是新娘。
在座各位好，我是伴娘，也是唯一一個比新娘更早知道她會嫁給他的人。

en.content:
Kind, thoughtful, brilliant,
and the best friend a person could ask for —

okay, that's enough about me.
Let's talk about the bride.
en.editorNote: A bulletproof opener for a maid of honor or best friend toast: rattle off the praise like you're describing the bride, then reveal you were describing yourself. It breaks the ice in three seconds and earns the room before you get sincere
variations: Loyal, caring, honest, hilarious — sorry, I did it again, I'm supposed to be talking about the bride.
Hi everyone, I'm the maid of honor, and the only person here who called this wedding years before they did.

items.sourceNote: 經典 toast 哏「marriage is a walk in the park... Jurassic Park」重新創作

zh-tw.content:
有人說，
婚姻就像在公園散步。

沒錯——
是侏羅紀公園。

但我看著你們，
我一點都不擔心，
因為你們是我見過
最會一起逃命、
也最捨不得放開對方手的一對。
zh-tw.editorNote: 敬酒前帶這段，前兩句先用「公園 vs 侏羅紀公園」的反轉逗笑全場，後半立刻收回溫情。笑點和淚點各佔一半，是致詞最好操作的節奏
variations: 婚姻就像散步——在恐龍會追你的那種公園。但你們牽著手的樣子，讓我相信再大的暴龍都追不散你們。
別人說婚姻是甜蜜的冒險，我只想說：恭喜你們找到一個願意在你尖叫時也不放手的隊友。

en.content:
They say marriage is a walk in the park.

It is —
Jurassic Park.

But watching you two,
I'm not worried at all.
You're the best team I know
at running for your lives together
and never once letting go of each other's hand.
en.editorNote: Open a reception toast with this: the 'park / Jurassic Park' flip lands an instant laugh, then the back half snaps into warmth. Half punchline, half heart — the easiest rhythm to pull off at a mic
variations: Marriage is a walk in the park — the kind with dinosaurs. But the way you two hold hands, no T. rex is ever splitting you up.
People call marriage a sweet adventure. I'll just say: congrats on finding the one teammate who won't let go even when you're both screaming.

items.sourceNote: 「真心比華麗冗長更動人」的祝福原則，重新寫成短句

zh-tw.content:
我沒有什麼華麗的祝福，
只希望——

你們吵架隔天還是會一起吃早餐，
累的時候有人幫你關燈，
開心的事，
第一個想分享的永遠是對方。

這樣就很好了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 紅包賀詞或長輩致詞都能用。刻意不用四字成語、不堆排比，反而靠生活細節打動人——「華麗冗長不如真心」，這版就是把這句話本身寫成祝福
variations: 願你們的婚姻沒有什麼了不起的大事，只有一堆「還好有你」的小事。
祝你們：吵得起來，也和得回去；走得再遠，回頭都還有對方。

en.content:
I don't have a fancy blessing for you,
just this —

that after a fight, you still share breakfast the next morning,
that someone turns off the lights when you're too tired to move,
and that the first person you want to tell good news
is always each other.

That's more than enough.
en.editorNote: Works as a card message or an elder's toast. It skips the grand metaphors on purpose and leans on small daily details — proof that sincerity beats a long, flowery speech every time
variations: May your marriage hold no spectacular moments — just a thousand small ones that quietly say 'thank goodness it's you.'
Here's to a love that can argue and still find its way back, that can travel far and always turn around to find each other there.

items.sourceNote: 現代口語誓詞 register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我不保證每天都浪漫，
但我保證——

難的時候我不會先走，
錯的時候我會先道歉，
你說的話我會真的在聽，
而不是邊滑手機邊「嗯嗯」。

這就是我給你的誓言。
zh-tw.editorNote: 現代新人誓詞範本，刻意不講「永遠」「一生一世」這種大詞，改成具體可兌現的承諾。越具體越可信，台下會比聽到華麗誓詞時更感動
variations: 我發誓：吵架不翻舊帳、生氣不超過一天、永遠把最後一塊留給你。
我不會承諾完美，我只承諾——每一次想放棄的時候，我都會選擇再試一次。

en.content:
I won't promise you romance every single day,
but I do promise —

I won't be the one who walks out when it gets hard,
I'll be the one who apologizes first when I'm wrong,
and when you talk, I'll actually listen,
not just nod while scrolling my phone.

That's the vow I'm giving you.
en.editorNote: A modern vow template that skips 'forever' and 'till the end of time' for concrete, keepable promises. The more specific the vow, the more believable it is — and the room feels it more than any grand declaration
variations: I vow to never bring up old fights, to never stay angry past a day, and to always save you the last bite.
I won't promise you perfect. I'll only promise that every time I want to give up, I'll choose to try again.

items.sourceNote: 「致詞收尾要回扣前面故事再舉杯」的技巧，寫成可直接抄的舉杯結語

zh-tw.content:
我認識她這麼多年，
看過她猶豫該點哪杯飲料三十分鐘——

但選你，
她一秒都沒猶豫。

來，大家舉杯，
敬這對
終於不用再讓我當電燈泡的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 致詞最難的是收尾——這版示範「回扣前面的吐槽 + 反轉 + 舉杯」的標準收法。前面鋪『她很難下決定』，最後落在『但選你毫不猶豫』，舉杯時全場情緒剛好到頂
variations: 她做什麼都要想很久，唯獨愛你這件事，她比誰都快。來，敬這份難得的果斷。
今天之後，我終於從她的緊急聯絡人退休了。這個位子交給你，我很放心。乾杯。

en.content:
I've known her for years,
long enough to watch her take thirty minutes to pick a drink —

but choosing you?
She didn't hesitate for a second.

So everyone, raise your glasses,
to the two people
who finally let me off third-wheel duty.
en.editorNote: Closings are the hardest part of a toast — this models the standard move: call back an earlier joke, flip it, then lift your glass. You set up 'she can't decide anything,' land on 'but she chose you instantly,' and the toast peaks right on cue
variations: She overthinks everything — except loving you, which she did faster than anyone I know. Here's to that rare bit of certainty. Cheers.
As of today, I'm officially retired as her emergency contact. I'm handing the job to you, and I couldn't be happier about it. Cheers.

items.sourceNote: 幽默 register（侏羅紀公園哏 + 70/30 笑點誠意比例），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
有人說婚姻像公園散步——
沒錯，是侏羅紀公園。

會有突發狀況、會有跑給彼此追的時候，
但只要兩個人都還願意牽著手往前跑，
再大的暴龍也只是茶餘飯後的故事。

敬你們，跑得很穩的這一對。
zh-tw.editorNote: 致詞想破冰又怕太冷，這版用「侏羅紀公園」哏開場，但收尾拉回誠意——前面笑、後面暖，剛好是司儀建議的三分笑七分情比例
variations: 婚姻是場兩人三腳，跌倒不可怕，可怕的是有人想自己先衝。恭喜你們找到願意配速的人。
別人說相愛容易相處難，我看你們倆，是把難的部分笑著過完。敬這份默契。

en.content:
They say marriage is a walk in the park.
Sure — Jurassic Park.

There'll be surprises, there'll be days you're running for your lives,
but as long as you keep running side by side,
even the T-Rex becomes a story you tell at dinner.

Here's to you two — the steadiest runners I know.
en.editorNote: A safe icebreaker that lands the laugh up front, then pulls back to warmth — the classic 70/30 toast: mostly heart, just enough joke to wake the room up
variations: Marriage is a three-legged race. Falling isn't the problem — it's when one person tries to sprint ahead. You two found someone willing to match your pace. Cheers.
They say falling in love is easy and staying is hard. Watching you two, it looks like you just decided to laugh your way through the hard part.

items.sourceNote: 現代俏皮 register（交出存摺密碼哏），重新改寫成更口語的版本

zh-tw.content:
認命吧！

從今天起，
存摺密碼乖乖交出來，
前任的訊息全部刪乾淨，
你的是老婆的，
老婆的還是老婆的。

恭喜入坑，永不退坑。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合寫給熟到可以開玩笑的好朋友的紅包，俏皮但不失祝福。字數控制在紅包袋寫得下的範圍，最後一句「永不退坑」收得剛好
variations: 結婚以後請記得：吵架可以輸，但不能冷戰過夜。恭喜你們，從此有人陪你一起怕另一半生氣。
正式公告：本人帥氣/美麗的單身好友，今日下架，永久缺貨。恭喜你們！

en.content:
Time to face it.

From today on:
hand over the bank PIN,
delete every ex from your phone,
what's yours is hers,
and what's hers is also hers.

Welcome to the deep end. No refunds.
en.editorNote: A playful card message for a close friend you can tease — cheeky but still affectionate. The 'no refunds' button-up keeps it light without tipping into mean
variations: New rule for married life: you can lose the argument, but never go to bed mid-cold-war. Congrats — now you've got someone to be scared of disappointing together.
Public notice: my devastatingly single friend is now officially off the market, permanently out of stock. Congratulations to you both.

items.sourceNote: 真摯告白 register（『願你們愛得最少的一天』反轉哏），重新詮釋

zh-tw.content:
我對你們最大的祝福是——

希望多年以後回頭看今天，
會發現原來婚禮這天，
是你們這輩子
愛對方愛得最少的一天。

因為往後的每一天，
都會比昨天再多一點。
zh-tw.editorNote: 這是經典的反轉祝福句型——先讓人愣一下「最少？」再恍然「原來是說以後更愛」。適合長輩致詞或誓詞收尾，是會讓全場安靜下來的那種句子
variations: 願今天是你們笑得最少、卻也最緊張的一天，因為以後的日子只會越笑越多。
我不祝你們永遠不吵架，我祝你們每次和好，都比上一次更靠近一點。

en.content:
Here's my biggest wish for you both —

that years from now,
looking back on today,
you'll realize your wedding day
was the day you loved each other
the least.

Because every day after
will somehow hold a little more.
en.editorNote: The classic flip-blessing — it makes the room pause on 'the least?' before the warmth lands. Perfect for an elder's toast or the close of a vow; this is the line that quiets a room
variations: May today be the day you laugh the least and worry the most — because from here, the laughing only grows.
I won't wish you a marriage without arguments. I'll wish you that every making-up leaves you a little closer than the last.

items.sourceNote: 古風成語 register（四字賀詞組合），重新編排成紅包可抄版本

zh-tw.content:
永結同心，白首偕老。

願你們如比翼之鳥，
同向一處飛；
如連理之枝，
一起慢慢長。

歲歲年年，相敬如賓，相愛如初。
zh-tw.editorNote: 需要正式一點的紅包賀詞時的安全牌——四字成語開頭撐場面，中間用「比翼」「連理」鋪畫面，結尾「相愛如初」比單純堆成語多了溫度。寫給長輩或公司同事都得體
variations: 願你們永結同心，且把日子過成一首寫不完的詩，平平淡淡，卻字字是你。
比翼雙飛，連理同心，願你們往後的每個四季，都有彼此在身邊。

en.content:
Two hearts, one path. Grey hair, side by side.

May you be like birds that fly
always in the same direction,
like two branches grown together,
rising slowly as one.

Year after year — respect like guests, love like the first day.
en.editorNote: The safe, formal pick when a card needs some gravity — opens with weight, builds an image with the birds-and-branches metaphor, then warms up on 'love like the first day.' Appropriate for elders or a colleague's wedding
variations: May your hearts stay one, and may you turn your ordinary days into a poem that never quite finishes — plain, quiet, and every line about you.
Two wings, one sky; two branches, one root. May every season ahead find you with each other close by.

items.sourceNote: 伴娘致詞 register（從電燈泡到證婚人的視角），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
她跟我抱怨過所有人，
唯獨從來沒抱怨過你。

我聽過她講你講到睡著，
也看過她為你緊張到吃不下飯。

所以今天，
我這個當了十年的好朋友，
很放心地把她交給你。

請好好照顧我最珍惜的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 伴娘致詞最好用的結構——用「她從不抱怨你」當切入點最動人，因為它證明感情的深度不靠甜言蜜語。最後一句直接對新郎說，全場會跟著鼻酸
variations: 我認識她二十年，看過她最狼狽的樣子，而你，是唯一讓她願意素顏出現的人。這就夠了。
今天之後，半夜接她電話的人換成你了。這個責任很重，但我相信你扛得住。

en.content:
She's complained to me about everyone —
except you.

I've heard her talk about you until she fell asleep,
and watched her too nervous to eat because of you.

So today,
as the friend who's known her for ten years,
I hand her over without a single worry.

Please take good care of the person I treasure most.
en.editorNote: The most reliable maid-of-honor structure — 'she never complained about you' is the strongest opener, because it proves depth without a single sweet word. Turn to the groom on the last line and the whole room will well up
variations: I've known her twenty years — seen her at her absolute worst. You're the only one she'll show up to barefaced for. That tells me everything.
As of tonight, the 2am phone calls are yours to answer. It's a big job, but I know you've got it.

items.sourceNote: 伴郎自嘲式開場 register（先承認自己會講壞話），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
大家好，我是伴郎。

接下來五分鐘，
我會告訴大家新郎有多好，
其中大概有三分鐘是真的，
剩下兩分鐘，
是他昨天用三杯啤酒跟我買的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 伴郎致詞最怕一開口就尬——這版先自爆「我等下會說謊」，全場立刻放下戒心、準備聽笑話，比一本正經誇新郎安全十倍。記得真的要留三分鐘講真心話
variations: 大家好，我是伴郎，也是今晚唯一一個知道新郎所有黑歷史、但被收買到願意閉嘴的人。
我跟新郎認識十五年，今天能站在這裡，證明他這輩子至少做對了兩個選擇：娶了新娘，還有沒讓我當主持人。

en.content:
Hi, I'm the best man.

For the next five minutes,
I'm going to tell you how wonderful the groom is.
About three minutes of that will be true.

The other two
he bought from me last night
for the price of three beers.
en.editorNote: The safest way to open a best-man speech is to admit upfront that you're about to lie — the room relaxes and gets ready to laugh instead of cringing through forced praise. Just make sure you actually save three real minutes
variations: Hi, I'm the best man — also the only person here who knows all the groom's secrets and got bribed enough to keep them.
I've known the groom fifteen years, and the fact that I'm allowed to speak tonight proves he's made at least two good decisions in his life: marrying the bride, and not letting me MC.

items.sourceNote: 敬酒一句話 register（把酒舉給工作人員），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
請大家舉杯。

第一杯，敬新人——
祝你們白頭偕老。

第二杯，敬今晚的吧台和廚房，
因為沒有他們，
我根本沒勇氣講完這段話。
zh-tw.editorNote: 短到隨時能用的萬用敬酒詞——把第二杯敬給工作人員是全場最討喜的小心機，既好笑又顯得你大方，敬完直接乾杯收場，不會冷
variations: 敬新人，也敬今晚辛苦的所有人：少了你們，這場婚禮就只剩兩個人傻笑而已。
這杯敬新娘，因為她願意嫁；這杯敬新郎，因為他終於有人管了。

en.content:
Everyone, raise your glasses.

The first toast goes to the happy couple —
may you grow old side by side.

The second goes to the bar staff and the kitchen,
because without them
I'd never have found the nerve
to finish this speech.
en.editorNote: A short, drop-in toast that works anywhere — turning the second glass to the staff is the crowd-pleaser move: it lands a laugh, makes you look generous, and gives you a clean cue to drink and sit down
variations: To the newlyweds — and to everyone who made tonight happen, because without you this wedding is just two people grinning at each other.
This one's to the bride for saying yes, and this one's to the groom for finally having someone to keep him in line.

items.sourceNote: 母親給女兒 register（從寶貝女兒到放手），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我的女兒，
從今天起，你有了自己的家。

媽媽只想跟你說三件事：
別為了當好太太，弄丟了自己；
吵架沒關係，但別讓太陽下山還在生氣；
還有，無論什麼時候，
娘家的燈，都會為你留著。
zh-tw.editorNote: 母親給女兒的致詞最忌空泛——這版用三句具體叮嚀代替大道理，最後一句「娘家的燈留著」是放手又承諾的雙重溫柔，台下媽媽們都會哭
variations: 親愛的女兒，我把你交給他，不是因為你需要被照顧，而是因為你終於找到一個值得你照顧的人。
你小時候我牽你的手過馬路，現在換他牽了。我很捨不得，但我看得出來，他會牽好。

en.content:
My daughter,
from today you have a home of your own.

I only want to tell you three things:
don't lose yourself trying to be a good wife;
it's okay to argue, just never let the sun go down on it;
and no matter when,
the light at home
will always be left on for you.
en.editorNote: A mother's speech to her daughter fails when it stays vague — this version trades platitudes for three concrete pieces of advice, and the closing 'the light will be left on' is both a letting-go and a promise. Expect the other mothers in the room to cry
variations: My darling, I'm handing you to him not because you need looking after, but because you've finally found someone worth looking after.
I used to hold your hand crossing the street. He holds it now. It breaks my heart a little — but I can tell he'll hold it well.

items.sourceNote: 敬酒收尾金句 register（夠現代也夠老派），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
敬這對新人。

願你們的愛夠現代，
撐得過這個變來變去的時代；
也夠老派，
老到可以一直走到最後。
zh-tw.editorNote: 笑點講完、要轉回真心話時最好用的一句——「夠現代又夠老派」這組對比把祝福講得既不肉麻又有分量，講完直接舉杯，是教科書級的收尾
variations: 願你們的愛新潮到跟得上世界，又守舊到捨不得放手。
敬新人——願你們吵得起架，也和得了好，一路吵吵鬧鬧到白頭。

en.content:
Here's to the happy couple.

May your love be modern enough
to survive these changing times,
and old-fashioned enough
to last all the way to the end.
en.editorNote: The perfect line for stepping out of the jokes and back into sincerity — the modern-versus-old-fashioned contrast makes the blessing feel weighty without going saccharine, and it sets up a clean raise-your-glass close. A textbook ending
variations: May your love stay new enough to keep up with the world, and old enough to never want to let go.
To the couple — may you fight well, make up well, and bicker your way happily to old age.

items.sourceNote: grandparents/long-married-elder toast angle，重新改寫成祖父母視角

zh-tw.content:
阿公阿嬤結婚五十年，
我問阿嬤祕訣是什麼，
她說：「就是每天早上，
決定再喜歡他一次。」

敬新人——願你們每天都做同一個決定。
zh-tw.editorNote: 長輩或家族代表致詞最好用的一句——借「五十年」的重量開場，把抽象的天長地久收斂成「每天再決定一次」這個具體動作，比喊「白頭偕老」更有說服力，最後一句直接接舉杯
variations: 阿嬤說婚姻沒有祕訣，只有每天早上重新選一次對方——敬新人，願你們天天都選得毫不猶豫。
他們結婚五十年，靠的不是從沒吵過，是每次吵完都還想留下來。敬你們。

en.content:
My grandparents have been married fifty years.

I asked Grandma the secret.
She said, 'Every morning I just decide
to like him again.'

To the couple — may you make that same decision every single day.
en.editorNote: The most reliable toast for a grandparent or family elder — leaning on the weight of 'fifty years' up front, then shrinking forever down to one concrete daily choice, which lands harder than any 'happily ever after.' The last line hands you straight into the raise-your-glass
variations: Grandma says there's no secret to marriage, just choosing each other again every morning — to the couple, may you never hesitate.
Fifty years married, and it wasn't because they never fought. It's because after every fight they still wanted to stay. To you two.

items.sourceNote: 俏皮「交出存摺密碼」哏，重新改寫去除冒犯感

zh-tw.content:
恭喜結婚！
從今天起，
你的是你們的、你們的還是你們的，
只有洗碗是你的。

祝幸福。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合寫給熟到可以開玩笑的好朋友的紅包，俏皮但不失祝福。刻意把老哏「你的變老婆的」改成不分性別的「你們的」，最後用「洗碗」自嘲收尾，笑點乾淨又不會冒犯任何一方
variations: 恭喜結婚！以後帳戶合併、行程合併、連追劇進度都要合併，唯一保留獨立的只剩打呼。祝幸福。
從今天起你們共享一切——存款、假日、還有對方的家人。恭喜，勇敢的人。

en.content:
Congratulations!

From today, what's yours is ours,
what's ours is ours,
and the dishes are yours.

Wishing you both the best.
en.editorNote: A playful card line for a friend close enough to tease. It flips the tired 'what's yours is hers' joke into a gender-neutral 'ours,' then lands on the dishes for a clean, self-aware punchline that can't offend either side
variations: Congratulations! From now on you share everything — the bank account, the calendar, the streaming queue. The only thing still yours alone is the snoring.
From today you share it all: the savings, the weekends, and each other's families. Congrats, you brave souls.

items.sourceNote: family tree / roots 意象，改寫成結婚週年賀卡

zh-tw.content:
結婚週年快樂。

這些年你們一起種下的東西，
不只是感情，
是一整個有人願意回來的家。
zh-tw.editorNote: 寫結婚週年卡最容易流於「祝你們永遠幸福」的空話——這版把時間累積的成果具象成「一個有人願意回來的家」，溫度夠又不肉麻，父母的週年、朋友的週年都能用
variations: 結婚週年快樂。你們不只走過了這些年，還把日子過成了別人羨慕的樣子。
又一年了。謝謝你們用這麼多年，證明了『一直在一起』真的做得到。

en.content:
Happy anniversary.

What you've grown over these years
isn't just a marriage —
it's a whole home that people keep coming back to.
en.editorNote: Anniversary cards slide into empty 'wishing you forever' territory fast — this one turns the years into something you can picture, a home people return to. Warm without being sappy, and it works for a parents' anniversary or a friend's
variations: Happy anniversary. You didn't just make it through the years — you turned them into the kind of life other people envy.
Another year down. Thank you for spending this many of them proving that 'staying together' is actually possible.

items.sourceNote: friend-toast humor 觀察式笑點，重新改寫成新人本人自嘲版

zh-tw.content:
謝謝大家今天來。

我知道你們有一半是來祝福，
另一半是來確認
這種人居然也有人要。

總之——她願意，我很意外，我們很幸福。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合新人本人（尤其新郎）致詞開場，先自嘲把全場逗笑、再急轉真心話。「一半祝福一半來看熱鬧」的觀察每個賓客都懂，最後三段式短句『她願意／我意外／我們幸福』收得又快又暖
variations: 謝謝大家赴約。今天的重點只有一個：這個世界上真的有人選了我，而且到現在還沒後悔。
在座每一位都見證了我最好的決定，也順便見證了她這輩子最大的冒險。敬各位。

en.content:
Thank you all for coming.

I know half of you are here to celebrate,
and the other half are here to confirm
that someone actually agreed to marry me.

So — she said yes, I'm as shocked as you are, and we're happy.
en.editorNote: Built for the couple's own opener (the groom especially): self-deprecate to get the laugh, then pivot fast into sincerity. Everyone in the room recognizes the 'half here to gawk' bit, and the three short beats — she said yes / I'm shocked / we're happy — close it quick and warm
variations: Thanks for coming. There's really only one headline today: somebody chose me, and so far hasn't regretted it.
Every one of you is witnessing the best decision I've ever made — and, let's be honest, the biggest gamble she's ever taken. To all of you.

items.sourceNote: AI 寫講稿的現代梗，重寫成伴郎致詞自嘲版

zh-tw.content:
說實話，這篇致詞我本來想叫 AI 幫我寫。

結果它跑出一行字：
『這兩個人這麼配，這種等級的幸福，我算不出來。』

連機器都認輸了，我還能說什麼——祝你們幸福。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合伴郎／伴娘致詞開場，用「連 AI 都算不出來」把老掉牙的「天生一對」講得有新意，2026 的賓客一聽就懂又會笑，最後一句急轉真心收尾，時間長度剛好一分鐘內
variations: 我承認我請 AI 幫我寫過，但它只回我一句『這種幸福超出我的訓練資料』，所以接下來只能靠我自己了——恭喜你們。
AI 幫我列了一百種祝福，我全刪了，因為沒有一句比得上今天親眼看到你們兩個站在一起。

en.content:
Honestly, I asked AI to write this toast for me.

It gave me one line:
"These two are so well matched that this level of happiness is outside my training data."

Even the machine gave up — so all I've got left is: congratulations, you two.
en.editorNote: A modern opener for a best man or maid of honor: the 'even AI couldn't compute it' bit refreshes the tired 'perfect match' line, lands a laugh with any 2026 crowd, then pivots fast to something sincere. Comes in under a minute
variations: I'll admit I tried to outsource this to AI, but it just said 'this kind of happiness exceeds my training data,' so you're stuck with me — congrats.
AI drafted me a hundred blessings and I deleted every one, because none of them beat actually watching you two stand up here today.

items.sourceNote: 『婚姻就是互傳要不要買牛奶』觀察式笑點，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
有人說婚姻很浪漫。

其實婚姻大部分時間，
就是兩個人互相傳訊息問
『家裡還有沒有衛生紙』，
一路傳到白頭。

而我真心希望，
你們一輩子都有人可以問這句話。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合來賓或朋友致詞，用生活裡最小的一件事（傳訊息問要不要買東西）把「陪伴」講得很具體，笑點溫和不冒犯長輩，最後一句把玩笑翻成祝福，任何年齡的賓客都吃得下
variations: 婚姻不是每天都轟轟烈烈，比較像是有一個永遠會回你『家裡沒醬油了』的人，然後你們就這樣過了一輩子。
祝你們以後每一次『晚餐吃什麼』的討論，都吵得心甘情願。

en.content:
People say marriage is romantic.

Honestly, most of marriage
is two people texting each other
"do we still have toilet paper?"
all the way until you're old and gray.

And I truly hope
you both always have someone to text that to.
en.editorNote: A gentle guest-or-friend toast that makes 'lifelong companionship' concrete through the smallest thing — texting about groceries. The humor won't offend anyone's grandparents, and the last line flips the joke into a real wish that lands with every age in the room
variations: Marriage isn't fireworks every day — it's more like having one person who'll always reply 'we're out of soy sauce,' and doing that together for the rest of your lives.
Here's to a lifetime of 'what do you want for dinner' arguments that you're both genuinely glad to be having.

items.sourceNote: 現代生活化誓詞 register，重組改寫成簡短口語版

zh-tw.content:
我不承諾給你完美的人生，
因為那太假了。

我只承諾：
難走的路一起走，
難吃的飯一起吃，
難過的日子——我不會讓你一個人。

這樣可以嗎？我覺得這樣很可以。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合想寫「不要那麼制式」誓詞的新人，刻意避開「永遠幸福」的空話，用三個具體的『難』把承諾講得真實可信，最後一句自問自答帶點口語的可愛，唸出來不會尷尬
variations: 我不會答應你一輩子都是晴天，但我會答應你，下雨的時候我一定在。
我承諾的不多：好日子一起笑，爛日子我還在，這輩子你身邊那個位置，永遠是我的。

en.content:
I won't promise you a perfect life —
that would be a lie.

What I promise is this:
the hard roads, we walk together;
the bad meals, we eat together;
and the hard days — I will never let you face alone.

Is that okay? I think that's more than okay.
en.editorNote: For couples who want vows that don't sound like a greeting card: it skips the empty 'happily ever after' and grounds the promise in three concrete hard things. The self-answering last line keeps it conversational and easy to say out loud without getting choked up
variations: I can't promise you clear skies for the rest of your life, but I can promise that when it rains, I'll be right there.
I don't promise much: I'll laugh with you on the good days, I'll still be here on the bad ones, and that spot beside you is mine for good.

items.sourceNote: 『找一個永遠是對的人』建議梗，改寫成新娘致詞回敬版

zh-tw.content:
有人問我婚姻幸福的秘訣。

很簡單：
找一個永遠是對的人。

幸好——
他很早就認清了這個事實。

開玩笑的啦，我愛你，謝謝你選我。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合新娘本人致詞或回敬新郎，先用「找一個永遠是對的人」翻轉成自己，逗全場笑，再急轉溫柔收尾。新郎版可把最後兩句改成『幸好她很有耐心』，男女都能套
variations: 婚姻長久的秘訣就是：其中一個人要一直是對的，另一個人要很聰明地假裝同意。敬我先生，今天開始上工。
謝謝你願意娶一個永遠是對的人，也謝謝你這麼多年都很配合演出。

en.content:
People ask me the secret to a happy marriage.

It's simple:
find someone who's always right.

Luckily for him —
he figured that out about me pretty early.

Kidding. I love you, and thank you for choosing me.
en.editorNote: Great for the bride's own toast or a comeback to the groom: flip the classic 'marry someone who's always right' onto herself for the laugh, then pivot to something tender. For a groom's version, swap the last lines to 'luckily, she's very patient' — works either way
variations: The secret to a long marriage: one of you is always right, and the other one is smart enough to agree. To my husband — your shift starts today.
Thank you for marrying someone who's always right, and thank you for playing along so gracefully all these years.

items.sourceNote: internal only

zh-tw.content: 我不是嫁給一個完美的人，而是決定和你一起，把日子過成我們喜歡的樣子。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合新娘在證婚儀式上對另一半說的誓詞，語氣真誠不浮誇。
variations: 我娶的不是一個完美的人，而是願意陪我把生活過成我們想要的樣子的你。
我要的從來不是完美，只是想和你一起，把平凡的每一天過成專屬我們的故事。

en.content: I'm not marrying someone perfect. I'm choosing you, so we can build the kind of life we actually want, together.
en.editorNote: A grounded, heartfelt vow to read during the ceremony.
variations: I didn't fall for someone flawless. I fell for you, and the life we get to build side by side.
Perfect was never the goal. You were, and the everyday life we'll share is everything I wanted.

items.sourceNote: internal only

zh-tw.content: 願你們的婚姻像老朋友一樣自在，像初戀一樣心動，也像家一樣，永遠有燈為彼此留著。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合賓客或親友在婚宴上的簡短祝福致詞。
variations: 祝你們相處像老友般自在，愛情像初見時心動，家裡永遠有一盞燈為對方亮著。
願你們既是最懂彼此的老朋友，也是永遠會為對方心動的戀人。

en.content: May your marriage feel as easy as old friends, as thrilling as a first crush, and as warm as a home that always leaves a light on for you.
en.editorNote: A short, warm toast for guests to give at the reception.
variations: Here's to a love that's as comfortable as old friends and as exciting as the very first spark.
May you always be each other's best friend, biggest crush, and safest place to come home to.

items.sourceNote: internal only

zh-tw.content: 我們決定把餘生交給彼此，也想把這一天最好的位置，留給重要的你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合喜帖或電子邀請卡上的邀請語。
variations: 我們要開始一段兩個人的故事，誠摯邀請你來見證這重要的一天。
把最好的日子留給最重要的人，期待你來分享我們的喜悅。

en.content: We're giving the rest of our lives to each other, and we'd love to save the best seat of the day for someone as important as you.
en.editorNote: An invitation line for a wedding invite or digital save-the-date.
variations: We're starting our story, and we can't imagine writing the first page without you there.
The best day of our lives deserves the best people in it, so please, come celebrate with us.

items.sourceNote: 伴郎致詞經典梗『找到一個想煩一輩子的人』，改寫成口語版

zh-tw.content: 婚姻最浪漫的地方，就是你終於找到那個人，可以放心煩他一輩子，而他還不能跑。
zh-tw.editorNote: 伴郎伴娘致詞的破冰句，先讓全場笑一波再接感性內容，比一開口就哽咽自然多了
variations: 恭喜你們找到彼此——從今天起，吵架有對象、煮飯有幫手、耍廢有藉口。
所謂結婚，就是簽下一份『這個人我包了』的終身合約，而且雙方都不想解約。

en.content: The most romantic thing about marriage is finally finding that one person you get to annoy for the rest of your life — and they legally can't leave.
en.editorNote: A crowd-warming opener for a best man or maid of honor toast; get the laugh first, then pivot to the sincere stuff
variations: Marriage: it's just picking the one person you want to argue with about the thermostat forever.
Congrats on finding someone who'll put up with you — and signing paperwork so it's official.

items.sourceNote: 『even the cake is in tiers』雙關梗，中英各自在地化改寫

zh-tw.content: 今天大家會流的淚，一半是感動，一半是等等要付紅包的心痛。來，先乾一杯，敬新人，也敬各位的荷包。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合婚宴敬酒時的短致詞，自嘲紅包梗全場都有共鳴，笑完再舉杯剛剛好
variations: 請大家舉杯——敬新人白頭偕老，也敬今晚辛苦的廚房和調酒師。
祝福的話我準備了很多，但主持人說時間到了，所以：新人萬歲，開動！

en.content: There's not a dry eye in the house tonight — even the cake is in tiers. So raise your glasses: to the happy couple, and to the open bar that got me through this speech.
en.editorNote: A short toast built on the classic 'tiers/tears' pun, then a nod to the bar for an easy shared laugh before the clink
variations: Please, everyone, charge your glasses — to the couple, and to the bartenders keeping us all standing.
I had a beautiful speech prepared, but the open bar had other plans. To the newlyweds!

items.sourceNote: 2026 現代梗：交友軟體 / AI 寫致詞，改寫成新人告白

zh-tw.content: 當初在交友軟體上滑到你，我只想右滑一次；沒想到一右滑，就滑到了今天，還打算滑一輩子。
zh-tw.editorNote: 新人本人告白用，現代感的相遇梗比老套的『一見鍾情』更貼近年輕世代的真實故事
variations: 別人的緣分是月老牽線，我們的緣分是演算法牽的——但我很慶幸它推的是你。
我試過把我對你的愛丟給 AI 幫我寫成致詞，結果它回我：這種等級的愛，超出處理範圍。

en.content: When I swiped right on you, I figured it'd be one more date that went nowhere. Turns out that one swipe led me all the way here — and I plan on keeping you way past my screen time limit.
en.editorNote: For the couple's own vows or toast; a modern how-we-met angle that lands truer than 'love at first sight' for a younger crowd
variations: Some people get set up by fate. We got set up by an algorithm — and honestly, it's the best match it ever made.
I asked AI to write a speech about how much I love you, and it just replied: 'Error — this much love does not compute.'

items.sourceNote: 致詞收尾祝福金句 register，重新組合改寫

zh-tw.content: 願你們的爭吵都很短，擁抱都很長；願你們窮的時候一起笑，富的時候還記得當年一起笑的樣子。
zh-tw.editorNote: 致詞的收尾句，或紅包賀詞，對仗好記又不落俗套，唸出來全場會安靜一下再鼓掌
variations: 願你們往後的每一次吵架，都吵不過想牽對方手的衝動。
祝你們一輩子：飯要一起吃，架要好好吵，被子要各蓋各的。

en.content: May your arguments be short and your hugs be long; may you laugh together when you have nothing, and still remember that laugh when you have everything.
en.editorNote: A closing line for a toast or a card — balanced and easy to remember, the kind that earns a beat of silence before the applause
variations: May every fight you ever have lose to the urge to reach for each other's hand.
Here's to a lifetime of shared meals, fair fights, and separate blankets.

items.sourceNote: 2026 現代梗：冷氣／室溫之爭（thermostat wars），改寫成致詞一句話

zh-tw.content:
婚姻裡最偉大的承諾，不是「不離不棄」，
是「冷氣要開幾度，我們慢慢喬」。

祝你們一輩子都喬得攏。
zh-tw.editorNote: 伴郎伴娘或朋友致詞用，室溫之爭是每對同居情侶都懂的梗，比空泛的「永浴愛河」更有共鳴，全場已婚來賓會先笑再點頭
variations: 他們能不能白頭偕老我不知道，但看他們為了冷氣幾度吵這麼久還沒分手，我覺得很有希望。
婚姻就是兩個人一輩子在爭一件事：到底是誰把冷氣調到 26 度的。

en.content:
The greatest vow in any marriage isn't "till death do us part."
It's "we'll figure out the thermostat together."

Here's to a lifetime of meeting in the middle — around 72 degrees.
en.editorNote: For a best man / maid of honor or friend's toast; the thermostat war is a universal cohabiting-couple joke that lands harder than a generic 'lifetime of happiness' — every married guest laughs, then nods
variations: I don't know if they'll last forever, but anyone who can fight this long about the thermostat and still hold hands is doing something right.
Marriage is just two people spending a lifetime negotiating one thing: who keeps turning the AC down.

items.sourceNote: 2026 現代梗：modern enough to survive / old-fashioned enough to last，重新創作為祝福句

zh-tw.content:
願你們的愛夠新，撐得過這個時代；
也夠舊，老得像你們爺爺奶奶那一輩。

夠新，所以吵架會傳訊息先說對不起；
夠舊，所以再忙都會等對方一起吃飯。
zh-tw.editorNote: 致詞收尾或卡片祝福用，「夠新又夠舊」的對比把現代與傳統一次收進來，適合祝福任何世代的新人，唸完剛好舉杯
variations: 祝你們的感情，跟得上這個時代的快，也守得住上個時代的慢。
願你們夠現代，所以懂得各自發光；也夠念舊，所以始終想照亮同一個家。

en.content:
May your love be modern enough to survive the times,
and old-fashioned enough to outlast them.

Modern enough to text "sorry" first after a fight.
Old-fashioned enough to always wait to eat dinner together.
en.editorNote: A toast closer or card line — the 'modern yet old-fashioned' contrast works for a couple of any generation and lands right before you raise the glass
variations: Here's to a love fast enough to keep up with the world, and slow enough to remember why you built it together.
May you be modern enough to shine on your own, and old-fashioned enough to keep lighting up the same home.

items.sourceNote: 2026 現代梗：婚禮成本／群組籌備的自嘲，改寫成敬酒開場

zh-tw.content:
為了今天，他們開了三個群組、改了十七版座位表、
吵了一場關於「要不要進場撒花瓣」的架——

能把這些都撐過來還笑得出來，
這婚就結對了。乾杯。
zh-tw.editorNote: 敬酒或致詞開場用，把籌婚的真實混亂講出來，比「新人天生一對」更真、更好笑；已經歷過籌婚的來賓會會心一笑
variations: 他們連婚禮座位表都能喬到今天，我相信之後的日子沒有喬不定的事。
光是籌這場婚禮就用掉他們一整年的耐心，剩下的耐心，就留著愛對方一輩子吧。

en.content:
To pull off today, they started three group chats, rebuilt the seating chart seventeen times, and had one full-blown argument about whether to throw flower petals down the aisle.

Anyone who can survive all that and still be smiling — you two married the right person. Cheers.
en.editorNote: A toast or speech opener; naming the real chaos of wedding planning lands truer and funnier than 'they were made for each other' — every guest who's planned a wedding grins
variations: If they can agree on a seating chart, I have zero doubts they can agree on the rest of their lives.
Planning this wedding used up a full year of their patience. The rest, they're saving to love each other with.

items.sourceNote: 誓詞風 register，重新創作（日常小事版誓詞）

zh-tw.content:
我不承諾給你一輩子的浪漫，
那太大，我怕做不到。

我承諾的是：
每天早上的第一杯水、
吵完架後先開口的那個人、
還有無論多晚，都幫你留一盞燈。
zh-tw.editorNote: 新人本人誓詞用，把「一輩子」拆成做得到的日常小事，比誇張的山盟海誓更可信、更催淚；唸的時候放慢，效果最好
variations: 我給不了你完美，但我可以保證：難過的時候，我一定在；平凡的日子，我陪你過成想留住的樣子。
我不會說永遠，我會說今天——然後把每一個今天，都認真地過成永遠。

en.content:
I won't promise you a lifetime of romance — that's too big, and I'm scared I'd fall short.

What I promise is this:
the first glass of water every morning,
being the one who speaks first after a fight,
and leaving a light on for you, no matter how late.
en.editorNote: For the couple's own vows; breaking 'forever' down into small keepable everyday things reads more believable and more moving than grand declarations — read it slowly for the full effect
variations: I can't promise you perfect, but I can promise this: I'll be there on the hard days, and I'll help make the ordinary ones worth keeping.
I won't say 'forever.' I'll say 'today' — and then quietly turn every today into forever.

items.sourceNote: 現代俏皮 register（冷氣溫度大戰梗），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
婚姻不是童話。
是兩個人為了「冷氣要開幾度」，
吵到天荒地老，
然後還是決定睡同一張床。

祝你們——
冷氣二十六度，
感情永遠不降溫。
zh-tw.editorNote: 伴郎伴娘致詞破冰神句，把每對情侶都懂的「冷氣溫度大戰」當笑點，全場已婚來賓會瘋狂點頭；最後一句雙關收得剛好，不油
variations: 願你們一個怕冷、一個怕熱，卻永遠願意為對方調整那兩度。
婚姻就是同一台冷氣、兩種體感、一輩子的協商，恭喜你們找到值得談判到老的對手。

en.content:
Marriage isn't a fairy tale.
It's two people arguing over the thermostat until the end of time —
and then choosing the same bed anyway.

So here's to you:
may the AC stay at 72,
and may your love never cool down.
en.editorNote: A crowd-warming toast opener; the universal thermostat war lands with every married guest, and the closing pun keeps it clean instead of corny
variations: May one of you always be cold, one always be hot, and both always willing to meet in the middle two degrees.
Marriage is one thermostat, two body temperatures, and a lifetime of negotiation — congrats on finding someone worth arguing with forever.

items.sourceNote: 現代俏皮 register（伴娘保密協議梗），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
身為伴娘，
我同時是她的化妝師、心理諮商師、
單身時期的分手危機處理專員，
以及知道太多黑歷史的人。

別擔心，
這些祕密我會帶進墳墓——
除非紅包不夠大。
zh-tw.editorNote: 伴娘致詞開場保證笑翻——先自嘲身兼數職，最後用「除非紅包不夠大」的威脅梗反轉；把新娘的糗事點到為止、不真的爆料才安全
variations: 我知道她所有的祕密，包括她說「這是最後一杯」之後又點了三杯的每一個晚上——放心，今天我只講她多愛你。
身為認識她最久的人，我有資格作證：她挑鞋子挑三小時，挑你只花三秒。

en.content:
As maid of honor,
I'm also her makeup artist, her therapist,
her single-days breakup-crisis hotline,
and the woman who knows far too much.

Don't worry —
those secrets go with me to the grave.
Unless the open bar runs dry.
en.editorNote: A guaranteed-laugh maid-of-honor opener; the self-deprecating job list plus the mock-blackmail twist works, and gesturing at the bride's secrets without actually spilling them keeps it safe
variations: I know all her secrets, including every night she said 'this is the last one' and ordered three more — relax, today I'm only telling everyone how much she loves you.
As the person who's known her longest, I can testify: she takes three hours to pick shoes and took three seconds to pick you.

items.sourceNote: 現代幽默 register（「家裡要不要買牛奶」訊息梗），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
有人問我婚姻是什麼。

婚姻就是——
傳一輩子的「家裡還有沒有衛生紙」，
傳一輩子的「你到底要吃什麼」，
然後在某個很普通的晚上突然發現：
啊，原來這個人我可以一直傳下去。
zh-tw.editorNote: 致詞或卡片都好用，把「戀愛=浪漫、婚姻=瑣碎」的落差寫得幽默又溫暖；結尾一句反轉，讓瑣碎變成告白，笑完會有點鼻酸
variations: 婚姻就是每天問「晚餐吃什麼」，得到「都可以」，然後兩個人餓著肚子相愛一輩子。
願你們吵得完的是晚餐吃什麼，吵不完的是還想多愛對方幾年。

en.content:
Someone asked me what marriage is.

Marriage is —
texting each other 'do we need milk?' for the rest of your lives,
texting 'so what do you actually want to eat?' for the rest of your lives,
and then, on some totally ordinary night, realizing:
oh — this is the person I want to keep texting forever.
en.editorNote: Works for a toast or a card; it plays the 'dating is romance, marriage is logistics' gap for laughs, then the final line flips the mundane into a quiet love confession
variations: Marriage is asking 'what's for dinner?' every day, hearing 'whatever you want,' and staying happily hungry together forever.
May the only thing you never settle be dinner, and the one thing you always choose be each other.

items.sourceNote: 真摯卡片 register（夠現代也夠老派），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
願你們的愛，
夠現代——
撐得過這個什麼都變得很快的時代；

也夠老派——
老到還是會在對方睡著後，
幫他把被子拉好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 卡片或紅包的高質感安全牌，用「夠現代／夠老派」的對比句撐起結構，結尾一個生活化的小動作把抽象的祝福落地，寫給同事朋友都得體
variations: 願你們夠新，跟得上世界；也夠舊，記得手寫卡片、記得紀念日、記得在停電時點一根蠟燭給對方看。
祝你們的感情有 Wi-Fi 的即時，也有寫信年代的耐心。

en.content:
May your love be modern enough
to survive a world that keeps changing —

and old-fashioned enough
that you still pull the blanket back over each other
after one of you falls asleep.
en.editorNote: A high-quality safe pick for a card; the 'modern enough / old-fashioned enough' contrast gives it structure, and grounding it in one small everyday gesture keeps the blessing from floating off into abstraction
variations: May you be new enough to keep up with the world, and old enough to still write cards, remember anniversaries, and light a candle for each other when the power goes out.
May your love have the speed of a good Wi-Fi signal and the patience of the letter-writing days.

items.sourceNote: 真摯短句 register（旁觀者視角），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我看著你們，
各自變成更好的人，
然後把更好的自己，
交到對方手上。

這是我見過最美的事。
新婚快樂。
zh-tw.editorNote: 短短幾句、字數剛好塞得進卡片或紅包，適合認識新人一段時間、真的看著他們一起成長的朋友寫——旁觀者視角比空泛祝福更有重量
variations: 你們讓彼此變成更喜歡的自己，光是這件事，就值得慶祝一輩子。
願你們往後的日子，繼續是對方最想回去的那個家。

en.content:
I've watched you both
grow into better people,
and then hand those better selves
over to each other.

It's the most beautiful thing I've ever seen.
Congratulations.
en.editorNote: Short enough to fit a card or envelope, this one's for the friend who has actually watched the couple grow together — the eyewitness angle carries more weight than a generic well-wish
variations: You've made each other into people you like better — and that alone is worth celebrating for a lifetime.
May you always be the home the other one most wants to come back to.

items.sourceNote: 伴郎致詞 one-liner register，重新創作（婚前準備太久的梗）

zh-tw.content:
我本來準備了一篇超好笑的致詞，
結果 open bar 比我開始寫還早十五分鐘開。

所以接下來的內容，
請大家用喝過三杯的標準來評分。
zh-tw.editorNote: 伴郎伴娘上台第一句最難開口，用「我根本沒準備好」自嘲反而最安全——把緊張攤在檯面上，全場笑了，你也放鬆了
variations: 新人叫我今天致詞要像新郎的西裝一樣：得體、經典，最好還要短。至少有一個做得到。
他們請我當伴郎，是因為相信我；請我致詞，是因為忘了我有多會離題。

en.content:
I had a genuinely funny speech prepared for today.

Unfortunately the open bar opened fifteen minutes before I started writing it.

So please grade everything from here on a three-drinks curve.
en.editorNote: The hardest part of any toast is the first line — leaning into 'I'm not ready' disarms the room and yourself at the same time; works for a best man, maid of honor, or a nervous friend grabbing the mic
variations: The couple asked me to keep this speech like the groom's suit: sharp, classic, and short. I can manage one of those.
They picked me as best man because they trust me. They picked me to give a speech because they forgot how much I ramble.

items.sourceNote: 婚姻建議 one-liner register（找個永遠對的人），重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
新郎跟我要婚姻的秘訣，
我說：找一個永遠是對的人。

他真的做到了。
新娘，恭喜妳。
zh-tw.editorNote: 經典「幽默捧新娘」句型，短短四句就把笑點跟祝福一起交出去——敬酒前帶一句、或喜宴上炒氣氛都好用，尊重新娘又不肉麻
variations: 婚姻長久的秘訣只有兩句話：你是對的，還有——親愛的，你說了算。
他問我怎麼維持一段快樂的婚姻，我說很簡單，兩個字：認輸。

en.content:
The groom asked me for the secret to a happy marriage.

I told him: find someone who's always right.

And he did. Congratulations to the bride.
en.editorNote: A classic 'roast the groom, honor the bride' one-liner — lands the laugh and the compliment in the same breath, perfect right before a toast when you want the room warm and on the couple's side
variations: The secret to a lasting marriage comes down to two phrases: 'You're right,' and 'Yes, dear.'
He asked me how to stay happily married. I said it's easy — just learn to lose gracefully.

items.sourceNote: 現代自創誓詞 register（承諾具體小事），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我不保證永遠不吵架，
但我保證每次吵完，
都會是我先去牽你的手。

我不保證天天浪漫，
但我保證你晚上回家，
燈永遠是亮的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 誓詞最怕空講「一輩子」「永遠」——把承諾換成「我先牽手」「燈幫你留著」這種具體小事，反而更讓人相信；適合想寫自創誓詞、又不想太肉麻的新人
variations: 我不承諾給你全世界，我承諾的是：不管那天多糟，回到家你都有一個站在你這邊的人。
我發誓不會永遠對，但我發誓，錯的時候會先低頭的那個人是我。

en.content:
I can't promise we'll never fight.

But I promise I'll always be the one who reaches for your hand first.

I can't promise every day will be romantic.

But I promise the porch light will always be on when you come home.
en.editorNote: Vows die when they float off into 'forever' and 'always' — swapping big words for one small concrete act ('I'll reach first,' 'the light stays on') makes the whole thing believable; ideal for a couple writing their own vows who want sincere over sappy
variations: I won't promise you the world. I'll promise you this: no matter how bad the day was, you'll come home to someone who's on your side.
I can't swear I'll always be right — but I can swear I'll be the first one to admit when I'm wrong.

items.sourceNote: 現代俏皮賀詞 register（Wi-Fi/共享帳號比喻），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
從今天起，
你們的 Wi-Fi 密碼合併，
串流帳號共用，
吵架的權利平分，
剩下的快樂——加倍。

新婚快樂。
zh-tw.editorNote: 寫給年輕新人的紅包或 IG 賀詞，用「共用帳號」這種當代日常當比喻，比古風成語親切、又比純搞笑多一點真心，字數也剛好塞得進紅包袋
variations: 恭喜你們正式從「兩份外送」升級成「一份外送兩雙筷子」，願省下來的運費都拿去買彼此愛吃的。
願你們往後的日子：訊號滿格、電量夠用、身邊那個人永遠已讀秒回。

en.content:
As of today,
your Wi-Fi passwords merge,
your streaming logins combine,
the right to argue is split evenly,
and everything else — doubled.

Congratulations.
en.editorNote: For a young couple's card or Instagram caption — the 'shared accounts' metaphor feels current and warm, funnier than a stiff blessing but with more heart than a pure gag, and it's short enough for a red envelope
variations: Congratulations on upgrading from two separate takeout orders to one order with two sets of chopsticks — may the saved delivery fees go straight to each other's favorite snacks.
May your years ahead have full bars, enough battery, and one person who always texts back in seconds.

items.sourceNote: 現代幽默致詞 register（找到可以煩一輩子的人），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
婚姻最浪漫的地方是什麼？

就是你終於找到那一個，
可以名正言順、
心甘情願地煩一輩子的人。

來，敬這對互相看不順眼、
卻又誰都離不開誰的兩位。
zh-tw.editorNote: 伴郎伴娘致詞想開場破冰又不想太尷尬時很好用——「煩一輩子」這種反話其實藏著甜，講完全場會笑、新人會害羞，長輩也不會覺得冒犯
variations: 祝你們接下來的五十年，都有一個可以理直氣壯翻白眼、翻完又忍不住牽手的對象。
敬愛情：讓兩個原本可以各自清靜的人，決定一起吵到白頭。

en.content:
You know the most romantic thing about marriage?

It's finally finding that one person
you get to annoy, on purpose,
for the rest of your life.

So here's to the two of you —
driving each other crazy,
and somehow never wanting anyone else to do it.
en.editorNote: A great icebreaker opener for a best man or maid of honor who wants a laugh without a cringe — the 'annoy forever' line is a backhanded way of saying 'you're stuck on each other,' so it lands warm even while the room's laughing
variations: Here's to the next fifty years of you two having full permission to roll your eyes at each other — and hold hands right after.
To love: the reason two perfectly peaceful people decided to spend the rest of their lives bickering happily ever after.

items.sourceNote: 婚禮雙關梗 register（蛋糕層層/情緒），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
今天真是感人到不行，
連蛋糕都一層一層堆到掉淚了。

（英文原梗是 in tiers＝一層層，聽起來像 in tears＝流淚。）

總之——願你們的婚姻像這座蛋糕，
層層甜、撐得住，
而且永遠有人記得幫你留最後一塊。
zh-tw.editorNote: 英文婚禮致詞最經典的雙關之一，中文很難直翻，所以我用括號把梗解釋清楚、再接一句真心祝福補位；適合想在致詞裡放一個小巧思、又怕全場聽不懂的人
variations: 願你們的日子像多層蛋糕：外表撐得住場面，裡面甜得剛剛好，而且再累都捨不得漏掉彼此那一口。
敬這對新人——願你們往後的爭執都像分蛋糕，吵歸吵，最後總是一人一半。

en.content:
What an emotional day it's been —

even the cake is in tiers.

So here's my wish: may your marriage be like that cake.
Sweet all the way down,
strong enough to hold its own,
and always with someone saving you the last slice.
en.editorNote: The classic 'tiers / tears' pun is the safest laugh in the wedding-speech book — pair it with one sincere follow-up so it doesn't just land as a groaner; perfect for a speaker who wants a clean, family-friendly zinger
variations: May your life together be like a good layered cake: it holds up under pressure, it's sweeter than it looks, and neither of you ever skips your bite.
Here's to a marriage where even the arguments split like cake — messy, maybe, but always one half each.

items.sourceNote: 現代同居日常 register（冷氣溫度戰爭），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
婚姻就是兩個人，
為了冷氣要開幾度、
棉被該歸誰、
最後一顆水餃誰吃，
吵一輩子——

卻怎麼也捨不得分兩間房睡。

新婚快樂，祝你們每一場小架，
都在同一張床上和好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「同居後的真實日常」寫進祝福，比空泛的「永浴愛河」有共鳴多了；冷氣溫度、搶棉被、最後一口這些梗年輕新人一聽就笑，很適合當紅包賀詞或婚禮小卡
variations: 願你們往後為了冷氣幾度、遙控器歸誰吵到天荒地老，卻始終擠在同一張沙發上。
祝你們的爭執永遠停在「那你想吃什麼」這種等級，其餘的日子都拿去偷笑。

en.content:
Marriage is two people
arguing forever
about the thermostat,
who stole the blanket,
and who gets the last dumpling —

and still never wanting separate beds.

Congratulations. May every little fight of yours
get settled on the same pillow.
en.editorNote: Naming the real domestic stuff — thermostat wars, blanket theft, the last bite — lands harder than any 'eternal love' line; younger couples recognize themselves instantly, so it works great as a card message or a light toast
variations: May you two argue about the thermostat and the remote until the end of time, and still always end up on the same couch.
Here's to a marriage where the biggest fight stays at 'okay but what do YOU want to eat' — and the rest is just quietly grinning at each other.

items.sourceNote: 現代真摯祝福 register（累的時候仍牽手），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
願你們最累的那一天，
還是願意伸手牽對方；
最想放棄的那一刻，
還是選擇不鬆開。

愛情會退燒，
但願你們都學會，
在退燒之後，
把它慢慢煮成家。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給想寫得真摯、又不落俗套的來賓或長輩——不講「永遠幸福」，而是承認會累、會想放棄，再把祝福收在「煮成家」這個溫度上，比一味喊甜更打動人
variations: 願你們往後的日子，激情會淡、脾氣會磨，但那隻牽著的手，始終沒有換人。
祝你們把最初的心動，慢慢過成最踏實的安心。

en.content:
May you still reach for each other
on the day you're most exhausted,
and still choose not to let go
in the moment you most want to.

The spark will cool —
may you both learn
to simmer it slowly into a home.
en.editorNote: For a guest or elder who wants sincere without the cliché — instead of 'happily ever after,' it admits the tired days and the give-up moments, then lands on 'simmer it into a home,' which moves people more than pure sweetness ever does
variations: May the years dim the fireworks and wear down the tempers — and may the hand you're holding still be the same one.
Here's to slowly turning that first flutter into the steadiest kind of calm.

items.sourceNote: 伴娘/伴郎幽默致詞 register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我認識新娘二十年，
借過她的衣服、頂過她的罪、
吃過她一半的便當。

所以聽到她要結婚，
我第一個念頭是：
終於有別人要負責了。

但講真的，
我從沒看過她笑得這麼傻。
zh-tw.editorNote: 伴娘或姊妹淘致詞的萬用開場——先自嘲二十年的相欠債逗全場笑，最後一句突然轉真摯，笑點和淚點都收得剛剛好，長輩晚輩都不會尷尬
variations: 我當她的姊妹二十幾年，欠的人情算不清，今天總算能把她交給另一個願意還的人。
認識她這麼久，看過她各種崩潰，但今天這種笑法，我是第一次看到。

en.content:
I've known the bride for twenty years —
borrowed her clothes, took the blame for her,
finished half her lunches.

So when she said she was getting married,
my first thought was:
finally, someone else's problem.

But honestly?
I've never seen her smile like this.
en.editorNote: A go-to opener for a maid of honor or best friend — the twenty-years-of-mooching self-roast lands the laugh, then the last line turns sincere, so it works for grandparents and college friends alike
variations: I've been her partner in crime for two decades, and today I finally get to hand her off to someone willing to pay off the debt.
I've seen every version of her meltdown — but this smile? First time for that one.

items.sourceNote: 經典名言 register（Mignon McLaughlin 概念），重新改寫非直引

zh-tw.content:
好的婚姻，
不是愛上一次就結束，
而是同一個人，
你願意一次又一次重新愛上。

吵過架的隔天、
累到不想說話的夜裡，
還是選擇他。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合放進誓詞或給新人的卡片——把「一輩子只愛一次」翻成「同一個人重新愛很多次」，更真實也更浪漫，比空喊永遠更有重量
variations: 婚姻不是找到對的人就結束，是每天早上醒來，還願意把他重新選一次。
願你們往後每次快撐不下去時，都還記得當初為什麼選了彼此。

en.content:
A good marriage isn't
falling in love once and calling it done.
It's falling for the same person
over and over —

the morning after a fight,
the night you're too tired to talk,
and still choosing them anyway.
en.editorNote: Great for a vow or a card to the couple — it reframes 'love of your life' as choosing the same person again and again, which reads truer and more romantic than promising forever
variations: Marriage isn't finding the right person once. It's waking up and picking them again every single day.
May you both remember, on the hardest days, exactly why you chose each other in the first place.

items.sourceNote: 現代溫馨柴米油鹽 register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
願你們在柴米油鹽裡，
也能煮出自己的浪漫；
把最普通的一天，
過成只有你們懂的甜。

新婚快樂。
zh-tw.editorNote: 紅包或卡片的短賀詞——不寫成語堆砌，用「柴米油鹽」對比「浪漫」，把祝福落在日常裡，收尾乾淨，字數也剛好塞得進紅包袋
variations: 願你們把每個平凡的晚餐，都過成想坐在對面的理由。
祝你們日子不用天天驚喜，但天天都想回同一個家。

en.content:
May you find your own kind of romance
in the grocery runs and the dishes,
and turn the most ordinary day
into something only the two of you understand.

Happy wedding.
en.editorNote: A short blessing for a card or envelope — instead of stacking clichés, it plants the romance inside everyday chores, so it feels lived-in and lands clean with room to spare
variations: May every plain dinner become a reason to want to sit across from each other.
Here's to days that aren't full of surprises, but always end at the same home.

items.sourceNote: 短敬酒詞 register（30-45 秒），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
話我就長話短說：
找到一個能一起大笑、
也能一起沉默的人，
很難。

你們找到了。

請大家舉杯——
敬他們的笑聲，
也敬那些不用說話的時刻。
zh-tw.editorNote: 來賓敬酒的萬用短講，唸出來大約三十秒——不搶新人風頭，一句「能一起大笑也能一起沉默」就把祝福講完，最後乾淨收在舉杯，緊張到忘詞也不怕
variations: 敬一對能吵完架還一起吃宵夜的人，這種默契比什麼誓言都可靠。
長話短說：我沒看過比今天更登對的兩個人，來，舉杯。

en.content:
I'll keep it short:
finding someone you can laugh with
and sit in silence with
is rare.

The two of you found it.

So raise your glasses —
to their laughter,
and to the quiet moments
that don't need words.
en.editorNote: An all-purpose guest toast that reads in about thirty seconds — it doesn't upstage the couple, sums the whole blessing into 'laugh together and be quiet together,' and lands cleanly on the glass-raise even if your nerves eat the rest
variations: Here's to two people who can finish a fight and still go grab a midnight snack — that beats any vow.
Long story short: I've never seen a better-matched pair. Now raise your glasses.

items.sourceNote: 幽默一句話敬酒 register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
婚姻真正的意義，
就是這輩子終於找到一個人，
可以名正言順地煩到老。

來，敬這對互相看不順眼卻誰也離不開的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 來賓臨時被叫上台時的救命短句——一句吐槽把祝福講完，全場都笑得出來，長輩到大學同學都接得住，唸完直接舉杯不用鋪陳
variations: 祝你們吵架永遠吵不過三分鐘，跟我這段致詞一樣短。
找到願意讓你煩一輩子的人不容易，你們找到了，敬你們。

en.content:
The whole point of marriage
is finally finding the one person
you get to annoy for the rest of your life —
with full permission.

So here's to two people who can't stand each other
and can't live without each other either.
en.editorNote: A lifesaver when you're pulled up to speak with zero prep — one gentle roast carries the whole blessing, everyone from grandma to the college crew can laugh, and you land straight on the toast with no runway needed
variations: May your arguments never last longer than three minutes — about as long as this speech.
Finding someone willing to be annoyed by you forever isn't easy. You found yours. Cheers.

items.sourceNote: 現代生活感誓詞 register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我發誓，冷氣要開幾度我們可以慢慢喬，
最後一塊鹹酥雞我讓你，
手機滑到半夜我也叫你睡。

那些小事我都願意讓，
因為剩下的一輩子，
我只想跟你一起過。
zh-tw.editorNote: 想寫誓詞又不想太肉麻的新人適用——用冷氣溫度、最後一塊消夜這種真實鬥嘴當開場，笑完再收一句真心話，比整段華麗排比更打中對方
variations: 我發誓遙控器可以給你，但週末睡到中午的權利我們一人一半。
那些為小事拌嘴的日子，我都想跟你一路吵到白頭。

en.content:
I promise we'll negotiate the thermostat like adults,
you can have the last dumpling,
and yes, I'll still nag you to put the phone down at midnight.

I'll give in on all the small stuff,
because the rest of my life
is the one thing I only want to spend with you.
en.editorNote: For couples who want a vow that's warm without being sappy — it opens on the real skirmishes (thermostat, last bite, doomscrolling) then lands one sincere line, which hits harder than a paragraph of poetry
variations: I promise you the remote, but weekend sleep-ins we split fifty-fifty.
All those days we bicker over nothing — those are the ones I want to keep, right to the end.

items.sourceNote: 莊重感人誓詞 register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我不敢保證每一天都是晴天，
但我保證，下雨的時候，
我一定站在你這邊撐傘。

順著也好，逆著也罷，
往後的路，我都想牽著你走。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「無論順境逆境」那種法定誓詞的骨架，換成撐傘的畫面來講——莊重但不生硬，唸出來不會像在背合約，適合正式證婚環節
variations: 我給不了你一輩子的好天氣，但我可以做你每一場雨裡的屋簷。
從今天起，你的難關不再是一個人扛，我把肩膀先借給你。

en.content:
I can't promise you clear skies every day,
but I can promise that when it rains,
I'll be the one standing on your side of the umbrella.

Good times or hard ones,
every road from here, I want to walk holding your hand.
en.editorNote: Takes the bones of the classic 'for better or for worse' vow and trades the legal cadence for one plain image — solemn without sounding like you're reciting a contract, ideal for the formal ceremony moment
variations: I can't give you a lifetime of good weather, but I'll be the roof over every storm.
From today, you never carry the hard days alone — my shoulder is yours first.

items.sourceNote: 現代俏皮紅包賀詞 register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
祝你們：
吵架有輸有贏，
消夜有你有我，
存款穩穩上升，
甜度天天爆表。

新婚快樂，早生一個小隊友。
zh-tw.editorNote: 紅包袋寫得下的四句吉祥話新版——不用「百年好合」老套，改成吵架、消夜、存款這種年輕人有感的日常，最後一句幽默收尾，長輩看了也會笑
variations: 祝你們家事輪流做、旅遊天天約、紅包越包越大包。
願你們婚後還是最想傳訊息的那個人，甜到閃瞎親友團。

en.content:
Wishing you both:
arguments that end in a draw,
midnight snacks always for two,
a savings account that only climbs,
and sweetness that maxes out daily.

Happy wedding — may a tiny teammate arrive right on cue.
en.editorNote: A fresh four-line blessing that fits inside a card or red envelope — skips the dusty clichés for things young couples actually feel (fights, late-night snacks, savings), then closes on a wink even the relatives will grin at
variations: May you split the chores, book every trip together, and see those envelopes only get fatter.
May you stay each other's first text after the wedding, sweet enough to blind the whole guest list.

items.sourceNote: 伴郎伴娘致詞 register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
認識他這麼多年，
我看過他為很多事情緊張，
但今天他站在那裡，
第一次那麼篤定。

我終於懂了——
原來對的人，
是會讓你連害怕都變得踏實。

敬新郎新娘。
zh-tw.editorNote: 伴郎伴娘致詞的萬用結構：先用「我認識他很久」建立可信度，再點出今天的不一樣，最後一句金句收在舉杯——不用爆料糗事也很動人，緊張的人照著講就好
variations: 我看過他失敗、看過他逞強，今天是我第一次看他這麼安心地笑。
謝謝你，讓我最好的朋友變成了最好的自己，敬你們。

en.content:
I've known him for years,
and I've watched him get nervous over plenty of things.
But today, standing up there,
he looked certain for the first time.

And it finally clicked for me —
the right person
is the one who makes even your fear feel steady.

To the bride and groom.
en.editorNote: The all-purpose best-man / maid-of-honor structure: open with 'I've known them forever' to earn trust, mark what's different about today, then land a keeper line on the toast — moving without dishing any embarrassing stories, so nervous speakers can just read it straight
variations: I've seen him fail and I've seen him fake being fine — today's the first time I've seen him this at peace.
Thank you for turning my best friend into the best version of himself. To you both.

items.sourceNote: 幽默致詞收尾梗（撲克牌雙關），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
有人說婚姻就像打牌，
只要手上有兩顆真心、一顆鑽石，
這一局就穩了。

看看新娘手上那顆——
新郎這張牌，出得漂亮。

乾杯！
zh-tw.editorNote: 敬酒前想來一句讓全場笑出來、又不會太尷尬的收尾，這種撲克牌雙關最安全——長輩聽得懂、年輕人覺得可愛，最後一句「乾杯」剛好帶大家舉杯
variations: 婚姻是一場沒有攻略的牌局，但只要願意一直跟對方同一隊，怎麼打都不算輸。
他這輩子最會做的一件事，就是今天娶到了坐在他旁邊的這位——這把牌他贏定了。

en.content:
They say marriage is like a card game —
all you really need is two hearts and a diamond.

Take a look at the ring on her finger.
I'd say the groom played his hand perfectly.

Cheers!
en.editorNote: The safest way to land a laugh right before the toast — a card-game pun the grandparents get and the cousins think is cute, with a built-in 'cheers' that cues everyone to raise their glass
variations: Marriage is the one card game nobody hands you the rules for — but stay on the same team long enough and you never really lose.
The smartest move he'll ever make was made today, sitting right next to her. He's holding a winning hand.

items.sourceNote: 俏皮誓詞 register（日常小承諾），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我答應你，
熱的時候幫你開冷氣，就算我冷到發抖；
你講的冷笑話，我還是會笑；
吵架的隔天，我一定先去買你愛吃的早餐。

我不是因為日子好過才選你，
是因為有你，日子才變得好過。
zh-tw.editorNote: 想寫自己的誓詞又怕太肉麻，可以用這種「具體小承諾＋一句真心」的結構——先講幾件生活裡真的會做的小事逗笑對方，最後一句認真收尾，比空泛的「愛你一輩子」更打動人
variations: 我答應把購物袋都提在我這邊、把最後一口甜點留給你、把每一個亂七八糟的日子都跟你一起過完。
我不保證永遠不吵架，但我保證每次吵完，都還是想牽你的手。

en.content:
I promise to turn on the AC when you're hot, even when I'm the one shivering.
I promise to laugh at your terrible jokes.
And the morning after a fight, I promise to be the first one out the door for your favorite breakfast.

I'm not choosing you because life is easy.
I'm choosing you because you're the reason it feels easy at all.
en.editorNote: If you want to write your own vows but dread getting too mushy, use this 'small specific promises + one honest line' shape — a few real everyday things to make your partner grin, then a sincere close that beats any generic 'forever and always'
variations: I promise to carry the heavier grocery bags, save you the last bite of dessert, and choose you all over again on the messy days.
I can't promise we'll never fight — only that I'll still want to hold your hand the second we're done.

items.sourceNote: 現代紅包賀詞 register，重新組合改寫

zh-tw.content:
願你們，
有事一起扛，有肉一起吃，
存款一起變多，皺紋一起變深。

吵得起來，也哄得回去，
這就是最厲害的幸福。
zh-tw.editorNote: 趕場婚禮、紅包袋只剩一點空白時最好用——跳過「白頭偕老」的官腔，寫幾件年輕夫妻真的在意的事（存款、拌嘴、和好），短短幾行就有誠意又不俗套
variations: 願你們吵架不過夜，旅行不缺席，紅包只會越包越大包。
祝你們一起變老、一起變好，日子越過越有默契。

en.content:
May you carry the hard stuff together and split the good stuff too —
watch your savings grow and your wrinkles deepen side by side.

May you always be able to argue,
and always find your way back.
That's the real happily ever after.
en.editorNote: Perfect when you're rushing between weddings and only have a sliver of card left — skip the dusty 'grow old together' line for things young couples actually care about (savings, bickering, making up), sincere in just a few lines
variations: May you never go to bed angry, never skip a trip together, and only ever see those envelopes get thicker.
May you grow older and better at the same time, more in sync with every passing year.

items.sourceNote: 電影台詞風致詞 register，重新創作（非引用原句）

zh-tw.content:
電影裡總說，
對的人會在對的時間出現。

但我覺得不是這樣——
是因為你們願意留下來、願意調整、願意每天重新選一次彼此，
才把那個「對的時間」，親手活成了真的。

敬你們一起寫下去的故事。
zh-tw.editorNote: 想讓致詞有電影感又不想直接引用別人的台詞時可以用——先立一個大家熟悉的浪漫說法，再溫柔推翻它、點出真正的愛是選擇不是命運，最後一句敬酒收得漂亮
variations: 愛情不是剛好遇到對的人，而是遇到之後，你們把彼此變成了對的人。
最好的愛情故事沒有劇本，是你們一天一天，一起寫出來的。

en.content:
The movies always tell us
the right person shows up at the right time.

But I don't think that's it.
It's that you two kept staying, kept adjusting, kept choosing each other every single day —
and that's how you turned 'the right time' into something real.

Here's to the story you keep writing together.
en.editorNote: For when you want a cinematic toast without lifting an actual movie line — set up a romantic idea everyone knows, gently flip it to say real love is a choice not fate, then land cleanly on the toast
variations: Love isn't stumbling onto the right person — it's turning each other into the right person after you do.
The best love stories don't come with a script. You've been writing yours one ordinary day at a time.

items.sourceNote: 溫馨祝福金句 register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
婚姻不是找到一個從此不用磨合的人，
是找到一個願意陪你一直磨、一直合的人。

願你們往後的每一天，
都比昨天更懂得對方一點點。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合長輩或證婚人致詞、也能寫進卡片——不談風花雪月，講的是「婚姻是持續的功課」這種過來人才懂的溫柔，成熟穩重又不說教
variations: 願你們把「習慣」過成「珍惜」，把「日子」過成「彼此」。
祝你們吵得少、笑得多，把柴米油鹽都過成幸福。

en.content:
Marriage isn't finding someone you never have to work things out with.
It's finding someone willing to keep working them out, right beside you.

May every day ahead
leave you understanding each other just a little more than the day before.
en.editorNote: Great for a parent's or officiant's remarks, and just as good in a card — no fairy-tale fluff, just the seasoned truth that marriage is ongoing work, warm and grounded without ever preaching
variations: May your habits turn into cherishing, and your ordinary days turn into each other.
Here's to fewer fights, more laughter, and turning the daily grind into something that feels like home.

items.sourceNote: 伴娘自嘲式幽默 register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
大家好，我是新娘的伴娘，
也是她的免費心理諮商師、包色顧問、
以及知道太多秘密的那個人。

別擔心，我簽過保密協議了——
所以今天我只講可以公開的部分。
zh-tw.editorNote: 伴娘致詞的萬用開場，先自嘲把全場笑點打開再進正題——「知道太多秘密」這種懸念比直接誇新娘更抓耳，長輩場記得把後面故事挑乾淨的講
variations: 身為伴娘，我的職稱包含：軍師、司機、備用衛生棉供應商，以及今晚負責在麥克風前不哭場的人。
新娘叫我「發自內心地說」，我的內心說：先讓我再喝一杯再開始。

en.content:
Hi everyone, I'm the maid of honor —
which also makes me the bride's unpaid therapist,
styling consultant, and the one who knows way too much.

Don't worry, I signed an NDA.
So tonight I'll only be sharing the parts that are cleared for release.
en.editorNote: A go-to maid of honor opener — the self-deprecating job title cracks the room open before you get sincere, and 'knows way too much' buys you a laugh you can cash in later; keep the follow-up story PG for a mixed crowd
variations: As maid of honor, my duties included: strategist, chauffeur, emergency snack supplier, and the person in charge of not crying into this microphone tonight.
The bride told me to speak from the heart. My heart says I need one more glass of champagne first.

items.sourceNote: 敬酒收尾一句話 register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我要敬新人一杯——
倒不完全是因為他們值得，
主要是我這杯已經舉了快五分鐘，手很痠。

祝你們的愛像今晚的酒，
越陳越香，而且千萬別喝完。
乾杯！
zh-tw.editorNote: 致詞快講完、想找一句漂亮又好笑的收尾時用——先自嘲「手痠」打破莊嚴感，再用酒做比喻收回真心，敬酒詞最怕拖，這版短又有記憶點
variations: 我要舉杯敬這對新人，順便提醒大家：吧台三十秒後重新開放，所以這杯請一口乾。
願你們的杯永遠是滿的，愛永遠是深的——好了我先乾為敬，因為我真的渴了。

en.content:
I'd like to raise a glass to the newlyweds —
not entirely because they deserve it,
but mostly because I've been holding this thing for five minutes and my arm hurts.

May your love be like tonight's wine:
better with age, and please, never run dry.
Cheers!
en.editorNote: For when the speech is nearly done and you want a landing that's pretty and funny — the 'my arm hurts' bit punctures the formality, then the wine metaphor pulls the sincerity back; toasts die when they drag, so keep it this tight
variations: I'd like to propose a toast to the happy couple — and also to remind everyone the bar reopens in thirty seconds, so drink up.
May your glasses stay full and your love stay deep. I'll go first, because honestly, I'm thirsty.

items.sourceNote: 現代生活現實梗 register（恆溫器/婚禮花費），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
婚姻是一場漫長的協商：
冷氣要開幾度、假日去誰家、
最後一塊雞排到底歸誰。

祝你們往後每一場談判，
都以「算了，我讓你」收場，
然後兩個人一起偷笑。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合平輩朋友致詞或喜帖裡的俏皮祝福——挑生活裡最真實的小爭執當梗，比空泛的「永浴愛河」更有共鳴，收尾溫柔不落俗套
variations: 婚姻九成是柴米油鹽，一成是恆溫器該開幾度的世紀談判，祝你們每一場都愉快地各退一步。
願你們搶最後一塊鹹酥雞時，永遠捨得讓對方先夾。

en.content:
Marriage is one long negotiation:
what to set the thermostat to, whose family for the holidays,
and who gets the last slice of pizza.

May every one of those negotiations
end with 'fine, you take it' —
and both of you quietly smiling about it.
en.editorNote: Great for a friend's toast or a cheeky line in a card — pulling the joke from real domestic squabbles lands harder than 'a lifetime of bliss,' and the ending stays warm instead of sappy
variations: Ninety percent of marriage is chores and logistics; the other ten is the great thermostat debate. May you always meet in the middle, cheerfully.
May you always be willing to let the other one have the last slice.

items.sourceNote: 現代連線遊戲比喻 register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
結婚就像從單人模式，
切換成一輩子的雙人連線。

以後有難一起扛、有寶一起分，
偶爾也會為了誰該去洗碗互丟表情包——
但願你們永遠選擇留在同一隊，
一路合作，無往不利。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合年輕朋友的紅包賀詞或IG祝福，字數剛好塞得進紅包袋——用「雙人連線」比喻婚姻，比制式祝賀多一分親切，最後兩句對仗收得穩
variations: 願你們這局雙人連線一玩就是一輩子，不下線、不擺爛、不互刪好友。
祝你們往後每一關都同隊，補血補到底，絕不半路棄坑。

en.content:
Marriage is switching from single-player
to a lifelong two-person co-op.

From here on you carry the hard parts together, split the loot,
and occasionally trade memes over whose turn it is to do the dishes —
but may you always choose to stay on the same team,
playing it out to the end, side by side.
en.editorNote: A good red-envelope or Instagram blessing for a younger crowd, short enough to fit on a card — the co-op metaphor feels more personal than a stock congratulations, and the closing lines land clean
variations: May this co-op run last a lifetime — never logging off, never rage-quitting, never unfriending each other.
Here's to staying on the same team through every level, reviving each other, never abandoning the quest.

items.sourceNote: 伴郎伴娘致詞開場 one-liner register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
各位好，我很榮幸能主持——
這是今天唯一一個新娘沒有事先排練過的環節。

流程表她抓了三個月，
座位表她改了十七版，
只有我要說什麼，她管不著。

所以趁現在，我要說：
她值得今天的每一分幸福。
zh-tw.editorNote: 伴郎伴娘致詞最怕開頭乾——先自嘲一句「唯一沒被新娘排練過的環節」，全場先笑一次，情緒鬆開了，最後那句真心話才收得進去
variations: 我準備了三分鐘的致詞，新娘準備了三個月的婚禮，等一下要是我講超時，請大家看在她今天很漂亮的份上原諒我。
今天現場每個細節她都掌控過，除了我這張嘴——所以我要趁機說一句她攔不住的：恭喜你，嫁給了一個很好的人。

en.content:
Hi everyone — I'm honored to be running
the one part of today the bride didn't get to rehearse.

She planned the timeline for three months.
She revised the seating chart seventeen times.
The only thing she couldn't control was what I'd say.

So while I've got the chance:
she deserves every bit of joy today is giving her.
en.editorNote: The scariest part of a best man / maid of honor speech is a flat opener. Self-deprecate about being 'the one thing the bride couldn't rehearse,' get the first laugh, and the room relaxes enough to actually feel the sincere line at the end
variations: I prepared three minutes of remarks; the bride prepared three months of wedding. If I run long, please forgive me — look how good she looks tonight.
She controlled every detail of today except this microphone, so let me use it for the one thing she can't stop: congratulations on marrying someone genuinely good.

items.sourceNote: 「友情燃成愛情」經典比喻 register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
最好的愛情，
是一段先變成朋友、
再慢慢燒起來的感情。

你們不是先愛上、再認識，
而是先認識、才捨不得。

敬我最好的朋友，
和她這輩子最好的朋友。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合伴娘或多年好友致詞的收尾——把「友情」當作愛情的地基，比「一見鍾情」更耐讀；最後一句雙關「最好的朋友」既敬新娘也敬新郎，敬酒時舉杯剛好
variations: 愛情最扎實的一種，是先當朋友、再動了心——恭喜你們把最熟的人，變成最愛的人。
敬一段慢慢燒起來的感情，願它燒得久、燒得暖，燒到你們都白頭。

en.content:
The best kind of love
is a friendship that slowly catches fire.

You two didn't fall first and figure it out later —
you knew each other first,
and then couldn't let go.

So here's to my best friend,
and to hers.
en.editorNote: A warm closer for a maid of honor or an old friend — framing friendship as the foundation reads truer than 'love at first sight,' and the final 'my best friend / hers' pun toasts both of them at once. Raise your glass on that last line
variations: The sturdiest love is the kind that starts as friendship and quietly ignites. Congratulations on turning your favorite person into the one you love most.
Here's to a love that caught fire slowly — may it burn long, burn warm, and keep you both glowing well into gray hair.

items.sourceNote: 「經得起時代、又老派得能長久」經典祝福 register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
願你們的愛，
夠新，撐得過這個時代；
夠舊，值得過一輩子。

新到能一起追新的劇、換新的手機、
吵新型態的架；
舊到還是牽手過馬路、
還是把最後一口留給對方。
zh-tw.editorNote: 紅包或喜帖賀詞用得上——「夠新／夠舊」的對照把祝福寫得有層次，比「白頭偕老」多一點現代感；字數控制得剛好塞得進紅包袋
variations: 願你們的感情，夠新，跟得上世界；夠舊，守得住彼此。
祝你們愛得夠現代——一起成長不脫節；也愛得夠老派——牽了手就不放。

en.content:
May your love be new enough
to survive the times,
and old-fashioned enough to last a lifetime.

New enough to binge new shows, upgrade new phones,
and argue about brand-new problems;
old-fashioned enough to still hold hands crossing the street,
and still save each other the last bite.
en.editorNote: Works for a card or a toast — the 'new enough / old-fashioned enough' contrast gives a blessing some texture and reads more modern than 'grow old together.' Short enough to fit inside a card, too
variations: May your love be new enough to keep up with the world, and old-fashioned enough to keep hold of each other.
Here's to loving modern enough to grow together, and old-fashioned enough to never let go of a hand once you've taken it.

items.sourceNote: 誓詞風 register（日常小事承諾），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我不會說要給你全世界，
那太大了，我背不動。

我能給你的是：
每天早上第一杯溫水，
你難過時不急著給建議的耳朵，
還有無論多晚，
都會留一盞燈等你回家。

世界太遠，我先把家給你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 誓詞越具體越動人——避開「給你全世界」這種空頭支票，改用「一杯溫水、一盞燈」的日常細節，比華麗辭藻更容易讓對方紅了眼眶
variations: 我給不起星辰大海，但我給得起：記得你所有的過敏原、你所有的難過，和你回家時永遠亮著的那盞燈。
承諾不必偉大——我只答應你,把每一個平凡的日子，都認真地陪你過完。

en.content:
I won't promise you the whole world —
it's too big, I can't carry it.

What I can give you is this:
the first warm cup of water every morning,
an ear that won't rush to fix your bad days,
and no matter how late it gets,
a light left on so you can find your way home.

The world is far. Let me give you a home first.
en.editorNote: The more specific a vow, the more it lands. Skip the blank check of 'the whole world' and trade it for a warm cup and a porch light — small, concrete details bring tears faster than grand words ever will
variations: I can't give you the stars and the sea, but I can give you this: remembering all your allergies, all your sad days, and a light always left on when you come home.
A promise doesn't have to be grand — I only vow to spend every ordinary day right there beside you, paying full attention.

items.sourceNote: 伴娘致詞（把新郎併入好友圈）register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
當她最好的朋友二十年，
我以為這個位子沒人搶得走。

結果今天，
我要正式把「第一順位」交出去了。

沒關係——
交給一個會把她照顧得比我更好的人，
我心甘情願退居第二。
祝你們幸福。
zh-tw.editorNote: 伴娘致詞的黃金結構：先假裝吃醋新郎搶了好姊妹，再話鋒一轉真心祝福——「退居第二」這種自嘲能讓全場笑著紅眼眶，敬酒收尾剛剛好
variations: 當了她二十年的頭號好友，今天我很樂意把這個頭銜讓給一個更值得的人——但備位隨時待命，需要我隨傳隨到。
我以為沒人能取代我在她心裡的位置，直到我看見她看你的眼神——好吧，這局我認輸，而且輸得很開心。

en.content:
I've been her best friend for twenty years.
I thought nobody could take this spot.

And yet, today,
I'm officially handing over the number-one slot.

And that's okay —
I'm handing it to someone who'll take even better care of her than I did,
so I'll happily settle for second place.
Here's to the two of you.
en.editorNote: The classic maid of honor arc: pretend to be jealous the groom stole your best friend, then pivot to genuine blessing. 'Settling for second place' is the kind of self-deprecation that makes a room laugh with wet eyes — perfect for the toast at the end
variations: Twenty years as her number-one, and today I'm gladly passing the title to someone more deserving — but I'm on standby, on call whenever she needs me.
I thought no one could replace me in her heart, until I saw the way she looks at you. Fine — I lose this round, and I couldn't be happier about it.

items.sourceNote: 伴郎自嘲式開場（被抓來致詞）register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
各位好，我是今天的伴郎。
之所以由我來致詞，
是因為問過一輪之後，
就我沒有理由拒絕。

但站上來我才發現——
能被新郎信任到託付這種丟臉風險的人，
大概也只有真朋友了。
來，敬他們。
zh-tw.editorNote: 伴郎致詞最怕一開口就緊張冷場，這種「我是被抓來的」自嘲開場能瞬間卸下全場戒心，笑完再自然收成一句真心敬酒
variations: 我不是最會講話的那個，我只是最推不掉的那個——但能站在這裡幫你們敬這杯，我一點都不後悔。
新郎找我致詞的時候說：講短一點就好。我說沒問題，然後準備了三頁——放心，我只念這一句：祝你們幸福。

en.content:
Hi everyone, I'm the best man.
The reason I'm the one giving this speech
is that after they asked around,
I was the only one who couldn't come up with an excuse.

But standing up here, I realized —
you'd have to be a real friend
to be trusted with this much embarrassment.
So here's to them.
en.editorNote: A best man's biggest fear is freezing in the first ten seconds. The 'I only got picked because I couldn't say no' opener disarms the room instantly, then folds neatly into a sincere toast
variations: I'm not the best speaker in the room — I'm just the one who couldn't dodge it. But I wouldn't trade standing here to raise this glass for anything.
The groom told me to keep it short. So I wrote three pages, and I'm only reading one line of it: to the two of you.

items.sourceNote: 男方家長致詞（七分虧兒子、三分誇媳婦）register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我這個兒子，
從小房間就沒整齊過，
襪子永遠一隻在床下、一隻在宇宙某處。

所以今天我最想說的，
其實是對媳婦說的：
謝謝你願意接手這個工程。
你眼光很好，就是選人這件事，
可以再考慮一下。
zh-tw.editorNote: 家長致詞的黃金比例是七分虧自家小孩、三分真心誇對方——先把兒子的糗事抖乾淨，最後那句「選人可以再考慮」把寵溺和幽默一起收進來，長輩來講最有味道
variations: 我沒教會他摺棉被，也沒教會他準時，但至少教會他認得出好的人——今天他把人娶回來了，這題他總算答對。
當爸的最怕兒子娶不到人，現在我只怕一件事：怕你哪天看清楚他，然後後悔。開玩笑的——歡迎進門，孩子。

en.content:
This son of mine —
his room was never once tidy growing up,
one sock under the bed,
the other somewhere in outer space.

So what I really want to say today
is actually to my new daughter-in-law:
thank you for taking on this project.
You have wonderful taste —
though on this one pick, maybe sleep on it.
en.editorNote: The golden ratio for a parent's speech is seventy percent roasting your own kid, thirty percent genuine praise for their partner. Air out the son's flaws first, then land the 'maybe sleep on it' line to pack warmth and teasing into one breath
variations: I never got him to fold a blanket or show up on time, but I did teach him to recognize a good person — and today he brought one home, so he finally got one right.
Every father worries his son won't find someone. Now I only worry about one thing: that you'll see him clearly one day and regret it. Kidding — welcome to the family, kid.

items.sourceNote: 極簡短敬酒詞 register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
願你們吵架吵得贏、
和好和得快，
每一次回頭，
對方都還在原地。

乾杯。
zh-tw.editorNote: 臨時被點名敬酒、腦袋一片空白時最好用——四句話短到記得住，「吵得贏、和好得快」比空泛的「白頭偕老」更貼近真實的婚姻，舉杯就能講
variations: 祝你們有吵不完的小架，和永遠不用擔心會走的那個人。乾杯。
願你們的愛，經得起吵、忍得住煩、撐得過沒錢，然後越過越甜。敬新人。

en.content:
May you win the arguments,
make up quickly,
and every time you turn around,
find the other one still standing there.

Cheers.
en.editorNote: The one to reach for when you're called on out of nowhere and your mind goes blank — four lines short enough to remember, and 'win the arguments, make up quick' feels truer to real marriage than any 'grow old together' cliché
variations: Here's to a lifetime of small arguments, and the one person you never have to worry about losing. Cheers.
May your love survive the fights, the boredom, and the broke years — and get sweeter for it. To the newlyweds.

items.sourceNote: 現代詩意賀詞（把日子過成喜歡的樣子）register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
願你們不必轟轟烈烈，
只要細水長流剛剛好。

把柴米油鹽過成浪漫，
把平凡的日子，
慢慢過成你們最喜歡的樣子。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合寫在紅包或卡片給不熟古風成語的年輕新人——「把日子過成喜歡的樣子」這種現代語感比「永結同心」更有畫面，字數也剛好塞得進紅包袋
variations: 願你們往後的每一餐、每一次晚安，都還是想留給彼此——這樣的細水長流，就是最好的幸福。
不用愛得驚天動地，能在最普通的一天裡，還想牽對方的手，就已經贏過很多人了。

en.content:
May your love not need to be spectacular,
just steady and just enough.

Turn the groceries and the dishes into something like romance,
and slowly, day by ordinary day,
build the life you both like best.
en.editorNote: A modern card or red-envelope line for younger couples who don't connect with old-school blessings — 'the life you both like best' paints a clearer picture than 'forever united' and still fits on a small card
variations: May every meal and every goodnight from here on still be the ones you save for each other — that quiet, steady kind of love is the best luck there is.
You don't need a love that shakes the earth. If on the most ordinary day you still reach for their hand, you're already ahead of most.

items.sourceNote: 婚姻幽默（兩人都覺得自己賺到）register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
美滿的婚姻是這樣的：
兩個人心裡都偷偷覺得，
自己才是賺到的那一個。

看看今天這兩位——
我只能說，
他們八成都猜對了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把老哏「都覺得自己賺到」倒過來收尾，變成對新人的雙重讚美——虧的是自己嘴笨、誇的是他們互相配得上，敬酒前講完全場都會笑著點頭
variations: 好的婚姻裡，兩個人都默默覺得自己高攀了——而看你們兩個，我覺得這次是真的雙贏。
有人說婚姻是各退一步，我看你們比較像各自撿到寶，還都不好意思說破。

en.content:
A great marriage works like this:
both people secretly believe
they're the one who got the better deal.

Look at these two today —
all I can say is,
they've probably both guessed right.
en.editorNote: Flips the tired 'both think they got the better deal' line into a double compliment for the couple — you play dumb, they come out looking perfectly matched. Say it right before the toast and the whole room nods and grins
variations: In a good marriage, both people quietly think they married up — and looking at you two, I'd say it's a genuine win-win.
They say marriage is about meeting halfway. You two look more like you each got lucky and are too polite to admit it.

items.sourceNote: 證婚人結尾祝福 register 參考，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
願你們的愛，
是所有事情都在變的時候，
那件沒有變的事。

房子會換、工作會換、
連喜歡吃的東西都會換——
只有轉頭找對方這件事，
希望你們一直忘不掉。
zh-tw.editorNote: 證婚人或主婚人收尾用，把「永恆」講成很具體的日常對照組，比喊口號更有重量；語速放慢、講到「轉頭找對方」時看著新人，畫面會很好看
variations: 願你們往後每一次遇到變化，第一個反應都是先看對方一眼。
祝福你們：世界怎麼翻，你們都還是彼此的固定座位。

en.content:
May your love be the one thing
that stays put
while everything else keeps changing.

The apartments will change.
The jobs will change.
Even your favorite restaurant will close.
Just don't lose the habit
of turning to look at each other.
en.editorNote: An officiant's closing benediction. It trades abstract forever-talk for concrete, ordinary change, which lands harder. Slow down on the last line and look at the couple
variations: May every change that comes send you looking for each other first.
Whatever the world rearranges, may you two stay each other's fixed point.

items.sourceNote: 台灣紅包賀詞短句 register 參考，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
白頭偕老太遠，
先祝你們：
每天回家都有人等門。
zh-tw.editorNote: 紅包袋空間小、又不想寫爛熟的四字成語時最好用。把宏大的祝福縮成一個很具體的畫面，寫給年輕新人特別有感；三行也可以壓成一行寫
variations: 不敢保證一輩子，先祝你們每一頓晚餐都有人一起吃。
願你們往後最平凡的一天，也有人在門口說「你回來啦」。

en.content:
Forever's a long way off.
For now, here's to this:
someone always waiting up for you.
en.editorNote: Perfect for a card with barely any room, when you want something better than 'congrats on your special day.' Shrinks a huge blessing into one small image, which reads sincere rather than stiff
variations: Can't promise you forever. Can wish you a lot of dinners with each other.
Here's to the most ordinary night of your life, and someone at the door saying, hey, you're home.

items.sourceNote: 敬酒詞（選擇彼此）register 參考，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
今天你們簽的不是一次的選擇，
是往後每一天都要重簽一次的那種。

敬你們：
願你們每次都還是選對方，
而且每次都選得很輕鬆。
zh-tw.editorNote: 敬酒環節的萬用結尾，適合朋友、同事、伴郎伴娘。重點在「重簽一次」這個比喻，把婚姻講成持續動作而不是終點；舉杯前停半秒再說最後一句
variations: 婚姻不是一次答對就結束的考試，是每天都要再答一次——祝你們每次都答得很快。
敬兩位：願你們往後每天醒來，都覺得昨天那個決定做得真好。

en.content:
What you signed today isn't one choice.
It's the kind you re-sign every morning.

So here's to you both:
may you always choose each other,
and may it always feel easy.
en.editorNote: A go-to toast ending that works for friends, coworkers, or the wedding party. The re-signing image reframes marriage as an ongoing act instead of a finish line. Pause half a beat before the last line, then raise your glass
variations: Marriage isn't a test you pass once. It's one you retake daily — may you two keep acing it.
Here's to waking up tomorrow and both thinking, yeah, good call.

items.sourceNote: 新人向父母致謝 register 參考，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
爸、媽，
今天很多人祝我們幸福，
可是我想先謝謝你們——
因為我會知道幸福長什麼樣子，
是從你們家的餐桌上學來的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 新人致詞裡最容易讓長輩掉淚的一段。把感謝落在「餐桌」這種具體場景，比「感謝養育之恩」真誠很多；可以把餐桌換成自己家真正的畫面，例如接送、宵夜、修東西
variations: 謝謝你們沒有教我們什麼叫愛情，只是每天做給我們看。
