我們今天才剛開始學怎麼當一家人，而老師一直坐在第一桌。

en.content:
Mom, Dad —
a lot of people wished us happiness today,
but I want to thank you first.
If I know what happiness looks like,
it's because I grew up watching it
at your kitchen table.
en.editorNote: The line in a newlywed's speech most likely to get the parents. Anchoring gratitude to one specific domestic image beats any 'thank you for raising me.' Swap the kitchen table for whatever's actually true in your family
variations: You never sat us down and explained love. You just did it in front of us every day.
We're brand new at being a family — luckily the experts are sitting at table one.

items.sourceNote: 伴郎幽默致詞（吐槽轉讚美）register 參考，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
認識他十五年，
我從沒看過他準時過。

只有一件事例外——
每次要去接她，
他都提早二十分鐘到樓下等。

所以我今天完全不擔心。
zh-tw.editorNote: 伴郎致詞的黃金公式：先吐槽一個大家都知道的小毛病，再用同一個毛病證明他有多在乎。時間長度、細節換成你朋友真的有的習慣就好，越具體越好笑也越感人
variations: 他這個人做什麼都拖，唯獨追她那年，效率高到我以為他被換人了。
十五年來他答應我的事有一半沒做到，但他答應她的事，一件都沒漏。

en.content:
I've known him fifteen years.
I have never once seen him show up on time.

With exactly one exception:
every time he picks her up,
he's downstairs twenty minutes early.

So I'm not worried about today at all.
en.editorNote: The best man formula that never misses — roast a flaw everyone already knows about, then use that same flaw as proof of how much he cares. Swap in your friend's actual habit; the more specific, the funnier and the more it lands
variations: He procrastinates on everything. Except the year he was chasing her, when he suddenly became a different, alarmingly efficient man.
In fifteen years he's flaked on me about half the time. He has never once flaked on her.

items.sourceNote: 同志婚禮賓客致詞 register（不喊口號、只講重量），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
有人問，這場婚禮跟別人有什麼不一樣。

我想了想——
蛋糕一樣甜，戒指一樣亮。

他們只是，
等這一天等得久了一點。

所以今天的掌聲，也請大家大聲一點。
zh-tw.editorNote: 同志婚禮賓客致詞最好的分寸——不喊口號、不談政治，只用「等得久了一點」這一句把重量講完。祝福的核心跟任何一場婚禮都一樣，這正是重點所在
variations: 我不想說「你們終於可以結婚了」，我只想說：歡迎加入這個叫婚姻的甜蜜麻煩，跟我們大家一樣。
愛就是愛，這句話講了好多年，今天他們終於能站在這裡，把它變成一件事實。

en.content:
Someone asked what makes this wedding different.

I thought about it —
the cake's just as sweet, the rings just as bright.

They just
waited a little longer to get here.

So let's make today's applause a little louder too.
en.editorNote: The right register for a guest toast at a same-sex wedding — no slogans, no politics, just 'they waited a little longer' to carry all the weight. The blessing at its core is the same as at any wedding, and that's exactly the point
variations: I won't say 'you can finally get married.' I'll just say welcome to the sweet, ridiculous mess called marriage — same as the rest of us.
'Love is love' got said for a lot of years. Today they finally get to stand here and make it a fact.

items.sourceNote: 青梅竹馬新人的賓客致詞 register（一起長大視角），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我認識他們的時候，
兩個人還在搶同一支冰棒。

三十年後，
他們還是為了小事鬥嘴——
只是現在，會把最後一口留給對方。

有些人你不是遇見的，
是一起長大的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 青梅竹馬新人的致詞神角度——用「搶冰棒→留最後一口」這種橫跨幾十年的小動作對照，比講「你們很配」有畫面一百倍。把冰棒換成你們小時候真的搶過的東西，越具體越好
variations: 別人是先愛上、再慢慢認識；他們是先認識了一輩子，才發現原來早就愛上了。
從小學同班到今天同一張結婚證書，中間隔了三十年，也只隔了一個「終於」。

en.content:
When I first met them,
they were fighting over the same popsicle.

Thirty years later
they still bicker over nothing —
except now they save the last bite for each other.

Some people you don't meet.
You just grow up beside them.
en.editorNote: The perfect angle for childhood-sweetheart couples — contrasting one small habit across decades ('fighting over a popsicle' becomes 'saving the last bite') beats any 'you two are perfect.' Swap in whatever they actually fought over as kids
variations: Most people fall in love and then get to know each other. These two knew each other a lifetime first, and only then realized they'd been in love the whole time.
From sharing a classroom to sharing a last name — thirty years apart, and one word between them: finally.

items.sourceNote: 媒人 / 介紹人敬酒致詞 register（自嘲加邀功），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
當初是我硬把他們湊在一起吃那頓飯的。

第一次見面，兩個人都嫌對方不是自己的菜。

結果今天——
菜是我配的，
帳單是他們請的，
幸福是他們自己掙的。

所以第一杯，媒人先乾為敬。
zh-tw.editorNote: 媒人 / 介紹人致詞的黃金結構——先自嘲「當初兩人都看不上對方」，再邀功「現在我最有面子」。適合被安排相親、朋友牽線、家人介紹認識的新人，全場會笑著舉杯
variations: 我這輩子做過最成功的一筆投資，就是把這兩個人約出來吃那頓飯。
謝謝你們沒有在第一次見面就落跑，讓我今天可以站在這裡光明正大地邀功。

en.content:
I'm the one who forced these two into the same room for one dinner.

First date, they both decided the other one wasn't their type.

And yet — here we are.
I set up the date,
they're paying for dinner,
and the happiness, that part they earned themselves.

So this first toast is on the matchmaker.
en.editorNote: The gold structure for a matchmaker's toast — open by roasting yourself ('they both thought the other wasn't their type'), then take a victory lap ('best setup I ever made'). Perfect for couples set up on a blind date or introduced by family
variations: Best investment I ever made was talking these two into showing up to that one dinner.
Thank you both for not bolting on the first date, so I could stand here today and take all the credit.

items.sourceNote: 死黨吐槽「以前發誓不婚」的搞笑致詞 register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
他發過誓，這輩子絕對不結婚。

我還替他跟別人打過賭。

所以今天我站在這裡，
一邊祝他幸福，
一邊算我輸了多少錢。

不過看到他這個笑容——
這場，我輸得心甘情願。
zh-tw.editorNote: 專門吐槽「以前口口聲聲說不婚」的死黨新人——用「我還下注賭他不會結」當笑點，最後翻轉成「輸得甘願」。認識越久越好用，全場老朋友都會笑到不行
variations: 他當年說「結婚是別人的事」，說得多大聲，今天這身西裝就穿得多帥。
能讓一個死不結婚的人改口，這件事本身，就是我聽過最浪漫的告白。

en.content:
This guy swore he'd never get married.

I even bet people money on it.

So here I am today,
half toasting his happiness,
half doing the math on how much I just lost.

But looking at that smile of his —
this is one bet I'm glad I lost.
en.editorNote: Made for roasting the friend who spent years insisting they'd never marry — 'I bet money he never would' as the punchline, flipped into 'a bet I'm glad I lost.' The longer you've known them, the harder it lands
variations: He used to say marriage was 'other people's problem' — and look how sharp that suit fits him today.
Getting a lifelong bachelor to change his mind is the most romantic thing I've ever witnessed.

items.sourceNote: 熟齡 / 第二段人生結婚的賓客致詞 register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
有人說，結婚要趁年輕。

他們用今天證明——
愛情不看你幾歲，
只看你願不願意，
在走了大半輩子之後，
還敢牽起另一隻手。

這份勇氣，比年輕更難得。
zh-tw.editorNote: 熟齡結婚 / 第二段人生新人的致詞——避開「怎麼現在才結」的尷尬，改用「敢再牽一次手的勇氣」正面收。適合晚婚、退休後再婚、二度結婚的場合，長輩來賓特別有感
variations: 年輕人的愛是衝動，他們的愛是選擇——看過了人生的難，還願意一起走完剩下的路。
不是找到得晚，是剛好走到現在，才遇到那個最對的人。

en.content:
They say you should marry young.

These two spent today proving otherwise.
Love doesn't check your age.
It only asks whether you're still willing —
after most of a lifetime —
to reach out and take another hand.

That kind of courage is rarer than youth.
en.editorNote: For a later-in-life or second-chapter marriage — sidestep any awkward 'why now?' and land on 'the courage to reach for a hand again.' Works beautifully for late marriages, remarriages, or couples finding each other after retirement
variations: Young love is impulse. Theirs is a choice — they've seen how hard life gets and still want to walk the rest of it together.
They didn't find it late. They found it exactly when they'd walked far enough to meet the right one.

items.sourceNote: 現代生活梗——家族群組 onboarding，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
從今天起，
你嫁的不只是他，
是整個「家族群組」。

早安長輩圖照三餐發，
已讀了記得要回，
想退群？
婆婆五分鐘內私訊你。

恭喜你解鎖終身群組，
祝你們永遠把對方置頂。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合伴娘或平輩朋友致詞——「家族群組」是全民都懂的梗，用它調侃婚姻，最後收在「把對方置頂」的溫馨結尾，笑點和祝福都有
variations: 歡迎加入這個群組，唯一的群規只有一條——不管吵得多兇，晚安一定要互道。

en.content:
Today you're not just marrying him.

You're being added
to the family group chat —
and there's no leaving.

Good-morning memes at 7am,
twenty photos of the same flower,
and if you mute it,
his mom will know.

Congratulations —
you two are pinned to the top
of each other's list for life.
en.editorNote: A modern toast for a friend or bridesmaid — the family group chat is a universal joke, and 'pinned to the top' gives it a warm landing
variations: Welcome to the group chat. One rule: no matter how big the fight, somebody still has to send 'goodnight.'

items.sourceNote: 花束 vs 花園比喻，溫柔文青 register，原創

zh-tw.content:
愛情不是一束花。

花很美，
但你只是把它剪下來、插進瓶裡，
看它慢慢枯。

婚姻是一座花園——
要天天澆水、拔草、曬太陽，
有時候颱風來還會倒。

但你們一起種下的，
會一年比一年茂盛。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給喜歡溫柔文青風的新人，把「花束 vs 花園」的對比講成經營婚姻的比喻，適合證婚人、長輩致詞或寫進卡片
variations: 願你們不只擁有花開的那一天，更享受一起翻土、澆水、等它慢慢長大的每一天。

en.content:
Love isn't a bouquet.

A bouquet is beautiful,
but you cut it, put it in a vase,
and watch it slowly fade.

Marriage is a garden —
you water it, pull the weeds,
turn it toward the sun,
and yes, some storms knock it flat.

But what you plant together
grows fuller every single year.
en.editorNote: For a gentle, sincere register — the bouquet-versus-garden image works for an officiant, an elder's toast, or a handwritten card
variations: May you love not just the day the flowers bloom, but every ordinary day of digging, watering, and waiting for them to grow.

items.sourceNote: 共同歌單比喻的誓詞，原創

zh-tw.content:
我們的婚姻，
像一張一起編的歌單。

有些歌我們會單曲循環，
聽到滾瓜爛熟還是捨不得跳過。

有些歌是你加的、我不愛，
但我還是留著，
因為那是你。

我答應你——
不管播到哪一首，
我都會把耳機分你一邊。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合新人本人誓詞，用「共同歌單」比喻一輩子的相處，最後「耳機分你一邊」畫面感很強，年輕族群超有共鳴
variations: 願我們的歌單一直加下去，加到白髮、加到要把音量調很大聲，還是想聽同一首。

en.content:
Our marriage
is a playlist we build together.

Some songs we'll put on repeat,
know every word, and still never skip.

Some songs you add that I'd never pick,
but I keep them anyway,
because they're you.

Here's my promise —
whatever's playing,
you always get one of the earbuds.
en.editorNote: A vow the couple can say themselves — the shared-playlist image lands with younger crowds, and 'one of the earbuds' is a vivid, tender close
variations: May we keep adding to this playlist until our hair goes white and we have to turn the volume way up — and still want to hear the same song.
